title: 🐛 fix(desktop): add missing Stats and Creds tabs to Electron componentMap
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ✅ test

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

`componentMap.desktop.ts` was missing `Stats` and `Creds` entries, causing blank pages at `/settings/stats` and `/settings/creds` in the Electron desktop app.

**Changes:**
- Added `Stats` and `Creds` to `componentMap.desktop.ts` to match `componentMap.ts`
- Added sync test (`componentMap.sync.test.ts`) that asserts both maps have identical keys
- Added sync test (`desktopRouter.sync.test.tsx`) that asserts both router configs have identical route paths
- Updated `react` and `spa-routes` skills with `.desktop` file sync rules to prevent future drift

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Tested locally

Run the sync tests:
```bash
bunx vitest run componentMap.sync.test.ts
bunx vitest run desktopRouter.sync.test.tsx
```

Verify in Electron that `/settings/stats` and `/settings/creds` now render content.

#### 📝 Additional Information

Root cause: when new settings tabs are added to `componentMap.ts` (web, dynamic imports), the `componentMap.desktop.ts` (Electron, sync imports) must be updated in tandem. The new sync tests will fail in CI if they drift again.

componentMap.desktop.ts, causing blank pages at /settings/stats and

config to prevent future drift between web and desktop variants.
Also strengthened react and spa-routes skills with .desktop file
sync rules.

title: 🐛 fix(editor): reset editor state when switching to empty page
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Resolves LOBE-6321

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When switching from a page with content to a blank page, the editor retained the previous content because `setDocument` was skipped for empty documents.

Changes:
- Explicitly call `setDocument('json', EMPTY_EDITOR_STATE)` when switching to an empty document, clearing stale content
- Consolidate `EMPTY_EDITOR_STATE` constant to `src/libs/editor/constants.ts` (removed duplicate definitions from route-specific files)
- Update tests to assert the editor resets to empty state on blank page load

#### 🧪 How to Test

1. Open a blank page
2. Switch to another page that has content — content should load correctly
3. Switch back to the blank page — editor should show empty state (not retain previous content)

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

title: 🐛 fix: compress uploaded images to max 1920px before sending to API
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-6315，
- fix https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/issues/12639
- close LOBE-5929

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Anthropic API rejects images exceeding 2000px in multi-image requests with error:
`At least one of the image dimensions exceed max allowed size for many-image requests: 2000 pixels`

This PR compresses uploaded chat images to max 1920px (with margin) before sending:

- Updated `compressImage` utility: max size 2160→1920, removed default webp conversion (preserves original format jpg/png)
- Added `compressImageFile` helper in upload flow to compress images before upload
- Added `maxSize` parameter to `compressImage` for flexibility

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

1. Upload an image larger than 1920px in chat
2. Verify the uploaded image is compressed to within 1920px while maintaining aspect ratio
3. Verify the image format is preserved (not converted to webp)
4. Upload an image smaller than 1920px and verify it is NOT compressed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: arvinxx

ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub

commits.message:
🐛 fix: compress uploaded images to max 1920px before sending to API

Anthropic API rejects images exceeding 2000px in multi-image requests.
Compress images during upload to stay within limits while preserving
original aspect ratio and format (no webp conversion).

Fixes LOBE-6315

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: skip canvas compression for GIF and SVG images

Canvas serialization flattens animated GIFs and rasterizes SVGs.
Restrict compression to safe raster formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: always compress images to PNG to avoid MIME mismatch

canvas.toDataURL with original file type can produce content that
doesn't match the declared MIME type, causing Anthropic API errors.
Always output PNG which is universally supported and consistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: progressively shrink images to stay under 5MB API limit

If compressed PNG still exceeds 5MB, progressively reduce dimensions
by 20% until it fits. Also triggers compression for small-dimension
images that exceed 5MB file size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: extract compressImageFile to utils and add comprehensive tests

Move compressImageFile, COMPRESSIBLE_IMAGE_TYPES, and constants to
@lobechat/utils/compressImage for reusability and testability.
Add tests for: dimension compression, file size limit, format filtering,
error handling, and progressive shrinking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

1. Preserve the default `model`, `provider`, and `parentMessageId` in the initial runtime payload when `@agent` builds an `initialContext`, so the first LLM hop does not fall back to `/webapi/chat/openai` unexpectedly.

title: 🐛 fix: add document parsing to knowledge base chunking pipeline
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Knowledge base file uploads were missing document parsing, causing detailed content to be unavailable.

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When files are uploaded to a knowledge base, the `parseFileToChunks` async handler only performed chunking (splitting into chunks for RAG/semantic search) but did not create a `documents` record. This meant the parsed document content was not available for detailed viewing.

This fix adds document parsing as a pre-step in the chunking pipeline:
- Before chunking begins, checks if a `documents` record already exists for the file
- If not, calls `DocumentService.parseFile()` to create one
- Wrapped in try-catch so document parsing failure does not block the chunking flow

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Upload a file (PDF, DOCX, MD) to a knowledge base
2. Verify that both chunking and document parsing complete
3. Check that the file's detailed content is viewable in the knowledge base

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: arvinxx

title: 🐛 fix: move nodrag from TabBar container to individual TabItems
body:
The TabBar's ScrollArea had `electronStylish.nodrag` with `flex: 1`,
causing the entire middle section of the title bar to be non-draggable.
Move `nodrag` to individual TabItem components so empty space in the
tab bar area inherits `drag` from the parent TitleBar.

title: 🐛 fix: map unsupported time_range values for Search1API
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ✅ test

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Follow-up to #13207

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Search1API only supports `time_range` values of `day`, `month`, `year`. However, upstream callers can pass `week` which causes a **422 Unprocessable Entity** error from the API.

This PR adds a `timeRangeMapping` to convert unsupported values:
- `week` → `month` (closest supported value)
- `day`, `month`, `year` pass through as-is
- Unknown values are filtered out

Also adds integration tests covering Chinese/English queries with `searchEngines` + `searchTimeRange: 'week'` combination that previously failed.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

```bash
SEARCH1API_API_KEY=<key> bunx vitest run --silent='passed-only' 'src/server/services/search/impls/search1api/index.integration.test.ts'
```

title: 🐛 fix: correct Search1API response parsing to match actual API format
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ✅ test

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The Search1API `/search` endpoint response format has changed. The code expected a `Search1ApiResponse[]` (direct array), but the actual API returns:

```json
{
  "results": [{ "success": true, "data": { "searchParameters": {...}, "results": [...] } }],
  "summary": "..."
}
```

This PR:
- Updates `type.ts` with `Search1ApiRawResponse` and `Search1ApiResponseItem` types matching the actual API format
- Fixes response parsing in `index.ts` to correctly extract results from `rawResponse.results[].data`
- Adds integration tests to verify the search functionality works end-to-end

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

Run with a valid API key:
```bash
SEARCH1API_API_KEY=<key> bunx vitest run --silent='passed-only' 'src/server/services/search/impls/search1api/index.integration.test.ts'
```

Tests auto-skip when no API key is set, so CI won't be blocked.

title: ✨ feat: add Coding Plan providers support
body: #### 💻 Change Type - [x] ✨ feat - [ ] 🐛 fix - [ ] ♻️ refactor - [ ] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [ ] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔗 Related Issue #### 🔀 Description of Change 本PR为LobeHub添加了5个Coding Plan providers支持：BailianCodingPlan、GLMCodingPlan、KimiCodingPlan、MiniMaxCodingPlan、VolcengineCodingPlan。 为什么Coding Plan需要单独的provider，而不是复用普通provider？ Coding Plan需要独立provider的主要理由如下： 1. **独立的API端点和计费体系**：Coding Plan使用专门的API端点（如`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`），而非通用endpoint。每个Coding Plan有独立的计费方式和配额管理。 2. **特殊的模型配置和参数**：Coding Plan包含特定的模型集合，这些模型可能不在通用provider的模型列表中，而普通api端点支持的模型，也可能不在Coding Plan中提供，需要独立维护。 3. **不同的功能特性和优化方向**：Coding Plan专注于编程任务优化，与通用chat模型有明显区别，例如大部分Coding Plan应该不支持模型内置搜索。 **修改概述：** 1. **新增Coding Plan Providers** - 为每个coding plan创建独立的provider配置（packages/model-bank/src/modelProviders/） - 添加对应的模型定义（packages/model-bank/src/aiModels/） - 实现运行时处理逻辑（packages/model-runtime/src/providers/） - 添加测试用例（index.test.ts） 2. **特殊参数支持** - 新增ReasoningTokenSlider32k和ReasoningTokenSlider80k组件，支持32k/80k两种reasoning token范围 - 更新ControlsForm组件，集成新的滑块组件 - 扩展chatConfig类型定义，支持新的extendParams 3. **UI集成** - 更新ProviderGrid/Card组件，添加Coding Plan标签 - 修改ExtendParamsSelect组件，支持coding plan参数 - 更新modelParamsResolver，处理coding plan特殊参数 4. **国际化支持** - 更新locales/en-US/models.json和providers.json - 在src/locales/default/modelProvider.ts中添加i18n键 5. **运行时集成** - 在runtimeMap.ts中注册所有新的coding plan providers - 配置专门的API端点和SDK类型 #### 🧪 How to Test - [x] Tested locally - [x] Added/updated tests - [ ] No tests needed #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos | Before | After | | ------ | ----- | | ... | | | ... || | ... | #### 📝 Additional Information Related to: lobehub/lobe-icons#272

✨ feat: add Aliyun Bailian Coding Plan provider

- Add new AI provider for Bailian Coding Plan (coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1)
- Support 8 coding-optimized models: Qwen3.5 Plus, Qwen3 Coder Plus/Next, Qwen3 Max, GLM-5/4.7, Kimi K2.5, MiniMax M2.5
- Reuse QwenAIStream for stream processing
- Static model list (Coding Plan does not support API model fetching)
- Add i18n translations for provider description

✨ feat: add MiniMax Coding Plan provider

- Add new AI provider for MiniMax Token Plan (api.minimax.io/v1)
- Support 6 models: MiniMax-M2.7, M2.7-highspeed, M2.5, M2.5-highspeed, M2.1, M2
- Static model list (Coding Plan does not support API model fetching)
- Add i18n translations for provider description

✨ feat: add GLM Coding Plan provider

- Add new AI provider for GLM Coding Plan (api.z.ai/api/paas/v4)
- Support 6 models: GLM-5, GLM-5-Turbo, GLM-4.7, GLM-4.6, GLM-4.5, GLM-4.5-Air
- Static model list (Coding Plan does not support API model fetching)
- Add i18n translations for provider description

✨ feat: add Kimi Code Plan provider

- Add new AI provider for Kimi Code Plan (api.moonshot.ai/v1)
- Support 3 models: Kimi K2.5, Kimi K2, Kimi K2 Thinking
- Static model list (Coding Plan does not support API model fetching)
- Add i18n translations for provider description

✨ feat: add Volcengine Coding Plan provider

- Add new AI provider for Volcengine Coding Plan (ark.cn-beijing.volces.com/api/coding/v3)
- Support 5 models: Doubao-Seed-Code, Doubao-Seed-Code-2.0, GLM-4.7, DeepSeek-V3.2, Kimi-K2.5
- Static model list (Coding Plan does not support API model fetching)
- Add i18n translations for provider description
✨ feat: update coding plan providers default enabled models and configurations

✨ feat: add reasoningBudgetToken32k and reasoningBudgetToken80k slider variants

- Add ReasoningTokenSlider32k component (max 32*1024)
- Add ReasoningTokenSlider80k component (max 80*1024)
- Add reasoningBudgetToken32k and reasoningBudgetToken80k to ExtendParamsType
- Update ControlsForm to render appropriate slider based on extendParams
- Update ExtendParamsSelect with new options and previews
- Fix ReasoningTokenSlider max value to use 64*Kibi (65536) instead of 64000

🔧 fix: support reasoningBudgetToken32k/80k in ControlsForm and modelParamsResolver

- Add reasoningBudgetToken32k and reasoningBudgetToken80k fields to chatConfig type and schema
- Update ControlsForm to use correct name matching for 32k/80k sliders
- Add processing logic for 32k/80k params in modelParamsResolver
- Add i18n translations for extendParams hints

🎨 style: use linear marks for reasoning token sliders (32k/80k)

- Switch from log2 scale to linear scale for equal mark spacing
- Add minWidth/maxWidth constraints to limit slider length
- Fix 64k and 80k marks being too close together

🎨 fix: use equal-spaced index for reasoning token sliders (32k/80k)

- Slider uses index [0,1,2,3,...] for equal mark spacing
- Map index to token values via MARK_TOKENS array
- Add minWidth/maxWidth to limit slider length when marks increase
✨ feat: add reasoningBudgetToken32k for GLM-5 and GLM-4.7 in Bailian Coding Plan

🔧 fix: update coding plan API endpoints and model configurations

- minimaxCodingPlan: change API URL to api.minimaxi.com (China site)
- kimiCodingPlan: change API URL to api.kimi.com/coding/v1
- volcengineCodingPlan: update doubao-seed models with correct deploymentName, pricing
- volcengineCodingPlan: add minimax-m2.5 model
- bailianCodingPlan & volcengineCodingPlan: remove unsupported extendParams from minimax-m2.5
✨ feat: add Coding Plan tag to provider cards with i18n support

♻️ refactor: set showModelFetcher to false for Bailian Coding Plan

- Coding Plan does not support fetching model list via API
- Set both modelList.showModelFetcher and settings.showModelFetcher to false
🔧 fix: correct Coding Plan exports case in package.json
✨ feat: update coding plan models with releasedAt and remove pricing
🔧 fix: remove unsupported reasoning abilities from MiniMax Coding Plan models

🐛 fix(modelParamsResolver): fix reasoningBudgetToken32k/80k not being read when enableReasoning is present

- Add nested logic to check which budget field (32k/80k/generic) the model supports when enableReasoning is true
- Move reasoningBudgetToken32k/80k else-if branches before reasoningBudgetToken to ensure correct field is read
- Fix GLM-5/GLM-4.7 models sending wrong budget_tokens value to API

title: Add /new and /stop slash commands for bot message management
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This PR adds support for two new slash commands in the bot message router:

- **`/new`**: Clears the thread's conversation state (resets `topicId`) so the next message starts a fresh topic/conversation
- **`/stop`**: Cancels any active agent execution running on the current thread by interrupting the operation via `AiAgentService.interruptTask()`

**Key changes:**

1. **BotMessageRouter.ts**:
   - Added `registerSlashCommands()` private method that registers both slash command handlers
   - Integrated slash command registration into the bot initialization flow
   - Imported `AiAgentService` for operation interruption capability

2. **AgentBridgeService.ts**:
   - Added `activeOperations` static Map to track `operationId` per thread ID
   - Added helper methods: `isThreadActive()`, `getActiveOperationId()`, and `clearActiveThread()`
   - Updated message execution paths to track `operationId` when operations are created, enabling the `/stop` command to interrupt them

The implementation uses the existing `AgentBridgeService` thread tracking infrastructure to maintain state about active executions, allowing users to cleanly reset conversations or cancel long-running operations.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] No tests needed

The changes integrate with existing bot command infrastructure and leverage established patterns for thread state management. Manual testing of the slash commands in a bot environment would verify the functionality.

#### 📝 Additional Information

This feature enhances user control over bot conversations by providing explicit commands to reset context or stop execution, improving the interactive experience when working with agent-based bots.

title: ✨ feat: add GLM-5 model support to LobeHub provider
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5086

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add GLM-5 model support to the LobeHub hosted provider, along with two streaming bug fixes discovered during testing:

**Model card & runtime**
- Add GLM-5 model card (`lobehub/chat/zhipu.ts`) with USD fixed pricing ($1/M input, $3.2/M output)
- Register `zhipu` as a new `ApiType` in RouterRuntime with `LobeZhipuAI` mapping
- Pass `payload` through `handleStream` so pricing flows to `withUsageCost` for correct billing

**Bug fixes**
- `MessageToolCallSchema`: use `.catch()` on all fields to prevent ZodError when providers (e.g., GLM-5) send null in early streaming tool_call chunks
- `StreamingHandler.handleFinish`: call `endReasoningIfNeeded()` to ensure the reasoning spinner completes even when the stop chunk doesn't reach `handleChunk` (tool call scenarios)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests
  - `StreamingHandler.test.ts`: 2 new test cases for handleFinish reasoning guard

Test prompts used with GLM-5:
- Simple conversation: "hello"
- Tool calling: "write a snake game" (triggers artifacts)
- Search: "what's the weather in Beijing today"
author: tjx666

ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
Codex has been enabled to automatically review pull requests in this repo. Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. When you [sign up for Codex through ChatGPT](https://openai.com/codex), Codex can also answer questions or update the PR, like "@codex address that feedback".

commits.message:
✨ feat: add GLM-5 model support to LobeHub hosted provider

- Add GLM-5 model card with USD fixed pricing
- Register zhipu as a new ApiType in RouterRuntime
- Pass payload through handleStream for correct billing

🐛 fix: make MessageToolCallSchema resilient to null streaming chunks

Some providers (e.g., Zhipu GLM-5) send null for function.name/id in
early streaming tool_call chunks. Use .catch() to prevent ZodError from
breaking the SSE stream.

🐛 fix: end reasoning in handleFinish for tool call scenarios

When a model calls tools, the stop chunk may not reach handleChunk,
leaving the reasoning spinner stuck. Call endReasoningIfNeeded() at
the start of handleFinish to ensure reasoning always completes.

✅ test: add handleStream payload passthrough test and revert unnecessary reasoning guard

- Add test verifying payload is passed to custom handleStream
- Revert endReasoningIfNeeded guard in handleFinish (reasoning is
  already ended by handleToolCallsChunk and handleTextChunk)

♻️ refactor: revert toolsCalling schema change and related tests

The .catch('') on MessageToolCallSchema was only needed for aihubmix
proxy which sends null values; the official zai API sends correct data.
Remove the global schema relaxation and its associated tests.

🐛 fix: filter incomplete tool_call placeholder chunks in zhipu provider

With tool_stream enabled, some proxies (e.g., aihubmix) send empty
placeholder tool_call chunks without id/function.name before the real
data arrives. Filter them out to prevent ZodError in parseToolCalls.

title: ✨ feat(cli): add shell completion and migrate to tsdown
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [x] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [x] ✅ test
- [x] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This PR expands the CLI packaging update with interactive shell-completion support for `@lobehub/cli`.

- migrate the CLI bundler from `tsup` to `tsdown`
- add shell completion support for `lh` / `lobe` / `lobehub`
- add a public `completion [shell]` command and an internal `__complete` resolver backed by the Commander command tree
- make completion context-aware so nested commands like `lh agent <TAB>` return agent subcommands instead of top-level commands
- document local build, `bun link`, and completion usage in `apps/cli/README.md`

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

Commands run:

```bash
cd apps/cli
bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.mts --silent='passed-only' 'src/commands/completion.test.ts'
bun run build
source <(bun src/index.ts completion zsh)
LOBEHUB_COMP_CWORD=1 bun src/index.ts __complete agent
```

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

N/A (CLI only)

#### 📝 Additional Information

`bun run build` only generates the binary in `dist/`. To use `lh` from the shell locally, run `bun run cli:link` and refresh shell command lookup if needed (for example `rehash` in `zsh`).

body:

- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Replace redundant dynamic `import()` calls with top-level static imports in store actions where the module was already imported (or can be co-imported with existing symbols):

- **Plugin optimistic update**: import `dbMessageSelectors` alongside `displayMessageSelectors`.
- **File manager**: rely on existing `documentService` import from `@/services/document`.
- **User memory**: import `memoryCRUDService` with `userMemoryService` from `@/services/userMemory`.

This keeps behavior the same while simplifying the module graph and avoiding unnecessary async import overhead in these paths.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

Smoke: open chat with plugins, rename a knowledge folder, update a memory entry — flows should behave as before.

None

body:

- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

None.

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add a cache-busting timestamp query to stable desktop manifest requests.
This avoids stale `stable*.yml` responses from the update server or intermediate caches, so desktop release metadata stays in sync with the latest published artifacts.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- Run `bunx vitest run --silent='passed-only' 'src/server/services/desktopRelease/index.test.ts'`

This change only appends a shared timestamp query string when fetching `stable-mac.yml`, `stable.yml`, and `stable-linux.yml`.

title: 🚀 release: 20260320
body:
This release includes **~90 commits**. Key updates are below.

### New Features and Enhancements

- Added **Agent Documents** implementation with knowledge management tools (#13093).
- Introduced **context engine** that injects referenced topic context into the last user message (#13104).
- Added **server-side context compression** for improved performance with long conversations (#12976).
- Introduced **category-based mention menu** in chat input for better discoverability (#13109).
- Added **slash action tags**, topic reference tool, and a command bus system (#12860).
- Added **skill activity switching** support (#12970).
- Improved **agent context injection** — skills discovery, device optimization, prompt cleanup (#13021).

### Models and Providers

- Added **XiaomiMiMo** v2-pro and v2-omni support with web search enablement and tool merging (#13137, #13133, #13123).
- Added **GPT-5.4** mini and nano models (#13094).
- Updated **Grok 4.20** to 0309 and added **MiniMax M2.7** (#13112).
- Added **Seedance 1.5 Pro** support (#13035).
- Updated **Claude 4.6** series to 1M context window (#12994).

### Desktop Improvements

- Added **Linux window specialization** (#13059).
- Display actual desktop app version with canary suffix (#13110).
- Removed `electron-liquid-glass` to fix click event blocking on macOS (#13070).
- Improved WelcomeStep layout centering in onboarding (#13125).

### Performance

- Optimized **search with BM25 indexes and ICU tokenizer** (#12914).
- Optimized **tool system prompt** — removed duplicate APIs, simplified XML tags (#13041).
- Optimized **user message renderer** performance (#13108).
- Used **incremental diff for snapshot messages** to prevent OOM (#13136).

### Image / Video

- Redesigned image / video generation UI (#13126).
- Added image/video mode switch (#13152).

### Stability and Fixes

- Fixed duplicate AI provider ID: return CONFLICT instead of 500, added frontend form validation (#13153).
- Fixed builtin provider race condition with `onConflictDoNothing` on auto-init insert (#13153).
- Fixed "Last Used" badge showing on login page when only one login option exists (#13153).
- Fixed XiaomiMiMo chat payload — normalize reasoning content, filter empty user messages, drop unsupported trailing assistant messages (#13153).
- Fixed `forkedFromIdentifier` not stripped before LLM API request (#13142).
- Fixed empty editor state structure and wide screen layout (#13131).
- Fixed missing `BusinessAuthProvider` slot in auth layout (#13130).
- Fixed artifacts code scroll preservation while streaming (#13114).
- Fixed SSRF block error distinction from network errors (#13103).
- Fixed SPA HTML entry returning stale content after server upgrade (#12998).
- Fixed PDF.js worker loading from local assets via Vite `?url` import (#13006).
- Fixed Responses API tool pairing and context limit errors (#13078).
- Fixed missing `userId` in embeddings API calls (#13077) and `initModelRuntimeFromDB` (#13100).
- Fixed internal thinking content filtering in openai-compatible payloads (#13067).
- Fixed unsupported xAI reasoning penalties pruning (#13066).
- Fixed input clearing immediately on send to preserve drafts during streaming (#13038).
- Fixed market OIDC lost call tools error (#13025).
- Fixed `jsonb ?` operator usage to avoid Neon `rt_fetch` bug (#13040).
- Fixed model provider popup problems (#13012).
- Fixed agent-level memory config priority over user settings (#13018).
- Fixed multi-provider model item selection (#12968).
- Fixed `invokeBuiltinTool` returning `undefined` instead of structured error (#13020).
- Fixed agent stream error in local dev (#13054).
- Fixed skill crash (#13011).
- Fixed desktop agent-browser upgrade to v0.20.1 (#12985).
- Fixed topic share modal inside router (#12951).
- Fixed Enter key submission during IME composition (#12963).
- Fixed error collapse default active key (#12967).
- Replaced per-message `useNewScreen` with centralized `useConversationSpacer` (#13042).
- Refactored agent-runtime hooks mode (#13145).
- Split Stats into separate settings tab (#13016).
- Merged beta settings into advanced tab (#12962).
- Centralized NavBar dev mode logic (#13037).

### Credits

Huge thanks to these contributors (alphabetical):

@arvinxx @canisminor1990 @Coooolfan @cy948 @hezhijie0327 @Innei @nekomeowww @ONLY-yours @Protocol-zero-0 @rdmclin2 @rivertwilight @SiruiHe @tjx666 @Xial-kotori

* ✨ feat: add Agent Runtime Hooks — external lifecycle hook system

Hooks are registered once and automatically adapt to runtime mode:
- Local: handler functions called directly (in-process)
- Production: webhook configs persisted to Redis, delivered via HTTP/QStash

- HookDispatcher: register, dispatch, serialize hooks per operationId
- AgentHook type: id, type (beforeStep/afterStep/onComplete/onError),
  handler function, optional webhook config
- Integrated into AgentRuntimeService.createOperation + executeStep
- Hooks persisted in AgentState.metadata._hooks for cross-request survival
- Dispatched at both normal completion and error paths
- Non-fatal: hook errors never affect main execution flow

title: 🐛 fix: misc UI/UX improvements and bug fixes
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] 💄 style

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

- **Duplicate provider ID**: Catch PostgreSQL unique constraint violation in `createAiProvider` TRPC mutation, return CONFLICT instead of 500. Add frontend form validation for duplicate ID check.
- **Builtin provider race condition**: Add `onConflictDoNothing` to builtin provider auto-init insert in `getAiProviderById`.
- **Referral copy**: Reframe reward delay as "verification" instead of passive waiting. Change `pending_reward` status from "Pending Reward" to "Under Review".
- **LastUsed badge**: Hide "Last Used" badge on login page when only one login option exists.
- **Minor**: Remove hot tag from video generation starter, add comment for SVG focus outline fix.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

#### 📝 Additional Information

N/A

## Summary by Sourcery

Improve Xiaomi MiMo chat payload handling, tighten AI provider creation robustness, and refine several UI text and visual behaviors.

Bug Fixes:
- Normalize and sanitize Xiaomi MiMo chat messages, including reasoning content handling, filtering empty user messages, and dropping unsupported trailing assistant messages before sending requests.
- Ensure built-in AI providers are initialized idempotently in the database by ignoring insert conflicts.
- Return a conflict error instead of an internal server error when creating an AI provider with a duplicate ID and add client-side validation to prevent submitting duplicate IDs.
- Hide the "Last Used" login badge when only a single login option is available to avoid misleading UI state.

Enhancements:
- Enable Xiaomi web search via a flag in chat payloads and merge the corresponding web search tool into any existing tools.
- Clarify referral program copy to emphasize verification and relabel the pending reward status as "Under Review".
- Remove the hot tag from the video generation starter and suppress unwanted focus outlines on SVG icons in the model switch trigger for a cleaner UI.

Tests:
- Add Xiaomi MiMo payload tests covering web search enablement and tool merging, reasoning message shaping, empty message filtering, and trailing assistant message trimming.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In the Xiaomi MiMo payload handling, when `enabledSearch` is true you always append `{ type: 'web_search' }` to `tools`; consider deduping if a web search tool is already present to avoid sending duplicate tool definitions.
- The global `svg:focus { outline: none; }` rule in the model switch trigger removes visible focus indication for keyboard users; it would be safer to switch to `:focus-visible` or a custom focus style so accessibility is preserved while avoiding the unwanted blue ring.
- In `hasMessageContent`, array content is considered non-empty solely based on `length`; if your message segments can be whitespace-only strings or empty fragments, you may want to recursively check for actual text content to avoid sending effectively empty user messages.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In the Xiaomi MiMo payload handling, when `enabledSearch` is true you always append `{ type: 'web_search' }` to `tools`; consider deduping if a web search tool is already present to avoid sending duplicate tool definitions.
- The global `svg:focus { outline: none; }` rule in the model switch trigger removes visible focus indication for keyboard users; it would be safer to switch to `:focus-visible` or a custom focus style so accessibility is preserved while avoiding the unwanted blue ring.
- In `hasMessageContent`, array content is considered non-empty solely based on `length`; if your message segments can be whitespace-only strings or empty fragments, you may want to recursively check for actual text content to avoid sending effectively empty user messages.
~~~

</details>

***

title: 🔧 chore(vercel): add SPA asset cache headers and no-store for dev proxy
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🔨 chore

#### 🔀 Description of Change

- Configure Vercel headers for `/spa/assets/*` with long-lived cache and `immutable` for hashed SPA assets.
- Set `no-store` / `no-cache` headers for `/_dangerous_local_dev_proxy` routes so the local dev proxy page is not incorrectly cached.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- Verify response headers on deploy preview for `/spa/assets/...` and `/_dangerous_local_dev_proxy`.
- [x] No tests needed


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

## Summary by Sourcery

Configure Vercel caching headers for SPA assets and local dev proxy routes.

Build:
- Add long-lived immutable cache headers for `/spa/assets/*` in Vercel configuration.
- Set no-store/no-cache headers for `/_dangerous_local_dev_proxy` routes in Vercel configuration.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Double-check that the `/spa/assets/*` pattern only matches fingerprinted/hashed asset paths; if any non-hashed assets are served from that directory, you may want a narrower pattern so they don’t get `immutable` + very long cache lifetimes.
- Consider scoping the `/_dangerous_local_dev_proxy` `no-store`/`no-cache` headers to the specific methods or paths actually used for the dev proxy, in case other unrelated routes ever get added under that prefix in the future.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- Double-check that the `/spa/assets/*` pattern only matches fingerprinted/hashed asset paths; if any non-hashed assets are served from that directory, you may want a narrower pattern so they don’t get `immutable` + very long cache lifetimes.

## Summary by Sourcery

Add a unified external lifecycle hook system for agent runtime and integrate it with operation execution and completion handling.

New Features:
- Introduce an AgentHook lifecycle system with support for before/after step and completion/error hooks that work in both local and production modes.

Enhancements:
- Wire hook registration and dispatch into agent runtime and AI agent services, including persistence of webhook-based hooks in agent state metadata.
- Replace direct completion callbacks with hook-based completion dispatch while keeping legacy callbacks for backward compatibility.

Tests:
- Add comprehensive unit tests for the HookDispatcher covering local dispatch, production webhook delivery, serialization, and different hook types.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In `dispatchCompletionHooks` the `state` parameter is typed as `any` and the event is manually plucked from nested fields; consider typing this with the existing agent state type (or a derived interface) so field access is type-checked and future refactors are safer.
- In `HookDispatcher.dispatch` (production branch), the `serializedHooks?.filter(...) || this.getSerializedHooks(...)` fallback means that an explicitly provided empty `serializedHooks` array will be ignored; if the intent is to allow call sites to fully control which hooks are dispatched, you may want to distinguish between `undefined` and an empty array.
- In `HookDispatcher.test.ts`, `global.fetch` is mocked but not restored to its original value after tests (only `vi.restoreAllMocks()` is called), which can leak the mock into other test suites; consider saving the original `global.fetch` and restoring it in `afterEach`.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

## Overall Comments
- In `dispatchCompletionHooks` the `state` parameter is typed as `any` and the event is manually plucked from nested fields; consider typing this with the existing agent state type (or a derived interface) so field access is type-checked and future refactors are safer.
- In `HookDispatcher.dispatch` (production branch), the `serializedHooks?.filter(...) || this.getSerializedHooks(...)` fallback means that an explicitly provided empty `serializedHooks` array will be ignored; if the intent is to allow call sites to fully control which hooks are dispatched, you may want to distinguish between `undefined` and an empty array.

commits.message:
✨ feat: add Agent Runtime Hooks — external lifecycle hook system

Hooks are registered once and automatically adapt to runtime mode:
- Local: handler functions called directly (in-process)
- Production: webhook configs persisted to Redis, delivered via HTTP/QStash

- HookDispatcher: register, dispatch, serialize hooks per operationId
- AgentHook type: id, type (beforeStep/afterStep/onComplete/onError),
  handler function, optional webhook config
- Integrated into AgentRuntimeService.createOperation + executeStep
- Hooks persisted in AgentState.metadata._hooks for cross-request survival
- Dispatched at both normal completion and error paths
- Non-fatal: hook errors never affect main execution flow

LOBE-6208

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✅ test: add HookDispatcher unit tests (19 tests)

Tests cover:
- register/unregister/hasHooks
- Local mode dispatch: matching types, multiple handlers, error isolation
- Production mode dispatch: webhook delivery, body merging, mode isolation
- Serialization: getSerializedHooks filters webhook-only hooks
- All hook types: beforeStep, afterStep, onComplete, onError

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: migrate SubAgent to hooks + add afterStep dispatch + finalState

- AgentHookEvent: added finalState field (local-mode only, stripped from webhooks)
- AgentRuntimeService: dispatch afterStep hooks alongside legacy callbacks
- AiAgentService: createThreadHooks() replaces createThreadMetadataCallbacks()
  for SubAgent Thread execution — same behavior, using hooks API
- HookDispatcher: strip finalState from webhook payloads (too large)

LOBE-6208

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: add Vercel bypass header to QStash hook webhooks

Preserves x-vercel-protection-bypass header when delivering hook
webhooks via QStash, matching existing behavior in
AgentRuntimeService.deliverWebhook and libs/qstash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: migrate Eval Run to hooks + add finalState to AgentHookEvent

Eval Run now uses hooks API instead of raw completionWebhook:
- executeTrajectory: hook with local handler + webhook fallback
- executeThreadTrajectory: hook with local handler + webhook fallback
- Local mode now works for eval runs (previously production-only)

Also:
- AgentHookEvent: added finalState field (local-only, stripped from webhooks)
  for consumers that need deep state access

LOBE-6208

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: dispatch beforeStep hooks + fix completion event payload fields

P1: Add hookDispatcher.dispatch('beforeStep') alongside legacy
onBeforeStep callback. All 4 hook types now dispatch correctly:
beforeStep, afterStep, onComplete, onError.

P2: Fix completion event payload to use actual AgentState fields
(state.cost.total, state.usage.llm.*, state.messages) instead of
non-existent state.session.* properties. Matches the field access
pattern in triggerCompletionWebhook.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: update eval test assertions for hooks migration + fix status type

- Test: update executeTrajectory assertion to expect hooks array
  instead of completionWebhook object
- Fix: add fallback for event.status (string | undefined) when passing
  to recordTrajectoryCompletion/recordThreadCompletion (status: string)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: update SubAgent test assertions for hooks migration

Update execGroupSubAgentTask tests to expect hooks array instead of
stepCallbacks object, matching the SubAgent → hooks migration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: 🐛 fix(chat): strip forkedFromIdentifier before LLM API request
body:
## Summary
Fork \& Chat stores `forkedFromIdentifier` in `agent.params` for resolving the source market agent. That object was merged into the outbound chat / Responses API payload, so strict OpenAI-compatible gateways (e.g. AiHubMix with `gpt-5.4`) rejected the request with `Unknown parameter: 'forkedFromIdentifier'`.

## Change
Delete `forkedFromIdentifier` from the request object in `getChatCompletion`, next to the existing strip of `scope` (also non-API metadata).

## Issue
Fixes #13071

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: Protocol-zero-0

commits.message:
fix(chat): strip forkedFromIdentifier before LLM API request

Fork & Chat stores forkedFromIdentifier in agent.params for DB lookup.
Spreading params into the chat payload forwarded it to Responses API,
causing strict providers (e.g. AiHubMix) to reject the request.

Remove the field in getChatCompletion alongside existing non-API keys.

Fixes lobehub/lobehub#13071

title: ♻️ refactor: use incremental diff for snapshot messages to prevent OOM
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-6202

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Replace full `messages`/`messagesAfter` duplication per step with baseline + delta approach to prevent OOM on large agent operations (77MB+ partial snapshots).

**Core changes:**
- `StepSnapshot` now uses `messagesBaseline` + `messagesDelta` instead of full `messages`/`messagesAfter` per step
- Step 0 and compression resets store full baseline; other steps store only delta
- Strip `llm_stream` events, `done.finalState.messages`, duplicate `toolResults` from `context.payload`, and `messages`/`toolsConfig` from `context_engine_result` events
- Add `reconstructMessages()` / `expandSnapshot()` utilities for rebuilding full state
- `AiAgentService` constructor now accepts `runtimeOptions` for dependency injection
- Backward compatible: old snapshots with `messages`/`messagesAfter` still work

**Size impact (4-step eval with 21 tool calls):**
- Messages: 59.9KB → 11.2KB (81% reduction)
- Total snapshot: 1,650KB → 1,135KB (31% reduction)
- For 125-step BrowseComp cases: estimated 77MB → 2-5MB (97%+ reduction)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

Unit tests for `reconstructMessages`, `expandSnapshot`, `isIncrementalFormat` (9 tests).
Verified with `agent-evals run deep-search` — snapshot generated correctly with incremental format, `agent-tracing inspect` CLI reads new format properly.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Refactor agent execution snapshots to store messages incrementally instead of duplicating full message arrays per step, reducing snapshot size while preserving backward compatibility and tooling support.

New Features:
- Add utilities to detect incremental snapshots, reconstruct per-step messages, and expand snapshots back to the legacy full-messages format.

Enhancements:
- Extend StepSnapshot schema to support baseline and delta message storage plus compression reset markers while deprecating direct messages/messagesAfter fields.
- Update snapshot recording to persist only message deltas with optional baselines and to strip heavy or redundant data from events and context payloads to avoid oversized snapshots.
- Adapt agent tracing viewer and CLI inspect command to resolve messages from either legacy or incremental snapshot formats transparently.
- Expose reconstruction utilities from the agent-tracing package public API for reuse by other tooling.
- Allow AiAgentService to receive AgentRuntimeService runtime options to support dependency injection in runtime construction.

Tests:
- Add unit tests covering message reconstruction across steps and compression resets, incremental format detection, and snapshot expansion back to the legacy format.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The incremental snapshot encoding in AgentRuntimeService appears inconsistent with the reconstruction logic: for baseline steps you set `messagesDelta = afterMessages`, but `reconstructMessages` assumes `messagesBaseline` is the full prior state and `messagesDelta` only contains newly added messages, so this will duplicate prior messages when reconstructing; consider changing the baseline case to compute `messagesDelta` as `afterMessages.slice(prevMessages.length)` as well.
- The reconstruction utilities assume `snapshot.steps` are ordered by `stepIndex`; if that ordering is not strictly guaranteed by all producers, it would be safer to sort or index by `stepIndex` inside `reconstructMessages`/`expandSnapshot` to avoid subtle bugs with out-of-order steps.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The incremental snapshot encoding in AgentRuntimeService appears inconsistent with the reconstruction logic: for baseline steps you set `messagesDelta = afterMessages`, but `reconstructMessages` assumes `messagesBaseline` is the full prior state and `messagesDelta` only contains newly added messages, so this will duplicate prior messages when reconstructing; consider changing the baseline case to compute `messagesDelta` as `afterMessages.slice(prevMessages.length)` as well.
- The reconstruction utilities assume `snapshot.steps` are ordered by `stepIndex`; if that ordering is not strictly guaranteed by all producers, it would be safer to sort or index by `stepIndex` inside `reconstructMessages`/`expandSnapshot` to avoid subtle bugs with out-of-order steps.

commits.message:
♻️ refactor: use incremental diff for snapshot messages to prevent OOM

Replace full messages/messagesAfter duplication per step with baseline + delta approach:
- Step 0 and compression resets store full messagesBaseline
- Other steps store only messagesDelta (new messages added)
- Strip llm_stream events from snapshot (not useful for post-analysis)
- Strip messages from done.finalState (reconstructible from delta chain)
- Strip duplicate toolResults from context.payload
- Reduce context_engine_result event size by removing messages and toolsConfig
- Add reconstructMessages() utility for rebuilding full state from delta chain
- AiAgentService constructor now accepts runtimeOptions for DI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: add incremental toolset delta for snapshot

- Store operationToolSet as toolsetBaseline in step 0 only (immutable)
- Track activatedStepTools changes via per-step activatedStepToolsDelta
- Strip operationToolSet/toolManifestMap/tools/toolSourceMap from done.finalState
- Add reconstructToolsetBaseline() and reconstructActivatedStepTools() utilities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: correct snapshot delta recording and restore context-engine output

- P1: messagesDelta now always stores only appended messages (afterMessages.slice),
  fixing duplication when isBaseline was true (step 0 / compression reset)
- P2: Restore context_engine_result.output (processedMessages) — needed by
  inspect CLI for --env, --system-role, and -m commands
- Add P1 regression test for message deduplication

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The new tiered pricing for `mimo-v2-pro` in `xiaomimimo.ts` uses `upTo: 256_000` with `unit: 'millionTokens'`, which looks like a unit mismatch compared to the original `[0, 0.256]` ranges and will effectively shift tiers by six orders of magnitude—this likely should be `0.256` instead of `256_000` for all three units.
- There is now duplicated configuration for Xiaomi MiMo models between `aiModels/xiaomimimo.ts` and `aiModels/lobehub/chat/xiaomimimo.ts` (descriptions, abilities, pricing, etc.); consider extracting shared constants or a factory to keep these in sync and reduce divergence risk.
- The `releasedAt` fields in the new LobeHub Xiaomi MiMo model cards are hard-coded to 2026 dates; if `releasedAt` is meant to reflect actual release time rather than a future placeholder, consider aligning this with the real or current release date.

title: 🐛 fix(database): add drizzle-zod and zod as peer dependencies to fix type-check errors
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

`packages/database/src/schemas/*.ts` files import `drizzle-zod` (`createInsertSchema`, `createSelectSchema`), but `packages/database/package.json` did not declare it as a dependency. This caused pnpm to resolve `drizzle-zod` from the root context, where its peer `drizzle-orm` was resolved to a different virtual store instance (due to inconsistent `better-sqlite3` optional peer auto-install), resulting in 12 TS2345 type errors:

```
Types have separate declarations of a private property 'shouldInlineParams'
```

This PR adds `drizzle-zod` and `zod` (required peer of `drizzle-zod`) as peer dependencies of `@lobechat/database`, ensuring pnpm resolves them in the same context as `drizzle-orm`, eliminating the duplicate instance mismatch.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

Run `pnpm run type-check` — should pass with 0 errors (previously had 12 errors across 7 files).

body: #### 💻 Change Type - [ ] ✨ feat - [ ] 🐛 fix - [ ] ♻️ refactor - [X] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [ ] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔗 Related Issue #### 🔀 Description of Change 1. 增加 `mimo-v2-pro` `mimo-v2-omni` 支持 2. 增加内建搜索功能，需到控制台开启 https://platform.xiaomimimo.com/#/docs/usage-guide/tool-calling/web-search 3. 更新模型定价与描述 https://platform.xiaomimimo.com/#/docs/pricing 4. 支持思考模式下 Reasoning Content 回传，同 DeepSeek 处理 5. 适配 XiaomiMiMo 格式的搜索来源引用，在第一个 Chunk 中返回完整 annotations 6. 修正 API 隐性 `max_completion_tokens` 限制为 `mimo-v2-flash = 65536` `mimo-v2-pro = 32768` `mimo-v2-omni = 32768` --- |功能|截图| |-|-| |图片理解|| |视频理解|| |联网搜索|| |思考内容回传|| --- ref: https://platform.xiaomimimo.com/#/docs/news/v2-pro-release ref: https://platform.xiaomimimo.com/#/docs/news/v2-omni-release ref: https://platform.xiaomimimo.com/#/docs/news/news20260303 #### 🧪 How to Test - [ ] Tested locally - [ ] Added/updated tests - [ ] No tests needed #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos | Before | After | | ------ | ----- | | ... | ... | #### 📝 Additional Information ## Summary by Sourcery Add support for new Xiaomi MiMo V2 chat models in the model bank. New Features: - Introduce configuration for the `mimo-v2-pro` chat model with long-context and reasoning capabilities. - Introduce configuration for the multimodal `mimo-v2-omni` chat model with vision, video, and reasoning support. ## Summary by Sourcery Add full Xiaomi MiMo V2 model support and streaming adaptations, including reasoning content handling, web search integration, and citation-aware streaming. New Features: - Expose Xiaomi MiMo V2 Pro, V2 Omni, and V2 Flash chat models in the model bank with updated capabilities, limits, and pricing metadata. - Support Xiaomi MiMo web search as a toggleable tool within chat payloads and map reasoning content into provider-specific fields for thinking mode. Enhancements: - Adjust OpenAI-style streaming transform to track chunk order and emit Xiaomi MiMo URL citation annotations as grounding events while stripping them from subsequent text chunks. - Extend Xiaomi MiMo provider tests to cover reasoning-content transformation, model listing behavior, and debug configuration, and refine base64 image cleanup implementation.

title: ✨ feat(ci): add Claude PR auto-assign reviewer workflow
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically assigns PR reviewers using Claude Code, similar to the existing `claude-issue-triage.yml` workflow.

**New files:**
- `.claude/prompts/pr-assign.md` — PR analysis and reviewer assignment guide
- `.github/workflows/claude-pr-assign.yml` — GitHub Actions workflow

**How it works:**
1. Triggers on PR opened or `trigger:assign` label added (skips bot PRs)
2. Claude analyzes PR changed files and maps them to feature areas
3. Checks related issues (referenced in PR body) for team member participants
4. Determines reviewer(s) using `team-assignment.md` (reused from issue triage)
5. Posts a comment @mentioning the appropriate reviewer(s)

**Reuses existing prompts:**
- `team-assignment.md` — team member to feature area mapping
- `security-rules.md` — security constraints

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

CI workflow — verify by opening a test PR or adding `trigger:assign` label to an existing PR.

#### 📝 Additional Information

Mirrors the pattern established by `claude-issue-triage.yml`. Key differences:
- Uses `pull_request_target` event instead of `issues`
- Filters out bot PRs at workflow level (`user.type != 'Bot'`)
- Analyzes file paths instead of issue template fields
- Only posts comments (no label changes)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add an automated PR reviewer assignment workflow powered by Claude Code.

New Features:
- Introduce a prompt guide for mapping PR changes to feature areas and selecting reviewers.
- Automatically assign and notify appropriate PR reviewers based on changed files and related issues.

CI:
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull_request_target to invoke Claude Code for reviewer assignment and cleans up the trigger label after execution.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The workflow sets `allowed_non_write_users: '*'` for the Claude action, which is very permissive; consider restricting this to specific trusted users/teams to reduce the risk of abuse via prompt or PR content manipulation.
- You're using both a custom `secrets.GH_TOKEN` and explicit `permissions` on the job; if possible, simplify by relying on the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (with the permissions you've already configured) for both the Claude action and the `gh` CLI step to avoid managing an extra secret.
- The workflow references `actions/checkout@v6`, but the latest stable major version is currently `v4`; consider pinning to `actions/checkout@v4` (or a specific SHA) to ensure the action actually resolves and remains stable.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The workflow sets `allowed_non_write_users: '*'` for the Claude action, which is very permissive; consider restricting this to specific trusted users/teams to reduce the risk of abuse via prompt or PR content manipulation.
- You're using both a custom `secrets.GH_TOKEN` and explicit `permissions` on the job; if possible, simplify by relying on the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (with the permissions you've already configured) for both the Claude action and the `gh` CLI step to avoid managing an extra secret.
- The workflow references `actions/checkout@v6`, but the latest stable major version is currently `v4`; consider pinning to `actions/checkout@v4` (or a specific SHA) to ensure the action actually resolves and remains stable.
~~~

</details>

### 💡 Codex Review https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/blob/c7e8fa8cdb7c86a84f5a6bb7b404b71571b8a920/packages/model-bank/src/aiModels/xai.ts#L105-L107 **![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat) Clear function calling on the new multi-agent xAI model** `abilities.functionCall` is used by tool-enabled chat paths (`RuntimeExecutors.isCanUseFC` and `aiAgent`'s `isModelSupportToolUse`) to decide whether builtin/plugin tools may be attached. Marking `grok-4.20-multi-agent-beta-0309` as `functionCall: true` therefore enables client-side tool use for this model, but xAI's multi-agent docs—and even this card's own description—say custom/client-side tools are not supported. Sessions that select the new multi-agent model and enable tools will advertise a capability the backend rejects.

title: ✨ feat(chat-input): add category-based mention menu
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- N/A -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Replace the flat mention dropdown with a structured, category-based mention menu for the chat input editor.

**Key changes:**
- **Home view**: shows recent items + category entries (Agents, Topics, Members) with drill-down navigation
- **Category view**: lists all items within a selected category, with a back button
- **Search view**: Fuse.js fuzzy search across all mention items when user types after `@`
- **Positioning**: uses `@floating-ui/react` for robust caret-anchored positioning with flip/shift
- **Keyboard navigation**: full arrow-key, Enter/Tab selection, Escape to go back
- **Unified hook** `useMentionCategories`: consolidates agent, topic, and group-member data sources into a single category-based structure

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Open a chat conversation, type `@` in the input editor
2. Verify the home view shows recent items and category entries (Agents, Topics)
3. Click or press Enter on a category to drill into its items
4. Press Escape or click the back arrow to return to home
5. Type after `@` to trigger fuzzy search across all items
6. Verify keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter/Tab, Escape) works in all views
7. Test in a group chat to verify Members category appears

#### 📝 Additional Information

- Added `@floating-ui/react` dependency for menu positioning
- Added `fuse.js` (already a transitive dep) for client-side fuzzy search
- Removed dependency on the editor's built-in `fuseOptions` — search is now handled externally

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `RichTextMessage`, you now assume `editorState` is a valid `SerializedEditorState`; consider adding a narrower runtime shape check (e.g., verifying `root`/`nodeMap` fields or a version flag) before casting to avoid unexpected runtime errors from malformed data.
- In `ReferTopicView`, you call `useChatStore(topicSelectors.getTopicById(topicId))`, which creates a new selector function on each render; wrapping this in a stable selector (e.g., `useChatStore((s) => topicSelectors.getTopicById(topicId)(s))`) or memoizing the selector would avoid unnecessary subscriptions and improve render performance.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `RichTextMessage`, you now assume `editorState` is a valid `SerializedEditorState`; consider adding a narrower runtime shape check (e.g., verifying `root`/`nodeMap` fields or a version flag) before casting to avoid unexpected runtime errors from malformed data.
- In `ReferTopicView`, you call `useChatStore(topicSelectors.getTopicById(topicId))`, which creates a new selector function on each render; wrapping this in a stable selector (e.g., `useChatStore((s) => topicSelectors.getTopicById(topicId)(s))`) or memoizing the selector would avoid unnecessary subscriptions and improve render performance.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="src/features/Conversation/Messages/User/components/RichTextMessage.tsx" line_range="18" />
<code_context>
-    } catch {
-      return null;
-    }
+    return editorState as SerializedEditorState;
   }, [editorState]);

</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** Casting `editorState` directly to `SerializedEditorState` can throw at render time if the shape is unexpected.

The previous `try/catch` around stringifying `editorState` effectively validated the shape before use. The new code now trusts any non-empt

title: ✨ feat(context-engine): inject referenced topic context into last user message
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When users `@refer_topic` in chat input, this PR injects the referenced topic's context directly into the last user message via a new `TopicReferenceContextInjector` in the context engine pipeline.

**Context resolution priority:**
1. **Has `historySummary`** → inject summary directly (type=`summary`)
2. **No summary, has messages** → inject last 5 user/assistant messages, each truncated to 300 chars (type=`recent_messages`)
3. **No context available** → inject hint prompting the agent to call `getTopicContext` tool

All injected content includes a top-level hint: *"these are brief summaries or excerpts — for complete history, use the getTopicContext tool"*, ensuring the agent knows to call the tool for full details.

**Architecture:**
- `engine/topicReference/resolveTopicReferences.ts` — shared parsing + resolution logic with dependency-injected `lookupTopic` and `lookupMessages` callbacks (works for both client zustand store and server DB)
- `providers/TopicReferenceContextInjector.ts` — pipeline processor that formats and injects into the last user message
- Wired into both client path (`contextEngineering.ts`) and server path (`RuntimeExecutors.ts`)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

28 unit tests covering:
- Tag parsing (including markdown-escaped `\<refer\_topic>`)
- Topic resolution with/without summary, with/without messages fallback
- Message truncation (300 char limit) and count limit (last 5)
- Injector formatting, system context wrapper reuse, edge cases

#### 📝 Additional Information

This works alongside the existing `lobe-topic-reference` tool — the injector provides a quick preview in context, while the tool remains available for fetching complete conversation history when the agent needs more detail.

title: 🐛 fix: distinguish SSRF block errors from network errors
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/issues/12417

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Previously, `ssrfSafeFetch` wrapped ALL errors (including network errors like `ECONNREFUSED`) with the message `"SSRF-safe fetch failed: ..."` and a link to SSRF documentation. This misled Docker self-hosted users into thinking their `SSRF_ALLOW_PRIVATE_IP_ADDRESS=1` config was not working, when the actual issue was Docker network connectivity.

Now the error handling distinguishes between:
- **SSRF block errors** (from `request-filtering-agent`) → `"SSRF blocked: ..."` with doc link
- **Network/other errors** → `"Fetch failed: ..."` without misleading SSRF reference

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

title: ♻️ refactor(nav): remove devOnly mode from nav layout and stabilize Footer
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

- Remove `devOnly` filtering mechanism from `useNavLayout` — all nav items now behave as non-dev mode
- Move Pages (文稿) to top nav position (after Home)
- Remove dev-only nav items (video, image, settings, memory) from sidebar navigation
- Extract Footer from `SideBarLayout` into `NavPanelDraggable` outside the animation layer, so Footer remains static during panel transitions
- In dev mode, show a standalone Settings ActionIcon in Footer (next to Help), hidden when already on the settings page

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Switch between nav panels (Home, Pages, Community) and verify Footer does not animate/flash
2. Toggle dev mode in settings and verify the Settings icon appears in Footer
3. Navigate to settings page and verify the Settings icon is hidden in Footer

#### 📝 Additional Information

Footer was previously rendered inside `SideBarLayout` which sits inside the `motion.div` animation layer. This caused Footer to participate in panel transition animations. By moving Footer to `NavPanelDraggable` as a sibling after the animated `.inner` div, it stays fixed during transitions.

title: 🐛 fix: pass userId in initModelRuntimeFromDB
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔀 Description of Change

`initModelRuntimeFromDB` was passing `{}` as params to `initModelRuntimeWithUserPayload`, which meant `userId` was not included in the runtime constructor options. This caused the router runtime's `this._options.userId` to be `undefined`, so `onRouteAttempt` recorded route attempt logs without `userId`.

Fixed by passing `{ userId }` as params so the router runtime has access to `userId` for route attempt logging.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

title: 👷 build: add `settings` column to `agent_bot_providers`
body:
This release includes a **database schema migration** adding a `settings` column to the `agent_bot_providers` table.

### Migration: Add settings column to agent_bot_providers

- Added `settings` jsonb column (default `{}`) to `agent_bot_providers` table for user-configurable bot provider settings (dm policy, charLimit, debounce, etc.)

### Notes for Self-hosted Users

- The migration runs automatically on application startup
- No manual intervention required

The migration owner: @arvinxx — responsible for this database schema change, reach out for any migration-related issues.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The migration defines a non-null default (`DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb`) for `settings` while the Drizzle schema leaves the column nullable (no `.notNull()`), which can lead to subtle mismatches in type expectations and runtime behavior—consider aligning nullability and defaults between the DB and the ORM model.
- Using `Record<string, unknown>` for `settings` is very loose and loses type safety for known configuration fields (dm policy, charLimit, debounce, etc.); consider defining a more specific interface/type for these settings to catch misconfigurations at compile time.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The migration defines a non-null default (`DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb`) for `settings` while the Drizzle schema leaves the column nullable (no `.notNull()`), which can lead to subtle mismatches in type expectations and runtime behavior—consider aligning nullability and defaults between the DB and the ORM model.
- Using `Record<string, unknown>` for `settings` is very loose and loses type safety for known configuration fields (dm policy, charLimit, debounce, etc.); consider defining a more specific interface/type for these settings to catch misconfigurations at compile time.
~~~

</details>

title: 🐛 fix: pass userId to all embeddings API calls
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- N/A -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Chat calls already pass `user: ctx.userId` to the OpenAI API via `options.user`, but all embedding call sites were missing this field. This caused the `user` parameter in OpenAI `embeddings.create` requests to be `undefined`.

- Add `user: userId` to all embedding call sites across routers and services
- Add `userId` parameter to `createEmbedder` helpers in `userMemories.ts` and `memory.ts`
- Add `userId` parameter to `generateEmbeddings` private method in `extract.ts`
- Replace hardcoded `'memory-extraction'` string with actual userId in `extract.ts`
- Add test assertions to verify `user` field is passed correctly

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

Existing `userMemories.test.ts` tests updated with assertions verifying `user: userId` is passed to all embedding calls.

commits.message:
🐛 fix: pass userId to all embeddings API calls

Chat calls already pass `user: ctx.userId` to the OpenAI API, but all
embedding call sites were missing this field. This caused the `user`
parameter in OpenAI `embeddings.create` requests to be undefined.

- Add `user: userId` to all embedding call sites across routers and services
- Add `userId` parameter to `createEmbedder` helpers and `generateEmbeddings`
- Replace hardcoded `'memory-extraction'` string with actual userId in extract.ts
- Add test assertions to verify `user` field is passed correctly

title: 🐛 fix(desktop): remove electron-liquid-glass to fix click event blocking
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The `electron-liquid-glass` native addon was blocking all click events in the Electron desktop app window, making the entire UI unresponsive. This PR:

- Removes `electron-liquid-glass` dependency and all related code from `WindowThemeManager`
- Restores macOS vibrancy (`sidebar`) for window transparency
- Adds `.desktop` CSS class to desktop HTML root for platform-specific styling
- Uses semi-transparent `color-mix()` body background on desktop so vibrancy shows through
- Defaults DevTools to `detach` mode to avoid docked DevTools issues in frameless windows
- Cleans up `StyleRegistry.tsx` to use pure CSS selectors instead of JS conditional string concatenation

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Launch the desktop Electron app on macOS
2. Verify all click events work normally (buttons, inputs, navigation)
3. Verify window has translucent vibrancy effect
4. Open DevTools (F12) — should open in a separate window

author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `convertOpenAIResponseInputs`, the `map` can return `undefined` for filtered `thinking` parts, but the `Promise.all` result is used as-is; consider filtering out `undefined` entries either before mapping or after `Promise.all` to avoid leaking `undefined` into content arrays.
- The filtering of `thinking` content parts is implemented slightly differently in `convertOpenAIMessages` (via `.filter`) and `convertOpenAIResponseInputs` (via early `return undefined`); extracting a shared helper that returns a sanitized content array would reduce duplication and keep behavior consistent.
- There are several casts to `OpenAICompatibleContentPart` and `OpenAI.ChatCompletionContentPart` that weaken type safety; tightening the types of `message.content` or overloading `convertMessageContent` to accept `UserMessageContentPart` directly would remove the need for these `as` casts and make the intent clearer.

title: 🐛 fix(model-runtime): prune unsupported xAI reasoning penalties
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #12971

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This PR prunes unsupported penalty parameters for xAI reasoning models before requests are sent.

It fixes `grok-4` compatibility by removing `presence_penalty` and `frequency_penalty` from xAI reasoning model payloads in both the Chat Completions and Responses API paths, while preserving those parameters for non-reasoning variants.

It also adds regression tests to cover both reasoning and non-reasoning xAI model behavior.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

Local verification:

- `bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.mts 'src/providers/xai/index.test.ts'`
- `mcp__vscode_mcp__get_diagnostics` on the modified files

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| N/A | N/A |

#### 📝 Additional Information

This change is intentionally scoped to the xAI provider runtime layer to avoid changing shared parameter behavior for other OpenAI-compatible providers.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `pruneUnsupportedReasoningParameters` helper is typed to `ChatStreamPayload` but is also used for `responses.handlePayload`, forcing casts to `any`; consider widening the parameter type (or overloading) so both payload shapes are handled in a type-safe way without casts.
- `pruneUnsupportedReasoningParameters` also unconditionally clears `stop`, but the PR description and comment only mention penalties; if `stop` is also unsupported for reasoning models, it would be good to make that explicit in the helper name/comment or, if not, avoid clearing it to prevent surprising behavior for callers.
- The `isXAIReasoningModel` heuristic relies on substring matches like `'reasoning'` and `'multi-agent'`, which may accidentally classify future models; consider centralizing model IDs in a shared config or narrowing the matching logic to reduce the risk of unintended pruning when new models are added.

- The `pruneUnsupportedReasoningParameters` helper is typed to `ChatStreamPayload` but is also used for `responses.handlePayload`, forcing casts to `any`; consider widening the parameter type (or overloading) so both payload shapes are handled in a type-safe way without casts.
- `pruneUnsupportedReasoningParameters` also unconditionally clears `stop`, but the PR description and comment only mention penalties; if `stop` is also unsupported for reasoning models, it would be good to make that explicit in the helper name/comment or, if not, avoid clearing it to prevent surprising behavior for callers.

commits.message:
✨ feat: thread request trigger metadata through RouterRuntime and agent execution pipeline

Pass trigger (api/bot/cron/eval) from execAgent through state.metadata to
modelRuntime.chat() and RouterRuntime.onRouteAttempt, enabling downstream
billing and route-attempt logging to record request source.

✨ feat: add billing hooks for memory extraction generateObject calls

Pass ModelRuntimeHooks through resolveRuntimeAgentConfig so that
generateObject calls during memory extraction go through budget check
and cost tracking. Only attach hooks when provider resolves to lobehub
to avoid billing users who use their own API keys.

Also pass `{ trigger: 'memory' }` metadata to all generateObject calls
so SpendLog records the request source.

✨ feat: add embeddings billing hooks with onUsage callback support

- Add onUsage and metadata to EmbeddingsOptions for billing/tracing
- Wire onUsage in OpenAI factory and Azure embeddings with pricing
- Add beforeEmbeddings/onEmbeddingsError/onEmbeddingsFinal hooks to ModelRuntime
- Pass metadata through RouterRuntime embeddings for route attempt logging
- Add trigger metadata to memory extraction embeddings calls
- Remove redundant checkBudgetsUsage middleware (now handled by hooks)

✨ feat: add RequestTrigger enum and populate trigger metadata across all runtime calls

Define a centralized RequestTrigger enum in @lobechat/types and use it to tag
every embeddings/generateObject/chat call with its originating trigger, so that
SpendLog and RouteAttemptLog entries are never missing the trigger field.
✨ feat: add trigger column i18n and update type enums for usage page
🐛 fix: pass userId to ModelRuntime in memory extraction for route attempt logs
📝 docs: update typescript skill description to emphasize mandatory pre-read
✅ test: update memory extractor tests to match new trigger metadata argument
🐛 fix: forward hooks to non-lobehub provider paths in resolveRuntimeAgentConfig
⏪ revert: remove hooks from non-lobehub provider paths in resolveRuntimeAgentConfig
✨ feat: add lobehub embedding model with pricing and disable protection, measure embedding latency
🐛 fix: add @/database alias to model-runtime vitest config for cloud repo compatibility
✅ test: update outputJSON tests to match new trigger metadata argument

- For `UserModel.findByIds`, consider documenting that the result order is determined by the database query (not the input `ids` order) or explicitly sorting to match the input if callers might rely on positional alignment.

title: 🐛 fix(memory): respect agent-level memory toggle when injecting memories
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- No related issue found -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

**Root Cause:**

The memory toggle in ChatInput (`Memory/index.tsx`) writes to the **agent-level** config: `updateAgentChatConfig({ memory: { enabled: false } })`.

However, when actually sending messages, `chat/index.ts` only read the **user-level** setting:

```ts
// Before (bug): ignores agent-level chatConfig.memory.enabled
const enableUserMemories = settingsSelectors.memoryEnabled(getUserStoreState());
```

This meant: turning off memory for a specific agent had no effect — the user-level global setting was always used instead.

**Fix:**

Align the injection logic with `useMemoryEnabled` hook (agent-level takes priority, falls back to user-level):

```ts
// After (fix): matches useMemoryEnabled hook behavior
const userLevelMemoryEnabled = settingsSelectors.memoryEnabled(getUserStoreState());
const enableUserMemories = chatConfig.memory?.enabled ?? userLevelMemoryEnabled;
```

**Also fixes:** Pre-commit hook updated to use `bunx` instead of `npx --no-install` to ensure ESLint v10 (lobehub's version) is used in the monorepo context, not the cloud root's ESLint v8.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

**Regression tests added** in `src/services/chat/chat.test.ts` covering three scenarios:

1. Agent-level OFF + user-level ON → `enableUserMemories = false` (the bug scenario)
2. Agent-level ON + user-level OFF → `enableUserMemories = true`
3. Agent-level not set + user-level OFF → falls back to user-level `false`

**Manual testing:**
1. Enable memory globally in settings
2. In a chat, click the memory toggle in ChatInput to disable it for this agent
3. Send a message — memory should NOT be injected into the system prompt

reviews.body: We've reviewed this pull request using the Sourcery rules engine

commits.message:
🐛 fix(memory): respect agent-level memory toggle when injecting memories

When the user disables the memory toggle in ChatInput (which writes to
agent-level chatConfig.memory.enabled), the actual message-sending path
in chat/index.ts was only checking the user-level memoryEnabled setting,
completely ignoring the agent-level override.

This aligns the injection logic with useMemoryEnabled hook:
agent-level config takes priority, falls back to user-level setting.

Also fix pre-commit hook to use bunx instead of npx to ensure the
correct ESLint version (v10) is used in monorepo context.

Adds regression tests verifying all three priority scenarios.

title: ✨ feat(builtin-tool-local-system): skip intervention for safe paths like /tmp
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add a safe-path whitelist (`/tmp`, `/var/tmp`) to `pathScopeAudit` so that file operations targeting these ephemeral directories no longer trigger user intervention confirmation.

Previously, any path outside the working directory required a secondary confirmation dialog. This was unnecessarily disruptive for temporary/scratch paths that pose no security risk.

**Key behavior:**
- Operations where **all** paths target safe directories → no intervention
- Operations mixing safe and non-safe paths → still requires intervention
- No change to working-directory-scoped operations

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

```bash
cd packages/builtin-tool-local-system && bunx vitest run src/__tests__/interventionAudit.test.ts
```

28 tests pass, including new cases for `/tmp`, `/var/tmp`, and mixed-path scenarios.
