title: 🐛 fix: use display messages for token counting in group chats
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-4447

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The `TokenTag` component used `dbMessageSelectors.activeDbMessages` to count chat message tokens. This selector generates a message map key via `currentDbChatKey` which does **not** include `groupId`, causing it to return an empty array in group chats.

As a result, in Group Agent mode:
- `chatsToken` was always ~0
- `totalToken / maxTokens` was always far below 0.5
- The Context Details token tag was completely hidden (non-dev mode) or showed incorrect data (dev mode)

**Fix**: Use the `messageString` prop directly (sourced from `displayMessageSelectors.mainAIChatsMessageString`) which correctly generates the key via `currentDisplayChatKey` that includes `groupId`.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Open a Group Agent conversation
2. Send a few messages
3. Check the Context Details token popup in the chat input action bar
4. Verify "Chat Messages" shows a non-zero token count
5. Verify the TOKEN tag appears when usage exceeds 50% of context window

#### 📝 Additional Information

Removed unused imports (`useMemo`, `dbMessageSelectors`) and the now-unnecessary `historyCount`/`enableHistoryCount` subscription since `messageString` already reflects history config changes.

title: 🐛 fix(api): Fix the issue where custom AI Providers cannot use custom APIs
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

<!-- For change type, change [ ] to [x]. -->

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #11296 Fixes #11271 

#### 🔀 Description of Change

修复自定义 AI Provider 无法使用服务端 API 的问题。 

问题原因： 
客户端使用 sdkType（如 'azure'、'openai'）构建 API 端点路径，但服务端使用该路径作为 provider ID 从数据库查询配置。这导致服务端查询了内置 provider 的配置，而不是用户自定义 provider 的配置。 

修复方案： 
将 API 端点路径从使用 sdkType 改为使用原始的 provider 标识。这确保服务端能正确查询用户配置，然后从配置中读取 sdkType 来初始化相应的运行时。 

修改文件：
src/services/chat/index.ts：聊天 API 端点使用 provider
src/services/models.ts：模型列表和模型拉取 API 端点使用 provider

为什么客户端模式之前能正常工作： 
客户端模式不走服务器 API，而是直接在浏览器中通过 createPayloadWithKeyVaults(provider) 从 store 读取配置（使用原始 provider），然后使用 ModelRuntime.initializeWithProvider(runtimeProvider, ...) 选择 SDK（使用 sdkType）。两个参数职责清晰分离，不存在路径混淆问题。



#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

<!-- If this PR includes UI changes, please provide screenshots or videos -->

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| ...    | ...   |

#### 📝 Additional Information

<!-- Add any other context about the Pull Request here. -->

<!-- Breaking changes? Migration guide? Performance impact? -->

## Summary by Sourcery

Ensure server-side AI API requests use the correct provider identifier for routing and configuration lookup.

Bug Fixes:
- Fix server-side chat streaming endpoint to address custom AI providers being unable to use their configured APIs.
- Fix model listing and model pull endpoints so custom AI providers resolve against the correct provider configuration instead of the SDK type.
author: hardy-one

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Given the subtle distinction between `provider`, `runtimeProvider`, and `sdkType`, consider tightening the types or renaming parameters in `API_ENDPOINTS` and call sites so it’s harder to accidentally pass the wrong identifier in the future.
- It may be helpful to add a small helper (e.g. `getProviderEndpointId(provider)` or similar) to centralize the mapping logic and avoid repeating the raw `provider` vs `sdkType` choice across multiple services.

## Overall Comments
- Given the subtle distinction between `provider`, `runtimeProvider`, and `sdkType`, consider tightening the types or renaming parameters in `API_ENDPOINTS` and call sites so it’s harder to accidentally pass the wrong identifier in the future.
- It may be helpful to add a small helper (e.g. `getProviderEndpointId(provider)` or similar) to centralize the mapping logic and avoid repeating the raw `provider` vs `sdkType` choice across multiple services.
~~~

title: 🐛 fix(gtd): fix frozen object mutation in updateTodos
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

<!-- For change type, change [ ] to [x]. -->

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [*] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #11135 

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Fix `updateTodos` failing silently when trying to mutate frozen/immutable objects from Zustand store.

Problem
When `updateTodos` executes `update` or `complete` operations, it attempts to directly modify `TodoItem` objects:
```typescript
item.text = op.newText;  // ❌ Throws on frozen objects
item.completed = true;   // ❌ Throws on frozen objects
```

These objects returns references from Zustand store state. Since Zustand (with immer) freezes state objects, direct mutation throws TypeError: Cannot assign to read only property.


Solution
Create new objects instead of mutating existing ones:
```
// update operation
const updatedItem = { ...updatedTodos[op.index] };
updatedItem.text = op.newText;
updatedTodos[op.index] = updatedItem;

// complete operation
updatedTodos[op.index] = { ...updatedTodos[op.index], completed: true };
```

#### 🧪 How to Test

## Summary by Sourcery

Adjust chat history trimming logic so historyCount represents only prior messages and ensure the current user message is always included once.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes how `historyCount` is calculated when preparing messages for `contextEngineering`, so that a configured `historyCount` represents *only historical messages* and the request still includes the current user message (without unintentionally pulling in the last assistant message when `historyCount = 0`). **Changes:** - Adjust `historyCount` passed into `contextEngineering` from `+ 2` to `+ 1` (to account for the current user message only). - Apply the same fix in both the main `ChatService` flow and the `AgentRuntimeService` flow. - Add unit tests validating message slicing behavior for enabled/disabled `historyCount`, including the `historyCount = 0` edge case. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | src/services/chat/index.ts | Fixes `historyCount` calculation for chat message preprocessing so `0` history doesn’t include prior assistant messages. | | src/services/chat/chat.test.ts | Adds tests to assert correct message count/ordering for history enabled/disabled and `historyCount = 0`. | | src/services/agentRuntime/index.ts | Keeps agent runtime behavior consistent with ChatService by applying the same `historyCount + 1` logic. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

        messageSuffix 供后续 LLM 继续使用。

title: 🐛 fix: SPA HTML entry returns stale content after server upgrade
body:
## Summary

After upgrading my self-hosted LobeHub server (Docker image swap + restart), the desktop app and browsers kept serving the old SPA JavaScript — even though the server was already running new code. Took me a while to track down why my changes weren't taking effect.

The root cause: the SPA HTML entry point (`/spa/[variants]/[[...path]]/route.ts`) inherits Next.js's default ISR cache headers (`s-maxage=31536000`). Since the HTML is what references the hashed JS/CSS asset filenames, a stale HTML means stale everything — the client never discovers that new assets exist.

The JS/CSS files themselves are fine — they have content hashes in their filenames, so they're naturally cache-busted. It's only the HTML entry that needs to be revalidated on each request.

## Changes

One line: explicitly set `Cache-Control: no-cache` on the SPA HTML response. This tells clients to always revalidate with the server before using a cached copy. When the server hasn't changed, this is just a 304 — no performance impact. When it has changed, the client gets the new HTML immediately.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `curl -sI` returns `Cache-Control: no-cache` for the SPA route
- [x] Confirmed JS/CSS assets still served with normal hashed-filename caching

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Set the SPA HTML route to send a `Cache-Control: no-cache` header so clients always revalidate the HTML shell and pick up new asset versions after server changes.
author: SiruiHe

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Since the goal is to avoid long-lived intermediary caches rather than disabling browser caching entirely, consider using a more targeted directive like `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate, s-maxage=0` (or similar) to explicitly override Next.js's ISR defaults while keeping semantics clear for proxies and CDNs.
- It may be worth adding a brief code comment above this header explaining that the SPA HTML shell must always be revalidated because it contains hashed asset URLs, so future changes to caching headers elsewhere don’t accidentally reintroduce long-lived caching here.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

- It may be worth adding a brief code comment above this header explaining that the SPA HTML shell must always be revalidated because it contains hashed asset URLs, so future changes to caching headers elsewhere don’t accidentally reintroduce long-lived caching here.
~~~

title: 🐛 fix: load PDF.js worker from local assets via Vite ?url import
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

<!-- For change type, change [ ] to [x]. -->

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR -->

Related to PDF viewer worker loading failures in SPA routing context.

<!-- Example: Fixes #xxx, Closes #xxx, Related to #xxx -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

`react-pdf` auto-detects and sets `pdfjs.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc` to a bare filename `pdf.worker.mjs` at import time. The existing `ensureWorker()` guard (`if (!workerSrc)`) never overrides it because it's already set.

In the SPA, this bare filename resolves relative to the current page URL — e.g. when viewing a PDF on `/agent/xxx`, the browser requests `/agent/pdf.worker.mjs` which returns 404 since it's not a real static file.

This PR replaces the conditional workerSrc assignment with Vite's `?url` import:

```ts
import pdfjsWorkerUrl from 'pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs?url';
pdfjs.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc = pdfjsWorkerUrl;
```

Vite copies the worker file into the SPA assets directory at build time and returns the correct hashed path (e.g. /spa/assets/pdf.worker.min-abc123.mjs), which works regardless of the current page route.

<!-- Thank you for your Pull Request. Please provide a description above. -->

#### 🧪 How to Test

<!-- Please describe how you tested your changes -->

<!-- For AI features, please include test prompts or scenarios -->

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

<!-- If this PR includes UI changes, please provide screenshots or videos -->

N/A — no UI changes, only fixes worker file resolution path.


#### 📝 Additional Information

<!-- Add any other context about the Pull Request here. -->

<!-- Breaking changes? Migration guide? Performance impact? -->

## Summary by Sourcery

Ensure the PDF.js worker is always loaded from a Vite-managed local asset URL instead of a route-dependent or CDN-based path to fix worker loading failures in the SPA.

Bug Fixes:
- Force pdfjs GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc to use a Vite ?url-imported worker asset so PDF rendering works reliably across SPA routes.

Enhancements:
- Add a Vite type declaration for the pdf.js worker ?url import to maintain proper TypeScript support.
author: Xial-kotori

- Setting `pdfjs.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc` at module top-level means it will run in any environment that imports this file; consider guarding it with a runtime check (e.g. `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`) if this module is ever imported in SSR or node-based tooling.
- Now that `ensureWorker` and the CDN URL are removed, you can also clean up any unused types or utilities that were only needed for the old worker configuration to keep this module focused on the Vite asset-based approach.

title: ♻️ refactor: replace per-message useNewScreen with centralized useConversationSpacer
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Replace the old per-message `useNewScreen` hook (which applied `minHeight` to each ChatItem individually) with a centralized `useConversationSpacer` approach:

- **New `useConversationSpacer` hook**: Appends a single spacer element to the virtual list's data source when the user sends a new message pair (user + assistant). The spacer pushes the user message to the top of the viewport and shrinks as the assistant response grows, then unmounts when height reaches 0.
- **Removed `useNewScreen`**: Deleted `useNewScreen.ts` and its test (~420 lines removed). The `newScreenMinHeight` prop is removed from `ChatItem`.
- **Refactored `useScrollToUserMessage`**: Now accepts explicit parameters instead of reading from the store internally, improving testability.
- **AutoScroll gating**: `useAutoScrollEnabled()` is lifted to `VirtualizedList` so AutoScroll is not mounted at all when user has disabled it. AutoScroll is also disabled while the spacer is active (spacer and auto-scroll-to-bottom would conflict).
- **Cleanup**: Removed all debug `console.log` statements.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Send a message in a conversation — the user message should scroll to the top of the viewport
2. As the assistant responds, the spacer should shrink smoothly
3. After generation completes and the response fills the viewport, auto-scroll behavior should resume normally
4. Toggle "Auto scroll on streaming" in settings — AutoScroll should respect the setting

#### 📝 Additional Information

- Net reduction: ~160 lines removed
- The spacer integrates with virtua's `getItemOffset`/`getItemSize` APIs for accurate height calculation, with DOM fallback
- ResizeObserver tracks both user and assistant message elements to recalculate spacer height during streaming

## Summary by Sourcery

Centralize conversation viewport spacing around new messages and refine auto-scroll behavior in the virtualized chat list.

New Features:
- Introduce a conversation spacer hook that adds a dynamic spacer item to the virtualized message list to keep newly sent user messages pinned near the top of the viewport during assistant responses.

Enhancements:
- Remove the per-message new-screen logic and associated ChatItem API in favor of the centralized conversation spacer mechanism.
- Refine the scroll-to-user-message behavior to retry pinning the target message shortly after it is added for more reliable alignment.
- Gate AutoScroll mounting on the user’s auto-scroll setting and suspend AutoScroll while the conversation spacer is active to avoid conflicting behaviors.
- Expose additional virtual list scroll methods through the store for use by layout and spacing logic.

Build:
- Update the Vite envRestartKeys plugin to ignore test files when watching for environment-related restart triggers.

Tests:
- Adjust scroll-to-user-message tests to cover the new retry behavior and add tests around the conversation spacer hook while removing legacy new-screen tests.

Chores:
- Delete the legacy useNewScreen hook, its tests, and related wiring from message components and ChatItem.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Both `useScrollToUserMessage` and `useConversationSpacer` use `window`/`document`/`ResizeObserver` directly; consider guarding with `typeof window !== 'undefined'` (or similar) so these hooks are safe under SSR or non-DOM test environments.
- In `useConversationSpacer`, `latestAssistantSignature` is recomputed on every render via a `displayMessages.find(...)`; consider memoizing this based on the tracked assistant id and `displayMessages` to avoid unnecessary work in large conversations.
- The DOM-based path in `updateSpacerHeight` returns early when `userHeight` is 0, which can leave the spacer in a stale state if the element isn't yet measurable; you might want a retry or fallback path instead of an immediate return to make this more robust.

title: 🐛 fix(chat): clear input immediately on send to preserve drafts during streaming
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-2647

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When the AI is still streaming a response, text typed into the input box was being silently discarded because `inputMessage` was cleared **after** the full streaming lifecycle completed, overwriting any draft the user had entered in the meantime.

This PR fixes two issues:

1. **Input draft overwritten on send completion** — Move the `set({ inputMessage: '' })` call to **before** the async streaming lifecycle in `sendMessage`, so the editor is cleared immediately and any new text typed during streaming is preserved.

2. **Cancel operations fails in null-topic sessions** — `cancelOperations` compared `topicId` and `threadId` with strict equality, causing `null !== undefined` mismatches. Added a `isSameNullableContextValue` helper that normalizes both to `null` before comparison, so operations in sessions without a topic can be correctly cancelled.

#### 🧪 How to Test

1. Start a conversation and send a message
2. While the AI is streaming, type new text in the input box
3. After streaming completes, verify the new text is still in the input box and the send button is active
4. In a session without a topic, verify that cancelling an operation works correctly

- [x] Added/updated tests

#### 📝 Additional Information

Three test files updated covering both fixes:
- `store.test.ts` — verifies draft preservation during pending send
- `useOperationState.test.ts` — verifies loading clears after cancel in null-topic context
- `actions.test.ts` — verifies null/undefined topicId treated as same context

## Summary by Sourcery

Fix message sending and operation cancellation behavior to preserve user drafts during streaming and correctly handle null-topic sessions.

Bug Fixes:
- Clear the conversation input state immediately on send so drafts typed during AI streaming are preserved after the response completes.
- Normalize nullable context values when filtering operations so cancellations work correctly in sessions without a topic or with null/undefined identifiers.

Tests:
- Add conversation store tests to verify drafts are preserved during pending sends and after streaming completes.
- Extend operation state and actions tests to ensure input loading clears and cancellations succeed in null-topic and mixed null/undefined topicId contexts.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `isSameNullableContextValue` helper is currently local to `actions.ts`; if null/undefined equivalence for context values is a broader invariant, consider moving this to a shared utility (or at least co-locating with the context type definitions) so other slices can reuse it consistently instead of re-implementing similar checks.
- In `ConversationStore` tests, `vi.mocked(useChatStore.getState).mockReturnValue({ ...useChatStore.getState(), sendMessage: ... })` relies on calling the mocked `getState` inside its own mock, which can be brittle; capturing the original state before stubbing or constructing the minimal needed shape explicitly would make these tests more robust against future changes to the store or mocking setup.

## Summary by Sourcery

Improve Market OIDC authentication robustness and recovery for tools and sandbox calls.

Bug Fixes:
- Handle expired or invalid Market access tokens by attempting refresh and falling back to re-authentication when needed.
- Correctly surface Market authentication failures from tools and sandbox APIs as UNAUTHORIZED errors to trigger the auth flow.
- Prevent global LobeChat logout on Market-specific 401s and route them through a dedicated Market auth event system.
- Ensure Market tools client also participates in 401 handling and emits centralized market-unauthorized events.

Enhancements:
- Add automatic Market token refresh on initialization and shortly before expiry to maintain continuous authenticated sessions.
- Introduce a Market auth event emitter and handler to coordinate 401 recovery between client, tools, and auth provider.
- Stabilize Market sign-in and refresh behavior using memoized callbacks in the auth provider context.

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The token refresh logic in `MarketAuthProvider` is duplicated between `tryRefreshToken` and `refreshToken`; consider extracting the common ‘refresh and update session + DB’ steps into a shared helper to reduce divergence and future maintenance risk.
- The market 401 handling and debouncing logic is now implemented both in the lambda client `errorHandlingLink` and the tools client `errorHandlingLink`; it would be cleaner to centralize this in a shared utility to avoid subtle differences in behavior over time.
- The repeated checks for market auth errors (e.g., `invalid_token`, `token_expired`, `unauthorized`, and message substring checks) in the tools router handlers could be wrapped in a small helper function (e.g., `isMarketAuthError`) to keep the handlers more readable and reduce duplication.

title: ⚡️ perf: optimize tool system prompt — remove duplicate APIs, simplify XML tags
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ⚡️ perf
- [x] 💄 style

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5778

#### 🔀 Description of Change

**1. Remove duplicate API descriptions from system prompt (~2k tokens saved)**

API identifiers and descriptions were repeated in both the system prompt (`<api identifier="...">desc</api>`) and the tools schema passed via the API `tools` parameter. Since LLMs already see tool schemas natively, the system prompt repetition was pure token waste.

- Before: every tool's APIs listed in system prompt + tools schema = double
- After: only `systemRole` (usage instructions) injected; API descriptions removed

**2. Simplify XML tag structure**

- `<plugins>` → `<tools>`
- `<collection name="X">` → `<tool name="X">`
- `<collection.instructions>` → `<tool.instructions>`

**3. Tool prompt behavior**

- Tools with `systemRole` → `<tool name="X"><tool.instructions>...</tool.instructions></tool>`
- Tools with only `description` → `<tool name="X">description</tool>`
- Tools with neither → `<tool name="X">no description</tool>`

**4. Remove platform-specific Spotlight reference (LOBE-5778)**

Replace hardcoded "using Spotlight (macOS) or native search" with "using native search" in both web and desktop systemRole.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

**Token impact (measured via agent-tracing):**
- Before: 36.4k tokens for a simple query
- After: 18.9k tokens (~48% reduction)

title: ✨ feat: improve agent context injection (skills discovery, device optimization, prompt cleanup)
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ⚡️ perf
- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #12848 (partial — skills portion)
Related: LOBE-5777
close LOBE-5777

#### 🔀 Description of Change

**1. Skills auto-discovery**
- All installed skills (builtin + DB) now appear in `<available_skills>` in the system prompt
- AI can discover and activate them via `activateSkill` without manual user activation
- Fixed incorrect data source: was using provider manifests (showed user names like "Arvin Xu"), now uses actual skill metadata
- Both frontend (`contextEngineering.ts`) and backend (`serverMessagesEngine`) support skills injection

**2. Remote Device optimization**
- Suppress Remote Device tool (`listOnlineDevices`, `activateDevice`) when device is already auto-activated — saves ~500 tokens + 2 tool functions
- Added `autoActivated` flag in `deviceContext` to signal when device selection is unnecessary
- Moved `activeDeviceId` computation before tool engine creation
- Extracted `<online-devices>` prompt to `@lobechat/prompts` package with XML structure

**3. Local System systemRole split**
- Split `systemRole.ts` into web (remote device) and desktop versions
- Web version: XML device context (`<device name="" os="" arch="" />`), minimal paths, simplified `<response_format>` without `<localFile>` tags (LOBE-5777)
- Desktop version: keeps full user directory paths + `<localFile>` tag instructions
- Added `hostname` to `deviceSystemInfo` for device identification

**4. OpenAPI improvements**
- `model` field in Responses API now accepts `agentId` directly (previously only slug)

**5. Bug fixes**
- Fixed `structuredClone` `DataCloneError` in `InMemoryAgentStateManager` when DB errors contain DOM `ErrorEvent` objects

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

1. Skills: Install a skill, don't add to agent plugins → AI should see it in `<available_skills>`
2. Device: With 1 device online in Discord bot → Remote Device tool should NOT appear, Local System should work directly
3. OpenAPI: `POST /api/v1/responses` with `model: "<agentId>"` should work

title: 🐛 fix: use jsonb ? operator to avoid Neon rt_fetch bug
body:
## Summary
- Replace `metadata->>'cronJobId' IS NOT NULL` with `metadata ? 'cronJobId'` in `getCronTopicsGroupedByCronJob`
- The `->>` operator in WHERE clauses triggers a Neon-specific `rt_fetch used out-of-bounds` error
- The `?` operator is semantically equivalent (checks jsonb key existence) and works correctly on Neon

## Test plan
- [x] Verified via direct DB query that `metadata ? 'cronJobId'` returns results correctly
- [x] Verified `metadata->>'cronJobId' IS NOT NULL` reproduces the error on both pooler and direct connections

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Replace the JSONB text-extraction condition in cron topic queries with a key-existence operator to avoid the Neon rt_fetch error.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The switch from `->> ... IS NOT NULL` to `?` slightly changes semantics (keys with explicit `null` values will now be treated as present); if that matters, consider guarding against `null` explicitly or clarifying the intended behavior in a comment.
- Since this is working around a Neon-specific bug, it might help future maintainers to reference the Neon issue/bug tracker ID in the inline comment so it’s clear why the `?` operator is required.

- Since this is working around a Neon-specific bug, it might help future maintainers to reference the Neon issue/bug tracker ID in the inline comment so it’s clear why the `?` operator is required.

🐛 fix: use jsonb ? operator instead of ->> to avoid Neon rt_fetch bug

The ->> operator in WHERE clauses triggers a Neon-specific
`rt_fetch used out-of-bounds` error. Switch to the ? operator
which is semantically equivalent for checking key existence.

Related to #12961

#### 🔀 Description of Change

PR #12961 ("feat: Simplify UI") introduced `isDevMode` checks scattered across multiple NavBar components. This PR centralizes that logic:

- **New `useNavLayout` hook** (`src/hooks/useNavLayout.ts`): reads `isDevMode` and all feature flags once, returns pre-computed nav configuration with declarative `devOnly` metadata on each item
- **Nav.tsx**: simplified to pure renderer consuming `topNavItems` from hook
- **BottomMenu**: simplified to pure renderer consuming `bottomMenuItems` from hook
- **Footer**: uses `footer.layout` ('expanded' | 'compact') instead of reading `isDevMode` directly
- **UserPanel useMenu**: uses `userPanel.showMemory` / `showDataImporter` flags from hook
- **Home page**: restores `RecentTopic`, `RecentPage`, `CommunityAgents`, `RecentResource` modules gated behind `isDevMode`
- **LangButton**: fixes visual misalignment in UserPanel by removing standalone `<Menu>` wrapper, using matching dimensions (content-box height 28, marginBlock/Inline 4), disabling popup animation, and switching to hover trigger

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Toggle Developer Mode in Settings → Advanced
2. Verify NavBar items change correctly (Pages/Video/Image appear in top nav, Settings/Memory appear in bottom menu)
3. Verify Footer switches between expanded (icons + ThemeButton) and compact (dropdown only) layout
4. Verify Home page shows RecentTopic/RecentPage/CommunityAgents/RecentResource in dev mode
5. Verify LangButton in UserPanel aligns visually with other menu items
6. Verify language dropdown appears on hover without animation

#### 📝 Additional Information

The `useNavLayout` hook uses a `devOnly` metadata pattern:
- `devOnly: true` → only visible in dev mode
- `devOnly: false` → only visible in simplified mode
- `devOnly: undefined` → always visible

A single `filterByMode()` utility handles the filtering, making it trivial to add/remove nav items.

## Summary by Sourcery

Centralize navigation layout and dev-mode behavior into a shared hook while adjusting footer and user panel to support distinct dev and simplified modes.

Enhancements:
- Introduce a reusable useNavLayout hook to derive top nav, bottom menu, footer layout, and user panel visibility from dev mode and feature flags.
- Refactor Nav and BottomMenu components into thin renderers driven by the centralized navigation layout configuration.
- Adjust footer to support compact vs expanded layouts with conditional visibility for settings, GitHub, and evaluation entries based on mode.
- Gate home page recent content and community modules behind dev mode flags to align with the simplified UI strategy.
- Restyle the language selector in the user panel for better alignment and update its dropdown behavior to open on hover without animation.

title: ⚡️ perf: optimize search with BM25 indexes and ICU tokenizer
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [x] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5694

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Replace ILIKE full-table scans with ParadeDB BM25 full-text search for Command Menu (Cmd+K) search.

**Migration changes:**
- Add `pg_search` extension (0090)
- Create BM25 indexes with ICU tokenizer for all 8 search tables (0091)
- All indexes include `user_id` (keyword tokenizer + fast:true) for filter pushdown into tantivy index scan
- Messages index additionally includes `role` for `WHERE role != 'tool'` pushdown

**Search repository changes:**
- Replace `ILIKE '%query%'` with BM25 `@@@` operator
- Add `sanitizeQuery()` to escape tantivy special characters
- Replace JS-level `calculateRelevance()` (exact/prefix/contains) with `paradedb.score()` BM25 scoring mapped to 1-3 relevance range
- Expand search to query all indexed columns per table (e.g. agents: title, description, slug, tags)

**Performance (tested on Neon fork with production-scale data):**

| Query | Before | After | Speedup |
|-------|--------|-------|---------|
| "js" | 22.4s | 2.3s | 10x |
| "agent" | 29.0s | 2.3s | 13x |
| "压缩" | 22.5s | 0.5s | 45x |
| "zustand" | 3.6s | 0.5s | 7x |

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

#### 📝 Additional Information

- ICU tokenizer chosen for best Chinese+English mixed-language support
- `keyword` tokenizer with `fast:true` on `user_id`/`role` enables tantivy-level filter pushdown, avoiding post-filtering in PostgreSQL
- Tested on Neon PostgreSQL 17 with pg_search 0.15.26

commits.message:
⚡️ perf: optimize search with BM25 and improve memory search

- Migrate sidebar, command menu, and memory page search from ILIKE to BM25
- Use AND semantics for multi-word queries (sanitizeBm25Query)
- Expand memory search to cover both parent and subtable fields
- Add chatGroup support to command menu search
- Add skipIf guards for BM25-dependent tests in PGlite environment
- Fix contexts page not resetting on search and total not updating
✅ test: fix BM25 sanitize test assertions to match AND-joining behavior

🐛 fix: address BM25 search regressions and test failures

- Treat hyphens as word separators in sanitizeBm25Query (align with ICU tokenizer)
- Add ilike fallback for non-BM25-indexed fields (documents.filename, topics.historySummary)
- Update relevance ranking tests for BM25 score-based values instead of exact 1/2/3
- Skip searchTopics integration test (requires pg_search extension)

🐛 fix: add ilike fallback for file name search and skip message integration test

- files.name BM25 may not match due to tokenization; add ilike fallback
- Skip searchMessages integration test (requires pg_search extension)

🐛 fix: split file search into BM25 query + JOIN enrichment

LEFT JOIN with paradedb.score() interferes with BM25 index scan.
Split into two queries: BM25 search on files table first, then
enrich with document content and knowledgeBase info via separate JOIN.
Also remove all ilike fallbacks - pure BM25 search.

🐛 fix: revert file search to single query and fix test data for ICU tokenizer

ICU tokenizer treats hyphenated/dotted names as single tokens, so
"react-component.jsx" and "test.txt" are not split into separate words.
Revert unnecessary two-step query, add comment documenting the limitation,
and update test data to use space-separated names that ICU tokenizes properly.

title: ✨ feat: add ModelRuntime hooks for billing lifecycle interception
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to LOBE-5904

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add a generic hooks mechanism to `ModelRuntime` for lifecycle interception (billing, tracing, etc.):

- **`ModelRuntimeHooks` interface**: `beforeChat` (pre-call, throw to abort) + `onChatFinal` (post-stream callback)
- **`ModelRuntime.chat()`**: Calls `beforeChat` before the LLM call; merges `onChatFinal` into `onFinal` callback internally
- **`ChatMethodOptions.metadata`**: New optional field for passing caller context (billing metadata, tracing info) to hooks
- **`initModelRuntimeWithUserPayload`**: Accepts optional `hooks` parameter
- **`initModelRuntimeFromDB`**: Auto-injects hooks via `getBusinessModelRuntimeHooks` business hook
- **Business stub**: `getBusinessModelRuntimeHooks` returns `undefined` in OSS (cloud overrides with billing logic)
- **RuntimeExecutors**: Passes `operationId`/`topicId` metadata to `chat()` call
- **OpenAPI ChatService**: Gets hooks and passes to runtime initialization

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

#### 📝 Additional Information

Cloud repo will override `getBusinessModelRuntimeHooks` to inject budget pre-check and cost tracking. This replaces the previous per-route billing pattern with a unified hook at the ModelRuntime level.

## Summary by Sourcery

Introduce a hookable ModelRuntime lifecycle for pre- and post-chat interception and wire it through server, OpenAPI chat service, and business layer.

New Features:
- Add ModelRuntimeHooks interface to support beforeChat and onChatFinal lifecycle hooks around LLM chat calls.
- Allow ModelRuntime initialization functions to accept optional hooks so business logic can intercept runtime operations.
- Add optional metadata field to ChatMethodOptions for passing contextual information such as billing or tracing data to hooks.
- Expose ModelRuntimeHooks from the model-runtime package and add a business-layer getBusinessModelRuntimeHooks hook provider stub.

Enhancements:
- Propagate operation and topic identifiers as metadata into chat calls to enrich hook context.
- Wire business-provided ModelRuntime hooks into runtime initialization from DB and OpenAPI ChatService paths so cloud deployments can inject billing logic.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `ModelRuntime.chat`, `finalOptions` is built as `{ ...options, ...merged }` where `merged` already comes from `mergeMultipleChatMethodOptions([hookCallback, ...(options ? [options] : [])])`; this can lead to subtle overrides of merged values (e.g., callbacks/headers) and is likely clearer/safer to just pass `merged` directly as the options.
- When wiring `onChatFinal` into the `onFinal` callback, any thrown error or rejection from the hook will currently propagate via the underlying runtime; consider wrapping the hook call in a try/catch (or using a safe wrapper) so billing/tracing failures don’t interfere with normal response completion.

title: 🐛 fix: return structured error from invokeBuiltinTool instead of undefined
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5318

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When `invokeBuiltinTool` cannot find a registered executor for a tool, it previously returned `undefined` silently, causing the `call_tool` executor to mark the operation as failed with no meaningful error message, and the agent loop to terminate abnormally.

**Changes:**
1. **`pluginTypes.ts`**: `invokeBuiltinTool` now returns a structured error object `{ content, error, success: false }` instead of `undefined`
2. **`createAgentExecutors.ts`**: `call_tool` executor adds a fallback for `undefined`/`null` results, generating a meaningful error result so the agent loop can continue and the LLM can adjust its strategy

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

#### 📝 Additional Information

This is a defensive fix. Fix 1 addresses the root cause (structured error return), and Fix 2 adds a safety net in `call_tool` for any other plugin type that might also return `undefined`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Ensure built-in tool invocations always yield structured results instead of silent undefined values, allowing the agent loop to handle tool failures gracefully.

Bug Fixes:
- Return a structured error result when no executor is found for a built-in tool instead of returning undefined.
- Add a fallback in the call_tool executor to convert undefined or null tool results into a meaningful error result so the agent loop can continue.

- The structured error objects you’re returning in both `invokeBuiltinTool` and the `call_tool` fallback are inlined; consider reusing a shared result/error type or factory to keep the shape and error `type`/`message` values consistent across tools.
- In the `call_tool` fallback branch, the error message and type strings are duplicated between the operation failure and the `fallbackResult`; extracting these to a constant or helper would reduce the risk of them drifting out of sync.

title: 👷 build: add BM25 indexes with ICU tokenizer for search optimization
body:
This release includes a **database schema migration** adding **BM25 full-text search indexes** with ICU tokenizer across 14 tables.

### Migration: Add BM25 Indexes with ICU Tokenizer

- Added BM25 indexes for **14 tables**: `agents`, `topics`, `files`, `knowledge_bases`, `user_memories`, `chat_groups`, `user_memories_contexts`, `user_memories_preferences`, `user_memories_activities`, `user_memories_identities`, `user_memories_experiences`, `user_memory_persona_documents`, `documents`, `messages`
- All indexes use **ICU tokenizer** with English stemmer and stopwords for multilingual support
- Each index includes `user_id` as a keyword field with `fast` flag for efficient filter pushdown
- Enum/filter fields (`type`, `status`, `memory_layer`, `memory_category`, `relationship`, `source_type`, `role`) added as `keyword+fast` for filter pushdown into tantivy index scan
- `chat_groups.content` (system prompt) and `messages.summary` added as searchable text fields
- Large tables (`documents`, `messages`) are ordered last to avoid blocking smaller index builds
- All `CREATE INDEX` statements are preceded by `DROP INDEX IF EXISTS` for idempotency

### Notes for Self-hosted Users

- The migration runs automatically on application startup
- Requires the `pg_search` extension (added in migration 0090)
- Index creation on large tables (`messages`, `documents`) may take several minutes depending on data volume
- No manual intervention required

The migration owner: @tjx666 — responsible for this database schema change, reach out for any migration-related issues.

## Summary by Sourcery

Add a database migration that creates BM25 full-text search indexes with ICU tokenization across key content and metadata tables to improve search performance and filter pushdown.

New Features:
- Introduce BM25 full-text search indexes with ICU tokenizer on 14 user- and content-related tables for richer search capabilities.
- Make additional text fields such as chat group content and message summaries searchable via the new indexes.

Enhancements:
- Configure keyword+fast fields for user_id and other filterable enums to enable efficient filter pushdown during index scans.
- Order index creation so large tables are processed last and guard all index creation with DROP INDEX IF EXISTS for idempotent migrations.

Build:
- Add migration 0093 and its snapshot to the database migration system to apply the new BM25 indexes automatically on startup.
author: tjx666

<code_context>
+  key_field = 'id',
+  text_fields = '{
+    "description":  {"tokenizer": {"type": "icu", "stemmer": "English", "stopwords_language": "English"}},
+    "role":         {"tokenizer": {"type": "icu", "stemmer": "English", "stopwords_language": "English"}},
+    "type":         {"fast": true, "tokenizer": {"type": "keyword"}},
+    "relationship": {"fast": true, "tokenizer": {"type": "keyword"}},
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion (performance):** Align `role` indexing with the stated keyword+fast strategy for enum-like fields.

The header notes that enum-like fields (type, status, role, etc.) should use `keyword` + `fast` for efficient filter pushdown, but `role` is configured as a stemmed ICU text field here. If `role` is a categorical/enum field, consider switching it to `{ "fast": true, "tokenizer": { "type": "keyword" } }` to reduce index size and avoid unnecessary tokenization. If it’s actually free‑form text, the header comment should be updated to reflect that and prevent confusion.

```suggestion
    "role":         {"fast": true, "tokenizer": {"type": "keyword"}},
```
</issue_to_address>
~~~

The platform resolve plugin picks up `.vite` variants, so this stub stops Vite SPA warmup from traversing the server router graph.

- [ ] No tests needed

Run `bun run dev:spa` and verify the SPA warmup completes without errors.

title: 🐛 fix: respect agent-level memory config priority over user settings
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #12980

#### 🔀 Description of Change

**Problem**: `globalMemoryEnabled` only checked user-level `settings.memory.enabled`, completely ignoring agent-level `chatConfig.memory.enabled`. This caused memory tools to be injected even when an agent explicitly disabled memory. The UI toggle also didn't reflect the actual state correctly.

**Fix**: Agent-level memory config now takes priority:
1. Agent `chatConfig.memory.enabled = false` → disabled
2. Agent `chatConfig.memory.enabled = true` → enabled
3. Agent has no memory config → fallback to user-level setting (default: enabled)

**Changes**:
- **Backend** (`aiAgent/index.ts`): Compute `globalMemoryEnabled` with agent-level priority
- **Frontend UI** (`Memory/Controls.tsx`, `Memory/index.tsx`): New `useMemoryEnabled` hook that combines agent store + user store fallback, so the toggle reflects actual state
- **Frontend tool engine** (`toolEngineering/index.ts`): Same priority logic for client-side tool enablement
- **Integration tests**: 4 tests with real DB verifying the full flow

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

1. Create an agent, disable memory in agent config → memory tools should NOT appear
2. Enable memory in agent config → memory tools should appear regardless of user setting
3. Remove memory config from agent → should fallback to user-level setting
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The agent-vs-user memory precedence logic is now duplicated in the backend (`execAgent`), the client tools engine, and the new `useMemoryEnabled` hook—consider extracting a shared helper so future changes to the precedence rules don’t get out of sync across these call sites.
- In `createAgentToolsEngine`, `agentChatConfigSelectors.currentChatConfig(agentState).memory?.enabled` assumes `currentChatConfig` always returns an object; using optional chaining on the result (e.g. `agentChatConfigSelectors.currentChatConfig(agentState)?.memory?.enabled`) would make this more robust to undefined configs.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The agent-vs-user memory precedence logic is now duplicated in the backend (`execAgent`), the client tools engine, and the new `useMemoryEnabled` hook—consider extracting a shared helper so future changes to the precedence rules don’t get out of sync across these call sites.
- In `createAgentToolsEngine`, `agentChatConfigSelectors.currentChatConfig(agentState).memory?.enabled` assumes `currentChatConfig` always returns an object; using optional chaining on the result (e.g. `agentChatConfigSelectors.currentChatConfig(agentState)?.memory?.enabled`) would make this more robust to undefined configs.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="src/features/ChatInput/ActionBar/Memory/useMemoryEnabled.ts" line_range="11-16" />
<code_context>
+ * Agent-level config takes priority; falls back to user-level setting.
+ */
+export const useMemoryEnabled = (agentId: string): boolean => {
+  const agentMemoryEnabled = useAgentStore(
+    (s) => chatConfigByIdSelectors.getMemoryToolConfigById(agentId)(s)?.enabled,
+  );
+  const userMemoryEnabled = useUserStore(settingsSelectors.memoryEnabled);
+
+  return agentMemoryEnabled ?? userMemoryEnabled;
+};
</code_context>
<issue_to_addr

title: 👷 build: add `agent_documents` table
body:
This release includes a **database schema migration** adding **1 new table** for the Agent Document system.

### Migration: Add Agent Documents Table

- Added `agent_documents` table with the following capabilities:
  - Links agents to canonical document rows with per-user ownership
  - 5-bit access bitmask for self / shared / public channels (delete, list, write, read, execute)
  - Policy-based context injection control (`policyLoad`, `policyLoadPosition`, `policyLoadFormat`, `policyLoadRule`)
  - Soft delete support with actor tracking and reason

### Notes for Self-hosted Users

- The migration runs automatically on application startup
- No manual intervention required

The migration owner: @nekomeowww — responsible for this database schema change, reach out for any migration-related issues.
author: nekomeowww

reviews.body:

- Consider using a more constrained type (e.g., enum or a check constraint) instead of plain varchar for `auto_load_access`, `policy_load_position`, and `policy_load_rule` to prevent invalid string values and better document the allowed states at the database level.

title: 🛠 chore: support injectable snapshot store in AgentRuntimeService
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to LOBE-5599, LOBE-5590

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add optional `snapshotStore` to `AgentRuntimeServiceOptions` so callers can inject a custom `ISnapshotStore` implementation for production trace persistence (e.g. S3-backed store).

**Changes:**

1. **`AgentRuntimeServiceOptions.snapshotStore`** — new optional field accepting any `ISnapshotStore` implementation
2. **`executeStep()` refactor** — the two dev-only tracing blocks now use the injected store when available, falling back to `FileSnapshotStore` in dev mode
3. **`ExecutionSnapshot` type** — added `agentId` and `userId` optional fields for downstream storage partitioning

This is a non-breaking change. Without `snapshotStore`, behavior is identical to before (dev-only `FileSnapshotStore`).

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] No tests needed — existing executeStep tests cover the fallback path; injected store path will be tested in cloud repo

#### 📝 Additional Information

This is the open-source half of the agent snapshot S3 persistence feature. The cloud repo will implement `S3SnapshotStore` and inject it at the `/api/agent/run` route level.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add support for an injectable snapshot store in agent runtime to enable production-grade trace persistence while preserving existing dev-only file-based behavior.

New Features:
- Allow AgentRuntimeService to accept an optional snapshotStore for persisting execution traces across steps and completion.
- Extend ExecutionSnapshot type to include optional agentId and userId fields for downstream partitioning of stored traces.

Enhancements:
- Refine executeStep tracing logic to use the injected snapshot store when available, fall back to FileSnapshotStore in development, and log failures instead of silently swallowing them.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:

- The logic that selects and instantiates the snapshot store (in `executeStep` and in the finalization block) is duplicated; consider extracting a small helper (e.g. `getSnapshotStore()`) so the behavior stays consistent and easier to change.
- Since `snapshotStore` is stored as `ISnapshotStore | null` but the option type is non-nullable `ISnapshotStore`, you could tighten the field type (and initialization) to reflect that only `null` or a fully initialized store is ever used, which would simplify some of the `if (!store && dev)` branching.

commits.message:
✨ feat: support injectable snapshot store in AgentRuntimeService

Add optional `snapshotStore` to `AgentRuntimeServiceOptions` so that
callers can inject a custom `ISnapshotStore` implementation (e.g. S3)
for production trace persistence. Dev mode still falls back to
`FileSnapshotStore` when no store is injected.

Also adds `agentId` and `userId` fields to `ExecutionSnapshot` type
for downstream storage partitioning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: add S3SnapshotStore and wire up in agent run route

Add S3-backed snapshot store implementation and inject it into the
agent step execution route when S3 env vars are configured.

- S3SnapshotStore: persists final snapshots at agent-traces/{agentId}/{operationId}.json
- Partial snapshots accumulated via S3 read-modify-write per step
- Auto-enabled when S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET/ENDPOINT/BUCKET are set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: unify snapshot store initialization in route

- Route creates FileSnapshotStore (dev) or S3SnapshotStore (prod)
- executeStep() only uses the injected store, no dev-mode fallback
- Cleaner separation: route decides which store, service just uses it

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: dedicated env var for S3 tracing + restore S3 partials

- ENABLE_AGENT_S3_TRACING=1 → S3SnapshotStore
- NODE_ENV=development → FileSnapshotStore
- Otherwise → no tracing
- Restore S3 partial read-modify-write (needed for QStash cross-request)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: add topicId to snapshot and S3 path

S3 final path: agent-traces/{agentId}/{topicId}/{operationId}.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: auto-create FileSnapshotStore in dev mode

AgentRuntimeService constructor now auto-creates FileSnapshotStore
when NODE_ENV=development and no snapshotStore is injected. This
ensures dev tracing works for all execution paths (tRPC, executeSync,
LocalQueue) without manual injection.

Route only handles S3 store creation (ENABLE_AGENT_S3_TRACING=1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

♻️ refactor: internalize snapshot store creation in AgentRuntimeService

Move all snapshot store logic into AgentRuntimeService constructor:
- ENABLE_AGENT_S3_TRACING=1 → S3SnapshotStore
- NODE_ENV=development → FileSnapshotStore
- Otherwise → null

Route no longer needs to know about snapshot stores at all.
All execution paths (QStash, tRPC, executeSync) get tracing
automatically based on environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat(agent-tracing): support inspecting snapshots from URL

`agent-tracing inspect <url>` now auto-fetches remote snapshot JSON
when the argument is an HTTP(S) URL, enabling inspection of S3-stored
production traces directly from the CLI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat(agent-tracing): add `at` bin alias and `i` inspect shorthand

- `at` as short alias for `agent-tracing` CLI
- `at i` as shorthand for `at inspect`

Usage: `bunx at i <url>` or `bunx at i -s 0 -r`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Summary

Add i18n keys and locale translations for the auto top-up feature in the cloud repo.

- Add i18n keys for auto top-up settings UI (threshold, targetBalance, monthlyLimit labels and tooltip descriptions)

title: 🐛 fix: prevent Enter key submission during IME composition in LoginStep
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Extract `useIMECompositionEvent` hook to fix Enter key submission during IME (Input Method Editor) composition across multiple input components.

**Problem:** When using IME (e.g. Chinese, Japanese input), pressing Enter to confirm candidate characters would accidentally trigger form submission or send actions instead of just selecting the candidate.

**Solution:** Created a reusable `useIMECompositionEvent` hook that tracks IME composition state via `onCompositionStart`/`onCompositionEnd` and provides `isComposingRef` for key handlers to skip Enter during composition.

**Components updated:**
- ChatInput InputEditor
- FormInput
- FormPassword
- TextArea
- AgentSetting AutoGenerateInput
- Desktop Onboarding LoginStep

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Open desktop Onboarding flow, select self-hosted login
2. Type in the endpoint input using Chinese/Japanese IME
3. Press Enter to select candidate — should NOT trigger connect
4. After composition ends, press Enter — should submit correctly

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| Enter during IME triggers connect | Enter during IME selects candidate only |

#### 📝 Additional Information

<!-- Breaking changes? Migration guide? Performance impact? -->
author: Coooolfan

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `isChineseInput` ref name is a bit misleading since `compositionstart`/`compositionend` apply to any IME; consider renaming it to something like `isComposing` to better reflect its purpose.
- Instead of manually tracking composition state, you may be able to rely on `e.nativeEvent.isComposing` in the `onKeyDown` handler (`if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.nativeEvent.isComposing)`) to simplify the logic and avoid extra refs.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

Please address the comments from this code review:

- Instead of manually tracking composition state, you may be able to rely on `e.nativeEvent.isComposing` in the `onKeyDown` handler (`if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.nativeEvent.isComposing)`) to simplify the logic and avoid extra refs.

title: ✨ feat: add slash action tags, topic reference tool, and command bus system
body:
## Summary

Introduce slash action tags in the chat input editor that allow users to trigger skills and actions via `/` commands, add a topic reference builtin tool for referencing conversation topics, and implement a command bus system for processing slash commands during AI chat.

## Changes

### Slash Action Tags (Chat Input)
- `ActionTag` Lexical node and plugin for rendering `/skill` tags in the editor
- `ActionTagPlugin` handles slash trigger, menu navigation, and tag insertion
- `useSlashActionItems` / `useEnabledSkills` hooks for populating available actions
- `ReferTopic` node and plugin for `@topic` mention references
- `useAgentMentionItems` / `useTopicMentionItems` for mention data sources
- Refactored `InputEditor` to use modular plugin system (`plugins.ts`)
- Removed legacy `useSlashItems.tsx`

### Command Bus System
- `parseCommands` - extracts structured commands from message content (skills, topic refs)
- `processCommands` - applies parsed commands to chat params (skill preload, topic context)
- `editorDataHelpers` - converts Lexical editor state to command metadata
- `handlers.ts` / `types.ts` - extensible command handler registry

### Topic Reference Builtin Tool
- New `@lobechat/builtin-tool-topic-reference` package with manifest and executor
- Server runtime in `serverRuntimes/topicReference.ts`
- TRPC endpoint `topic.getTopicContent` for fetching topic messages

### Context Engine Enhancements
- `SelectedSkillInjector` provider for injecting selected skill context
- `AgentManagementContextInjector` updated with metadata support
- Added `MessageMetadata` types to `types.ts`
- All processors/providers updated with optional metadata parameter

### Conversation Flow
- `conversationLifecycle` refactored to support command bus pipeline
- `streamingExecutor` enhanced with skill preloading and command processing
- `skillPreload.ts` service for pre-fetching skill context before LLM calls
- Message compression utilities (`compression.ts`)

### UI Updates
- `RichTextMessage` component for rendering structured user messages
- `MessageContent` updated to support `editorData` rendering
- `PluginTag` filters tools by environment visibility
- `EditorCanvas` / `EditorModal` support `editorData` persistence
- i18n keys added for editor actions and plugin labels

## Type of Change

- [x] New feature
- [x] Enhancement to existing feature

## Testing

- Unit tests for `parseCommands`, `processCommands`, `compression`, `skillPreload`
- `RichTextMessage.test.tsx`, `MessageContent.test.tsx`, `EditorCanvas.test.tsx`
- `conversationLifecycle.test.ts`, `streamingExecutor.test.ts`, `state.test.ts`
- `SelectedSkillInjector.test.ts`, `AgentManagementContextInjector.test.ts`
- `metadata.types.test.ts`, `logic.test.ts`
- Follow-up regression test for placeholder parsing on malformed repeated `{{{{` input

## Follow-up Fixes

### CodeQL
- Addressed a CodeQL `js/polynomial-redos` alert in placeholder parsing.
- Root cause: the previous implementation used regex-based `{{...}}` matching on uncontrolled input, which could backtrack heavily on malformed input with many repeated opening braces.
- Fix: replaced regex scanning/replacement with a linear parser based on `indexOf('{{')` and `indexOf('}}')`, preserving existing placeholder behavior without regex backtracking.
- Validation: added regression coverage for spaced placeholders like `{{ username }}` and pathological repeated `{{{{` input.

title: ♻️ refactor: merge beta settings into advanced tab
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to settings UI consolidation.

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Merges the beta settings tab into the advanced settings tab to reduce UI complexity:

- **Removed**: Standalone `/settings/beta/` route (desktop-only)
- **Merged**: Update channel selection moved to advanced settings with conditional desktop-only rendering
- **Renamed**: All i18n keys from `tab.beta.*` to `tab.advanced.updateChannel.*` across 18 locale files
- **Deprecated**: `SettingsTabs.Beta` enum value marked as deprecated
- **Cleanup**: Removed unused `FlaskConical` icon import

The update channel feature is now accessible via the Advanced settings tab only on desktop, reducing navigation complexity while preserving functionality.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

**Test steps**:
1. Navigate to settings > Advanced (desktop build)
2. Verify "Update Channel" dropdown appears with options (Stable, Nightly, Canary)
3. Verify the option works as expected
4. On mobile, verify the Update Channel section is not shown
5. Verify settings still function normally in all locales

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| Separate "Beta" tab with update channel selection | Update channel integrated into "Advanced" tab |

#### 📝 Additional Information

This refactoring consolidates related settings and improves UX by reducing the number of settings tabs. The beta settings are now logically grouped with other advanced/experimental features.

**Note**: The `SettingsTabs.Beta` enum value remains available but marked as deprecated for backwards compatibility.

## Summary by Sourcery

Consolidate the desktop beta settings into the Advanced settings tab while preserving update channel selection functionality.

Enhancements:
- Expose update channel selection within the Advanced settings form on desktop only, backed by the existing auto-update service.
- Remove the dedicated Beta settings tab, its route, and associated desktop component wiring in favor of the Advanced tab.
- Deprecate the Beta settings enum entry while keeping it defined for backward compatibility.
- Clean up unused beta-related icon references and component mappings.

Documentation:
- Rename i18n keys for update channel texts from the beta tab namespace to the advanced tab namespace across all locales so labels and descriptions continue to render correctly.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The empty catch block in the `useEffect` around `autoUpdateService.getUpdateChannel()` silently swallows errors; consider at least logging or reporting them so update-channel retrieval failures can be surfaced and debugged.
- The `UpdateChannelValue` union and `channelOptions` are hardcoded here; if `autoUpdateService` or some shared config already defines the supported channels, consider reusing that source of truth to avoid divergence when channels change.
- Initializing the update-channel select to `'stable'` and rendering it immediately can cause a temporary mismatch before the real value loads; consider a loading state or deferring rendering until the channel has been fetched to avoid showing an incorrect selection briefly.

title: ✅ test: add unit tests for search impls (brave, exa, tavily)
body:
## Summary

- Added unit tests for `src/server/services/search/impls/brave`, `exa`, and `tavily`
- Total test files added: 3
- Test cases added: 59 (18 brave + 19 exa + 22 tavily)
- Coverage focus: HTTP request construction, response mapping, error handling, time range filtering, category filtering

## Changes

- [x] All tests pass successfully
- [x] Business logic coverage improved
- [x] Edge cases and error handling covered
- [x] Tests follow existing patterns

## Module Processed

`src/server/services/search/impls/brave/`
`src/server/services/search/impls/exa/`
`src/server/services/search/impls/tavily/`

## Test Coverage

- Functions tested: `BraveImpl.query`, `ExaImpl.query`, `TavilyImpl.query`
- Coverage type: unit
- Test approach: Mock `fetch` globally, assert on request construction (headers, method, body/query params) and response mapping (result fields, error codes)

Key scenarios covered per provider:
- Successful query with multiple results
- Empty results handling
- Time range filtering (day/week/month/year/anytime)
- Category filtering (news, general)
- API key injection in headers
- Network error → `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`
- Non-OK HTTP response → `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`
- JSON parse failure → `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`
- Default values (score, content, parsedUrl)

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add comprehensive unit test coverage for the Brave, Exa, and Tavily search provider implementations.

Tests:
- Add unit tests for BraveImpl.query covering request URL construction, time range handling, API key usage, response mapping, and error conditions.
- Add unit tests for ExaImpl.query covering POST request body construction, time range and category filters, API key headers, response mapping, and error handling.
- Add unit tests for TavilyImpl.query covering POST payload construction, time range and topic mapping, environment-driven depth settings, response mapping, and error scenarios.
author: lobehubbot

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `createMockResponse` helper and related response-factory helpers (`makeBraveResponse`, `makeExaResponse`, `makeTavilyResponse`) are duplicated across the three test files; consider extracting them into a shared test utility to reduce repetition and keep behavior consistent across providers.
- In the Brave invalid URL test, the comment says it should handle URL parsing errors gracefully or return an empty string, but the assertion expects `impl.query` to throw; it would be clearer to align the expectation (either update the code to handle this case and assert on the mapped result, or change the comment to reflect that throwing is the intended behavior).

title: 🚀 release: 20260313
body:
This release includes **~400 commits**. Key updates are below.

### New Features and Enhancements

- **Bot Platform Integration**: Added abstract bot platform layer with **QQ Bot**, **Telegram Bot**, **Lark/Feishu Bot**, and **Discord Bot** integrations, including remote device support for IM integration.
- **LobeHub CLI**: Full CLI implementation across 5 phases — agent run/status, generate (text/image/video/TTS/ASR), doc, search, device, bot integration, cron, topic share, agent KB/file/pin, thread, and eval commands.
- **Agent Skills**: Added built-in skills management, skill store, agent browser automation skill, and tool detection.
- **Video Generation**: End-to-end video generation feature with free quota, webhook handling, and skeleton loading.
- **Agent Benchmark**: Added benchmark support with external scoring mode and dedicated DB schema.
- **Memory Settings**: Support for memory effort/tool permission configuration, user persona injection, and improved memory analysis.
- **Batch Topic Deletion** from file support.
- **Runtime Config** support for flexible deployment configuration.
- **V1 API** and **Response API** support (including OpenAI Responses API).
- **Device Code Auth Flow** for CLI authentication.
- **Emoji Reactions** for messages.
- **Starter Suggested Questions** and recommend agents.
- **Page Tabs** for Electron desktop.
- **Sort Topics by Updated Time** option.
- **Change Email Address** in profile settings.
- **Model Detail Dropdown** in model switch panel.
- Added **unread completion indicator** for agents and topics.

### Models and Provider Expansion

- New providers: **Straico**, **LongCat (美团)**.
- Added/updated model support:
  - **GPT-5.4** series
  - **Claude Sonnet 4.6** and **Claude Opus 4.6** (including Bedrock)
  - **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview** and **Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Preview**
  - **Qwen3.5** series (including Flash, OSS, and SiliconCloud models)
  - **Grok 4.20** series and **Grok Imagine** image generation
  - **Kimi K2.5** thinking models
  - **MiniMax 2.5** / **MiniMax M2.5**
  - **Nano Banana 2**
  - **Seedream 5 Lite** / **Seedance 2.0**
  - **NVIDIA** new models
  - **GLM-5**, **GLM-4.6V**, **GLM-Image** for Zhipu
  - Additional Qwen image-to-image and text-to-image models
- Added video input support for SiliconCloud provider.
- Use Response API for Grok as default.

### Desktop Improvements

- Integrated `electron-liquid-glass` for macOS Tahoe.
- Unified canary with stable app name/icon, added channel tag in About.
- Support clearing hotkey bindings in ShortcutManager.
- Subscription pages embedding with webview.
- Enhanced desktop menu and navigation system.
- Proactive token refresh on app startup and activation.
- DMG background image configuration.
- S3 publish for canary/nightly with cleanup.
- Unified update channel switching with S3 distribution.

### Architecture and Infrastructure

- **Vite SPA Migration**: Migrated frontend from Next.js App Router to Vite SPA, restructured SPA routes to `src/routes` and `src/router`.
- **Response API Support** across agent runtime.
- Refactored client agent runtime and centralized tool availability checks.
- Added Redis pipeline support and Lua script execution.
- Database migrations: `pg_search` extension, video generation schema, agent skills schema, benchmark schema, topics description column, API key hash column, ID migration to nanoid.
- Preload bundled i18n resources with lazy-load for target language.
- Simplified build config, removed webpack customization, and resolved Vercel OOM.
- Class-based Zustand actions with `flattenActions` migration.
- Extracted `@lobechat/local-file-shell` shared package.
- Resolved all ESLint suppressions and enabled `consistent-type-imports` rule.

### Stability, Security, and UX Fixes

- Fixed model provider popup problems and ModelSelect crash.
- Fixed tool engine, input-loading, and MCP tool install loading issues.
- Hardened Anthropic message building and sampling parameter handling.
- Fixed Vertex AI 400 error from duplicate tool function declarations.
- Fixed context window exceeded error detection from message text.
- Added rate limit custom rules for password reset and email verification.
- Fixed `sanitizeFileName` path traversal risks.
- Fixed multiple Docker build issues (`@napi-rs/canvas`, `librt.so.1`, `ffmpeg-static`).
- Fixed desktop advanced mode, onboarding redirect, and auth modal during onboarding.
- Added unsaved changes guard to prevent data loss on navigation.
- Fixed SiliconCloud thinking mode toggle issue.
- Improved Moonshot interleaved thinking and circular dependency.
- Fixed multimodal `content_part` images rendered as base64 text.
- Security: upgraded `next-mdx-remote` to v6 for CVE-2026-0969.

### Credits

Huge thanks to these contributors (alphabetical):

@AmAzing- @AntoineRoux @BrandonStudio @CanisMinor @Coooolfan @eronez @Hardy @huangkairan @Innei @Kingsword @LiJian @LuisSambrano @MarcellGu @MikeLambert @Neko @rdmclin2 @Rdmclin2 @RenéWang @RuxiaoYin @RylanCai @Shinji-Li @Sun13138 @sxjeru @VarunChawla @WangYK @YuTengjing @Zephyr @ZhijieHe
author: rdmclin2

commits.message:
👷 build(ci): fix changelog auto-generation in release workflow (#12763)

After auto-tag-release.yml was introduced, semantic-release in release.yml
stopped working because the tag already exists when it runs. This caused
CHANGELOG.md to never be updated.

Fix: move changelog generation into auto-tag-release.yml with a custom
script that parses git log and generates gitmoji-formatted entries,
matching the existing CHANGELOG.md format. Remove the broken
semantic-release step from release.yml.
🐛 fix: resolve message reordering in Responses API input conversion (#12764)

🐛 fix(ci): add version prefix to latest*.yml URLs in S3 upload (#12770)

The latest*.yml files uploaded to S3 channel root lacked the $VERSION/
prefix in their URLs, causing electron-updater to request files at
the wrong path (e.g., /canary/LobeHub-Canary-xxx.zip instead of
/canary/2.1.38-canary.1/LobeHub-Canary-xxx.zip), resulting in 404.

Now sed -i modifies latest*.yml in-place before uploading, and
channel-specific yml files are copied from the already-modified ones.

🐛 fix(ci): add version prefix to S3 update manifest URLs (#12772)

🐛 fix(ci): target channel yml files instead of latest*.yml for version prefix

The merge-mac-files step already renames latest*.yml to {channel}*.yml
(e.g., canary-mac.yml). The previous fix targeted release/latest*.yml
which matched nothing, so the sed was a no-op.

Now targets release/${CHANNEL}*.yml directly, with latest*.yml as fallback.

✨ feat(cli): add full API integration commands In cli (#12795)

* ✨ feat(cli): add full API integration commands

Add comprehensive CLI commands for managing LobeHub resources:

P0 - Search, Knowledge Base, Memory:
- `lh search` - Global unified search across all resource types
- `lh kb` - Knowledge base CRUD, file management
- `lh memory` - User memory CRUD (identity/activity/context/experience/preference), persona, extraction

P1 - Agent, Session, Topic, Message:
- `lh agent` - Agent CRUD (list/view/create/edit/delete/duplicate)
- `lh session` - Session management with search
- `lh topic` - Topic CRUD with search and recent
- `lh message` - Message listing, search, delete, count, heatmap

P2 - Model, Provider:
- `lh model` - Model listing, toggle, delete per provider
- `lh provider` - Provider listing, toggle, delete

P3 - Plugin, Config:
- `lh plugin` - Plugin install/uninstall/update
- `lh whoami` - User info display
- `lh usage` - Usage statistics (monthly/daily)

Also refactors shared formatting utilities into utils/format.ts.
All commands support `--json` output for scripting.

Closes LOBE-5706, LOBE-5770

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: redirect to desktop onboarding when not completed (#12866)

* 🐛 fix: redirect to desktop onboarding when not completed

Desktop app was missing the redirect to `/desktop-onboarding` when
onboarding hadn't been completed. The `useDesktopUserStateRedirect`
callback silently returned instead of navigating, causing:
- Users never see the onboarding flow on fresh install
- `AuthRequiredModal` suppressed because onboarding guard fails

* 🐛 fix: remove desktop onboarding routes from proxy configuration

The `/desktop-onboarding` and its regex route have been removed from the proxy configuration. This change simplifies the routing logic as the onboarding flow is now handled directly in the user state redirect logic.

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>

## Summary by Sourcery

Reorganize settings and navigation, simplify default chat UI, and gate advanced controls behind a developer mode while updating terminology and suggestions across the app.

New Features:
- Add a consolidated Service Model settings page for agent, image, and speech configuration, and a new Appearance settings page combining general and chat appearance options.
- Introduce an agent profile header menu with advanced settings and community publish actions, including authentication, ownership checks, and publish result modals.
- Add rich default chat suggestion prompts and support a generic chat mode for suggested questions.
- Expose a notebook toggle in the conversation header actions and a dedicated Memory entry in the user panel menu.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure suggested questions and prompts work correctly when no explicit mode is selected by defaulting to a chat mode.

Enhancements:
- Simplify the home experience by using a static welcome message, refining starter shortcuts, and hiding secondary content modules by default.
- Streamline the model switcher UI for regular users while keeping detailed views, grouping controls, and extra model info available only in developer mode, and adjust default panel sizing.
- Reduce visual complexity in chat by simplifying input actions, hiding token and usage metrics unless in developer mode, and making the send button more prominent for standard users.
- Revise settings information architecture and labels (e.g., General vs Agent groups, Appearance, Service Model, Credits, Usage) and add redirects from deprecated tabs.
- Unify footer and navigation entry points by moving GitHub, evaluation tools, and settings into contextual menus and simplifying bottom navigation.
- Simplify title tags in agent conversations to focus on topic titles instead of model and knowledge badges.
- Adjust language selection and user panel layouts to better integrate theme and locale controls.

author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `preprocessLhCommand`, the detection/rewriting regex only handles `&&`, `||`, and `;` as separators, so cases like `lh foo | lh bar` or commands after a pipe won’t be rewritten; consider including `|` (and possibly other common separators) in the pattern if those usages should be supported.
- In `useCategory`, the API Key tab visibility is now gated only by `isDevMode` and no longer uses `showApiKeyManage`, but `showApiKeyManage` is still being derived and included in the dependency array; either fold the original flag into the new condition or remove it entirely to avoid stale/unnecessary state.

## Summary by Sourcery

Fix model switch panel popup behavior to prevent flashing, unwanted scrolling, and focus outlines, while simplifying model change handling.

Bug Fixes:
- Close model detail popup when the list scrolls to avoid stale open state.
- Prevent dropdown menu clicks from triggering default navigation and causing popup glitches.
- Delay model selection updates slightly so the panel close animation can complete without flashing the detail panel.
- Ensure submenu closes correctly when selecting a model from multiple providers and avoid async handling issues.

Enhancements:
- Disable text selection and constrain overscroll on model detail popup and list to improve interaction feel and avoid scroll chaining.
- Remove unused delayed render constants and hook from the model switch panel implementation.
- Apply consistent trigger styles without focus outline to model switch and chat action dropdown triggers for cleaner UI.
author: rdmclin2

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `setTimeout`-based deferral in `handleModelChange` uses a hardcoded 150ms delay without cleanup; consider extracting this into a named constant and clearing the timeout on unmount to avoid stale updates if the panel unmounts quickly.
- The `scrollCount` state in the list increments on every scroll purely to close detail popups; using a boolean toggle or a timestamp would avoid unbounded growth and make the intent clearer.
- Removing focus outlines (`outline: none`) and disabling text selection on interactive elements may negatively impact keyboard/accessibility users; consider applying these styles more selectively (e.g., custom focus styles) rather than globally on triggers and popups.
