title: ✨ feat: support batch topic deletion from file
body:
## Summary
- Add `--file` (`-f`) option to `lh topic delete` command for bulk deletion
- Supports two file formats: plain text (one ID per line) and JSON array
- Auto-deduplicates IDs from both file and CLI arguments

## Usage

```bash
# From plain text file (one ID per line)
lh topic delete --file ids.txt --yes

# From JSON array file
lh topic delete --file ids.json --yes

# Mix file + CLI args
lh topic delete id1 id2 --file more-ids.txt
```

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `--file` with plain text file (one ID per line)
- [ ] Verify `--file` with JSON array file
- [ ] Verify mixed usage (file + CLI args)
- [ ] Verify deduplication works
- [ ] Verify error when no IDs provided and no file given
- [ ] Verify error when JSON file is not an array

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Support deleting topics from CLI by accepting IDs via arguments or an optional file input and apply unified validation and deduplication before batch deletion.

New Features:
- Allow the topic delete command to read topic IDs from a file in either newline-delimited or JSON array format.

Enhancements:
- Make topic deletion accept optional IDs, aggregating and deduplicating IDs from both CLI arguments and file input before executing single or batch deletion, and improve validation and messaging when no IDs are provided or the JSON file shape is invalid.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- When falling back from JSON parsing to line-based parsing, any malformed JSON file will be silently treated as a plain text file; consider explicitly surfacing JSON parse errors (or using file extension/flag to determine format) so users aren’t surprised by typoed JSON being accepted but misinterpreted.
- The error messages for invalid input (`no IDs`, `JSON file must contain an array`) would be more actionable if they included the file path (when provided), e.g. `JSON file "${options.file}" must contain an array of topic IDs`.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- When falling back from JSON parsing to line-based parsing, any malformed JSON file will be silently treated as a plain text file; consider explicitly surfacing JSON parse errors (or using file extension/flag to determine format) so users aren’t surprised by typoed JSON being accepted but misinterpreted.
- The error messages for invalid input (`no IDs`, `JSON file must contain an array`) would be more actionable if they included the file path (when provided), e.g. `JSON file "${options.file}" must contain an array of topic IDs`.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="apps/cli/src/commands/topic.ts" line_range="147" />
<code_context>
+      let allIds = [...ids];
+
+      if (options.file) {
+        const content = fs.readFileSync(options.file, 'utf8').trim();
+        let fileIds: string[];
+        try {
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** Handle file read errors to avoid unhandled exceptions and noisy stack traces.

`fs.readFileSync` will throw if the path is invalid or unreadable, causing a raw stack trace in the CLI. Please wrap this call in a `try/catch`, log a clear error via `log.error`, and exit with a non‑zero status (e.g., `process.exit(1)`) for predictable behavior.
</issue_to_address>

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This centralizes tool availability checks into a shared helper so desktop-only tools, builtin skills, and web-incompatible stdio MCP plugins follow the same rules everywhere.
It updates `PluginTag`, tool selectors, builtin selectors, and tool/skill engineering to reuse that logic, which also fixes unsupported tools showing up as deprecated in web.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- `bunx vitest run --silent='passed-only' 'src/helpers/toolAvailability.test.ts' 'src/store/tool/slices/plugin/selectors.test.ts' 'src/store/tool/selectors/tool.test.ts'`

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| Unsupported desktop-only tools could appear as deprecated in web tags. | Unsupported tools are filtered out consistently across tag rendering and selectors. |

#### 📝 Additional Information

No breaking changes.

## Summary by Sourcery

Centralize tool and plugin availability checks into shared helpers and apply them consistently across selectors, engineering logic, and UI plugin tagging.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure unsupported or web-incompatible tools/plugins are consistently filtered out instead of appearing as deprecated or selectable in web contexts.

Enhancements:
- Introduce a shared tool availability helper that unifies environment-specific checks for builtin tools, skills, and installed plugins.
- Update tool and skill engineering, discovery, and plugin tag rendering to rely on the centralized availability logic for consistent behavior across environments.

Tests:
- Add unit tests for the new tool availability helper and update selector tests to cover the unified availability behavior.

- `ToolAvailabilityContext` exposes `isWindows`, but `isInstalledPluginAvailableInCurrentEnv` ignores it; either wire it into the availability logic or drop it from the context to avoid confusion about unused platform flags.

## Summary by Sourcery

Propagate activated skill context from chat messages through step context into the skills executor so execScript can automatically resolve and use all activated skills without requiring explicit config from the client.

New Features:
- Expose activated skills in the runtime step context and compute them from runSkill messages.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure skill execScript calls receive the correct skill context by deriving it from accumulated activated skills instead of relying on a manually supplied config parameter.

Enhancements:
- Add selectors, types, and wiring to track activated skills across the conversation and pass them through to skill execution.
- Simplify execScript API and manifest descriptions by preferring an activatedSkills list over the older single-skill config object and updating server and client wiring accordingly.

Documentation:
- Update builtin skill execScript manifest descriptions to reflect automatic use of the current skill context instead of requiring explicit config.

title: ✨ feat: preload bundled i18n resources and lazy-load target language
body:
## Summary
- Preload bundled en-US resources synchronously to avoid Suspense on first render
- For non-default languages, also bundle a shallow copy as immediate fallback, then reload actual translations from backend in background
- Add `react.bindI18nStore: 'added'` and `useSuspense: false` so components re-render when backend resources arrive

## Test plan
- [x] Added unit test verifying sync init + background reload behavior (`createI18n.test.ts`)
- [ ] Verify non-en-US language loads correctly in browser (fallback text briefly visible, then replaced)
- [ ] Confirm en-US users see no behavior change

## Summary by Sourcery

Preload bundled i18n resources for the default language and a shallow copy for the target language, then reload the target language from the backend in the background while ensuring React components re-render when translations arrive.

New Features:
- Support synchronous i18n initialization using bundled resources as an immediate fallback for both default and non-default languages.

Enhancements:
- Trigger background reload of full translation resources for non-default languages after initial synchronous setup.
- Configure React i18n integration to avoid Suspense and re-render components when backend language resources are added.

Tests:
- Add a unit test verifying synchronous initialization with bundled fallback resources and background reload of the actual language namespaces.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The background `reloadResources` call is fired and its promise ignored inside `initPromise.then(...)`; consider awaiting it or adding a `.catch` handler so failures don’t surface as unhandled rejections.
- `createBundledResources` is used to populate non-default languages with copies of the default (`en-US`) strings; renaming or commenting this helper to make it explicit that it returns default-language fallback data would make the behavior clearer for future maintainers.
- By setting `ns: []` in the i18next init options, you’re relying entirely on explicit reloads/background loading; it may be worth double-checking whether any existing code depends on i18next’s automatic namespace loading based on `defaultNS` during initialization and, if so, preserving that behavior for the default language case.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The background `reloadResources` call is fired and its promise ignored inside `initPromise.then(...)`; consider awaiting it or adding a `.catch` handler so failures don’t surface as unhandled rejections.
- `createBundledResources` is used to populate non-default languages with copies of the default (`en-US`) strings; renaming or commenting this helper to make it explicit that it returns default-language fallback data would make the behavior clearer for future maintainers.
- By setting `ns: []` in the i18next init options, you’re relying entirely on explicit reloads/background loading; it may be worth double-checking whether any existing code depends on i18next’s automatic namespace loading based on `defaultNS` during initialization and, if so, preserving that behavior for the default language case.
~~~

</details>

***

correct translations load asynchronously.

Enhancements: - Refresh Moonshot chat model catalog to match current Kimi offerings, including enabling the recommended turbo preview model and removing deprecated/undocumented models. - Update the default connection check model to a stable, recommended Kimi variant. - Clarify Kimi preview model display names to better reflect their preview status.
author: hezhijie0327

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The Moonshot base URLs are now hardcoded to the CN endpoints in multiple places; consider making the region-specific base URL configurable (e.g., via settings or env) so non-CN deployments can still target the .ai endpoints if needed.
- The Moonshot `proxyUrl.placeholder` and the runtime `DEFAULT_MOONSHOT_BASE_URL` / `DEFAULT_MOONSHOT_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` now duplicate the same strings; it may be safer to centralize these URLs in a shared constant to avoid future mismatches.

<!-- Breaking changes? Migration guide? Performance impact? -->

## Summary by Sourcery

Add an OpenResponses-compatible Response API endpoint backed by the existing agent runtime, including streaming support and compliance tooling.

New Features:
- Introduce a /responses OpenResponses API endpoint with controller, routing, and schema validation for creating model responses, supporting both streaming and non-streaming modes.
- Add Response API type definitions and SSE event schemas to align server responses with the OpenResponses protocol.
- Allow execAgent callers to pass additional instructions that are appended to an agent's system role for more precise control over behavior.

Enhancements:
- Wire the new Response API routes and types into the existing OpenAPI routing and export surface so they are available through the public API.

Build:
- Add a response-compliance test script and npm task to run the official OpenResponses compliance suite against the local API.

Documentation:
- Document how to run and debug the OpenResponses compliance tests in a new response-compliance skill guide.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- The new ResponsesService has grown quite large and mixes request translation, ID/topic handling, execution, and streaming orchestration; consider splitting it into smaller helpers (e.g., input translation, output mapping, streaming adapter) to make the logic easier to follow and maintain.
- generateResponseId currently relies on Math.random for identifiers; it would be more robust to derive response IDs from existing stable IDs (e.g., operationId/topicId) or use a cryptographically strong random UUID to avoid potential collisions.
- There is some duplicated setup logic between createResponse and createStreamingResponse (prompt extraction, instructions building, previous topic resolution, execAgent invocation); extracting a shared helper for that common path would reduce the chance of the two modes diverging subtly over time.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The new ResponsesService has grown quite large and mixes request translation, ID/topic handling, execution, and streaming orchestration; consider splitting it into smaller helpers (e.g., input translation, output mapping, streaming adapter) to make the logic easier to follow and maintain.
- generateResponseId currently relies on Math.random for identifiers; it would be more robust to derive response IDs from existing stable IDs (e.g., operationId/topicId) or use a cryptographically strong random UUID to avoid potential collisions.
- There is some duplicated setup logic between createResponse and createStreamingResponse (prompt extraction, instructions building, previous topic resolution, execAgent invocation); extracting a shared helper for that common path would reduce the chance of the two modes diverging subtly over time.

commits.message: add response api framework
finish response api structure
finish response api structure

✨ feat: implement basic text generation for Response API (LOBE-5858)

- Add instructions extraction from system/developer input messages
- Add instructions param to ExecAgentParams, append to agent systemRole
- Implement extractPrompt, extractAssistantContent, extractUsage in ResponsesService
- Wire up execAgent + executeSync flow for non-streaming and streaming
- Add logprobs field to output_text content parts for schema compliance
- Fix truncation field to output string enum instead of object

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: implement real token-level streaming for Response API (LOBE-5859)

- Replace fake streaming (executeSync → emit events) with real streaming
- Subscribe to InMemoryStreamEventManager for live stream_chunk events
- Run executeSync in background, convert text chunks to output_text.delta SSE events
- Add missing schema fields: item_id on content_part/text events, logprobs on delta/done events
- Fix content_part.added/done to include item_id per OpenResponses spec

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: implement tool calling output extraction for Response API (LOBE-5860)

- Add extractOutputItems to convert AgentState messages to OpenResponses output items
- Extract assistant tool_calls → function_call output items
- Extract tool result messages → function_call_output output items
- Skip message items for assistant messages that have tool_calls (avoid duplicates)
- Add status field to function_call_output items per OpenResponses spec
- Update FunctionCallOutputItemSchema with optional status field
- Output array reflects execution order: function_call → function_call_output → message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

✨ feat: implement multi-turn conversations via previous_response_id (LOBE-5861)

Encode topicId in response.id to enable stateless multi-turn conversation
chaining. When previous_response_id is provided, extract topicId and pass
to execAgent via appContext, which automatically loads history messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: add missing type fields for OpenResponses compliance (logprobs, item_id, input_tokens_details)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: 🐛 fix(i18n): preload default language from JSON to avoid Suspense on first render
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- None -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Preload default language (en-US) resources synchronously during i18n init to prevent `useTranslation` from suspending on first render.

**Problem**: `useTranslation` with `useSuspense: true` (default) throws a promise when translations are not ready. With `resourcesToBackend` loading async, the first `useTranslation('common')` call in Nav/Header triggered Suspense, showing a 44px skeleton fallback and causing CLS (layout shift).

**Solution**: Pass preloaded default language resources (common, error, chat) from `locales/en-US/*.json` to `init()`. Uses JSON files (not `locales/default/*.ts`) as runtime values since the TS source is for type inference only.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

- Verify app loads without layout jump on sidebar header
- Verify default language (en-US) shows correctly on first paint
- Switch to other language (e.g. zh-CN) and verify it loads async as before

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure the default language (en-US) translation namespaces are loaded synchronously to avoid Suspense during the first render.

title: 🐛 fix: filter out delisted lobehub provider models from DB residuals
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When models are removed from the lobehub provider's builtin model-bank list, residual records in the `ai_models` DB table (from user toggle preferences) are still returned by the backend API. This causes delisted models to appear in the model list.

**Changes:**
- **`getAiProviderModelList`**: After merging builtin + DB models, filter out DB residual models not in the builtin list for the branding provider
- **`getEnabledModels`**: Exclude branding provider models from `appendedUserModels` since they are already handled via the builtin model path
- **Remove retired Anthropic models**: `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` and `claude-3-5-haiku-20241022` (both retired Feb 19, 2026)
- **Add `@lobechat/business-const` dependency** to database package for `BRANDING_PROVIDER` constant

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] No tests needed

title: ♻️ refactor(desktop): move onboarding state to main process
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [x] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to #12817

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Move desktop onboarding completion state from renderer localStorage to Electron main process.

**Key changes:**
- `BrowserManager.initializeBrowsers()` now checks `isRemoteServerConfigured()` to determine initial path (`/` or `/desktop-onboarding`)
- Removed `DESKTOP_ONBOARDING_STORAGE_KEY` and related localStorage functions
- Onboarding completion is now determined by `dataSyncConfig.active` in electron-store (main process)
- Simplified renderer-side logic - no more localStorage checks for onboarding redirect

**Benefits:**
- State managed in single source of truth (electron-store)
- No localStorage sync issues between renderer and main
- Cleaner separation of concerns

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Fresh install desktop app → should show onboarding
2. Complete onboarding → should navigate to main app
3. Restart app → should go directly to main app (not onboarding)
4. Sign out from user panel → should return to onboarding login step

#### 📝 Additional Information

The `DESKTOP_ONBOARDING_SCREEN_KEY` is preserved for resuming onboarding progress after app restart.

title: ♻️ refactor(cli): extract shared `@lobechat/local-file-shell` package
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5871

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Extract common file and shell operations from Desktop and CLI into a new shared package `@lobechat/local-file-shell` to eliminate duplicated code.

**New package `packages/local-file-shell/`:**
- `shell/` — `runCommand`, `ShellProcessManager` (background process management), `stripAnsi`, `truncateOutput`
- `file/` — `readLocalFile` (now using `@lobechat/file-loaders` for PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX support), `writeLocalFile`, `editLocalFile` (with diff generation), `listLocalFiles`, `globLocalFiles`, `searchLocalFiles`, `grepContent`
- `types.ts` — All shared type definitions

**CLI migration:**
- `tools/file.ts` reduced from ~360 lines to pure re-exports
- `tools/shell.ts` reduced from ~234 lines to ~25 line thin wrapper
- CLI now supports rich file format reading via `@lobechat/file-loaders`

Desktop-side migration can follow in a separate PR (Controllers become thin wrappers calling the shared package).

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests

```bash
cd apps/cli
bunx vitest run --config vitest.config.mts --silent='passed-only' 'src/tools/'
# 70 tests pass

npx tsup && node dist/index.js --version
# Build succeeds, CLI runs
```

#### 📝 Additional Information

- `fast-glob`, `diff`, `debug` kept as external in tsup config to avoid CJS/ESM bundling issues
- `@napi-rs/canvas` (file-loaders dep for PDF) also externalized
- File loaders are code-split into lazy chunks (PDF ~3MB, DOCX ~1.1MB, etc.) — only loaded when needed

title: 🐛 fix: resolve ModelSelect crash and update default model
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] 🔨 chore
- [x] ✅ test

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

- **Fix ModelSelect crash**: Rendering `ModelInfoTags` (with `Tag`/`Tooltip` components) inside `@base-ui/react` Select popup triggers a `forkRef` ref cleanup infinite loop (`Maximum update depth exceeded`). Fixed by setting `showInfoTag={false}` in `optionRender`.
- **Update default model**: Change `DEFAULT_MODEL` from `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` to `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Remove `enabled` flag from legacy models (`claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`, `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) in lobehub provider.
- **Add guard test**: Verify that `DEFAULT_MODEL` and `DEFAULT_MINI_MODEL` are always present and enabled in `LOBE_DEFAULT_MODEL_LIST`, preventing future misconfigurations.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Delete user account from database, re-login through onboarding
2. On the last onboarding step, click the model dropdown — should no longer crash
3. Run `bunx vitest run src/services/aiModel/index.test.ts` — all tests pass

## Summary by Sourcery

Update default AI model configuration and prevent model selection crashes in the UI.

Bug Fixes:
- Prevent ModelSelect dropdown from crashing by disabling info tag rendering within options.

Enhancements:
- Change the global DEFAULT_MODEL constant to use the new claude-sonnet-4-6 model and mark older Anthropic models as non-default by removing their enabled flags.

Tests:
- Add guard tests ensuring DEFAULT_MODEL and DEFAULT_MINI_MODEL are present and enabled in the default model list.

title: 🐛 fix: harden market auth popup handoff and storage fallback
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

  - [ ] ✨ feat
  - [x] 🐛 fix
  - [ ] ♻️ refactor
  - [ ] 💄 style
  - [ ] 👷 build
  - [ ] ⚡️ perf
  - [ ] ✅ test
  - [ ] 📝 docs
  - [ ] 🔨 chore

  #### 🔗 Related Issue

 #### 🔀 Description of Change

  This PR hardens the Community Market authorization popup flow for browser edge cases.

  Previously, the parent page relied too heavily on direct popup state and could incorrectly treat the authorization window as closed before completion. In practice, users could see the popup success countdown while the parent page had already failed the flow. There were also storage-related failure paths that could break the handoff unexpectedly in privacy-restricted browser environments.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b87d6977-747a-4d02-a65d-5f273c106286

  This change improves the Market auth flow by:

  - adding a COOP-safe same-origin storage handoff fallback alongside the existing postMessage path
  - rejecting promptly when the popup is actually closed by the user without any handoff result
  - making handoff storage access best-effort, so storage read/write/clear failures do not break the existing message-based auth path
  - keeping the callback page compatible with both storage fallback and existing opener-based communication

  #### 🧪 How to Test

  - [x] Tested locally
  - [x] Added/updated tests
  - [ ] No tests needed

  Tested with:

  - pnpm exec vitest run --silent='passed-only' 'src/layout/AuthProvider/MarketAuth/oidc.test.ts' 'src/layout/AuthProvider/MarketAuth/handoff.test.ts'
  - targeted eslint on the changed Market auth files
  - targeted stylelint on the changed Market auth files

  Manual verification scenarios:

  1. Open Community and click Become a Creator
  2. Complete Market authorization in the popup
  3. Confirm the popup shows the success countdown
  4. Confirm the parent page no longer reports that the authorization window was closed before completion
  5. Re-run the flow and manually close the popup before reaching the callback page
  6. Confirm the parent page fails quickly instead of staying stuck in loading for minutes

  #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos


  #### 📝 Additional Information
This PR is scoped to the Community Market auth popup flow and its handoff mechanism. It does not change Market trusted-client token selection or unrelated market event reporting behavior.

author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In `UpdaterCtr.getBuildChannel`, consider switching from a dynamic `import('@/modules/updater/configs')` to a top‑level static import to avoid repeated module loads and potential bundler/runtime quirks around IPC handlers.
- The `BUILD_CHANNEL` and `buildChannel` values are currently typed as generic strings; it would be safer to define and reuse a union type (e.g. `'stable' | 'nightly' | 'canary' | 'beta'`) across main and renderer to avoid typo-prone string usage and keep translation keys in sync.
- In `Version.tsx`, the two `useEffect` hooks that call `autoUpdateService.getUpdaterState()` and `autoUpdateService.getBuildChannel()` could be combined into a single desktop-only effect to reduce duplicated `isDesktop` checks and keep related IPC calls co-located.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `UpdaterCtr.getBuildChannel`, consider switching from a dynamic `import('@/modules/updater/configs')` to a top‑level static import to avoid repeated module loads and potential bundler/runtime quirks around IPC handlers.
- The `BUILD_CHANNEL` and `buildChannel` values are currently typed as generic strings; it would be safer to define and reuse a union type (e.g. `'stable' | 'nightly' | 'canary' | 'beta'`) across main and renderer to avoid typo-prone string usage and keep translation keys in sync.
- In `Version.tsx`, the two `useEffect` hooks that call `autoUpdateService.getUpdaterState()` and `autoUpdateService.getBuildChannel()` could be combined into a single desktop-only effect to reduce duplicated `isDesktop` checks and keep related IPC calls co-located.

## 🔗 Related Issue

Related to agent enhancement and browser automation capabilities, fixes LOBE-5625

Re-opens #12696 (closed due to branch rename to lowercase)

## 🔀 Description of Change

This PR introduces the **Agent Browser** skill, enabling AI agents to automate web browsers and Electron desktop applications using the Vercel agent-browser CLI.

### Key Features:
- **New builtin skill**: `lobe-agent-browser` - Lightweight skill injected into system prompt
- **Command support**: Full snapshot-ref pattern workflow for browser interaction
  - Navigation (open, back, forward, reload, close)
  - Element interaction (click, fill, type, select, check, press, hover)
  - Information retrieval (get text, URL, title)
  - Wait conditions (element, text, URL, network idle)
  - Tab management (new, list, switch, close)
  - Screenshots and PDF export
  - JavaScript execution
  - Visual regression testing
- **Tool detection**: Desktop app now detects if agent-browser CLI is installed
- **Ecosystem integration**: ToolDetectorManager extended with browser-automation category

### Technical Details:
- Skill system (not tool schema) - lightweight XML prompt injection
- Executor uses `execScript()` to shell out to agent-browser CLI with --json flag
- ToolDetector for CLI availability detection on desktop
- Updated locale strings for browser automation detection

### Files Changed:
- Created: `packages/builtin-skills/src/agent-browser/` (skill definition and guides)
- Modified: Tool detector infrastructure and UI components
- Updated: Locale strings for browser automation

## 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

### Test Scenarios:
1. **Skill availability**: Verify Agent Browser skill appears in agent configuration
2. **Snapshot-ref workflow**: Agent can navigate, snapshot, and interact with elements
3. **Tool detection**: Desktop app detects agent-browser CLI installation status
4. **UI updates**: Tool detection section in settings reflects agent-browser availability

## 📝 Additional Information

This implementation follows the builtin-skills pattern established by other skills in the codebase. The skill provides comprehensive browser automation capabilities as an alternative to tool-based implementation for lighter-weight integration.

The agent-browser CLI reference: https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

- Added 'bin' to .gitignore to exclude binary files from version control.

- Introduced a new script to download the `agent-browser` binary, ensuring it is available for the application.

fix: mock binary directory path in tests

title: 👷 build: enable `pg_search` extension
body:
This release includes a **database schema migration** to enable the `pg_search` extension.

### Migration: Enable pg_search Extension

- Added migration `0090_enable_pg_search.sql` to enable `pg_search` extension

### Notes for Self-hosted Users

- The migration runs automatically on application startup
- No manual intervention required

The migration owner: @tjx666 — responsible for this database schema change, reach out for any migration-related issues.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In the custom test migration runner you still insert a row into `drizzle.__drizzle_migrations` even when a migration is skipped for `pg_search`/`bm25`; consider only recording hashes for migrations whose SQL was actually executed to avoid masking future schema assumptions in tests.
- The skip logic for PGlite checks for `pg_search`/`bm25` substrings anywhere in the SQL; you may want to tighten this (e.g., match specific `CREATE EXTENSION` or index statements) to avoid accidentally skipping unrelated migrations that mention those terms in comments or identifiers.

<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

- The skip logic for PGlite checks for `pg_search`/`bm25` substrings anywhere in the SQL; you may want to tighten this (e.g., match specific `CREATE EXTENSION` or index statements) to avoid accidentally skipping unrelated migrations that mention those terms in comments or identifiers.

body:
## Summary

- Added unit tests for `src/server/services/gateway`
- Total test files added: 2
- Test cases added: 37
- Coverage focus: `BotConnectQueue` Redis queue operations and `GatewayManager` lifecycle + bot management

## Changes

- [x] All tests pass successfully
- [x] Business logic coverage improved
- [x] Edge cases and error handling covered
- [x] Tests follow existing patterns

## Module Processed

`src/server/services/gateway`

## Test Coverage

- Functions tested:
  - `BotConnectQueue.push` — enqueueing with timestamp, Redis unavailable error
  - `BotConnectQueue.popAll` — parsing, expiry filtering, batch cleanup, malformed JSON, field format edge cases
  - `BotConnectQueue.remove` — field removal, Redis unavailable no-op
  - `GatewayManager.start` — lifecycle, idempotency, sync failure resilience
  - `GatewayManager.stop` — lifecycle, no-op when not running, stops all bots
  - `GatewayManager.startBot` — provider not found, unsupported platform, successful start, restart (stops existing)
  - `GatewayManager.stopBot` — bot not found, successful stop, isolation between bots
  - `createGatewayManager` / `getGatewayManager` — singleton pattern
- Coverage type: unit
- Test approach: mocked Redis, database, and KeyVaults dependencies; isolated state per test

Add unit test coverage for the gateway service, focusing on gateway manager lifecycle and bot connect queue behavior.

title: Simplify docker compose network architecture & Remove broken links from docker compose docs
body: #### 💻 Change Type - [ ] ✨ feat - [ ] 🐛 fix - [x] ♻️ refactor - [ ] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [x] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔗 Related Issue Fixes #12748 #### 🔀 Description of Change ##### Configuration Changes - ✅ Remove unnecessary `network-service` (alpine) container - All services now use `lobe-network` bridge network directly - Reduced container count and resource usage - ✅ Add `RUSTFS_ADMIN_PORT` environment variable - Allows optional exposure of RustFS admin console (port 9001) - Added clear comments for port exposure requirements - ✅ Update container-to-container communication - Use Docker service names for internal communication - `rustfs-init` uses `http://rustfs:9000` instead of removed network-service - ✅ Use relative path volumes for better data persistence - `./postgres_data`, `./redis_data`, `./rustfs_data` - Easier backup and management ##### Documentation Changes - ✅ Remove broken link to non-existent `server-database` documentation - Link was returning 404 - ✅ Add single-domain deployment documentation - Guide for users with only one domain - Port-based service differentiation - ✅ Update `INTERNAL_APP_URL` guidance - Changed from outdated `network_mode: 'service:network-service'` reference - Now recommends `http://lobe:3210` for container communication - ✅ Clarify Port Mode vs Domain Mode behavior - Port Mode: Script automatically uses default ports (3210/9000/9001) - Domain Mode: Customizable ports via reverse proxy (domains entered during setup) - ✅ Add S3_ENDPOINT configuration tips - Internal communication: `http://rustfs:9000` (avatar uploads only) - External access: Public URL required for conversation image uploads #### 🧪 How to Test I deployed the project locally with the updated `docker-compose.yml` and `.env` files. All 6 services started successfully, and `rustfs-init` completed and exited as expected. - [x] Tested locally - [ ] Added/updated tests - [ ] No tests needed #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos #### 📝 Additional Information ##### ⚠️ Deployment Script Impact This PR modifies `docker-compose.yml` and `.env.example`. I haven't verified if `setup.sh` generates these files dynamically. ## Summary by Sourcery Simplify the Docker Compose deployment by removing the dedicated network service container, standardizing container networking, and updating self-hosting documentation to reflect the new configuration and clarify single-domain and S3 endpoint usage. Bug Fixes: - Remove a broken documentation link to non-existent server database deployment docs. Enhancements: - Have application and storage services connect directly via the shared bridge network using Docker service names instead of an intermediary network container. - Add configurable exposure of the RustFS admin console via a new RUSTFS_ADMIN_PORT environment variable with guidance on when external ports are needed. - Switch database, Redis, and RustFS storage to use local relative-path host volumes for easier data persistence and management. Documentation: - Document single-domain deployment patterns and the differences between Port Mode and Domain Mode for the Docker-based installer. - Clarify recommended INTERNAL_APP_URL and S3_ENDPOINT values for container-to-container and external access scenarios in Docker Compose deployments.
author: MarcellGu

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- The new `rustfs` port mapping uses `${RUSTFS_ADMIN_PORT}:9001`; consider ensuring this env var has a sensible default (or guarding against it being empty) so docker-compose doesn't end up with an invalid `:9001` mapping when users don't explicitly set it.
- The English Docker Compose docs suggest `S3_ENDPOINT=http://rustfs:9000` when external S3 access isn’t needed, but only the zh-CN version warns that this breaks conversation image uploads; it would be helpful to align the two and explicitly state this limitation in the English callout as well.

- Redis: Keep redis_data named volume (not ./redis_data)
- RustFS: Keep rustfs-data named volume (not ./rustfs_data)

This ensures existing users can upgrade without data migration.

fix(docker): correct Port Mode vs Domain Mode description

- Fix reversed explanation in comments
- Port Mode: Uses default ports (3210/9000/9001)
- Domain Mode: Custom ports via reverse proxy

This aligns with the actual deployment script behavior.

title: ✨ feat(eval): add external scoring mode
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

<!-- For change type, change [ ] to [x]. -->

- [x] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR -->

<!-- Example: Fixes #xxx, Closes #xxx, Related to #xxx -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

<!-- Thank you for your Pull Request. Please provide a description above. -->

✨ **New Eval Mode: `answer-relevance` (LLM-Eq Judge)**

- ✨ `packages/eval-rubric/src/matchers/llmEq.ts`: add `matchLLMEq` — a new LLM-as-judge matcher for `answer-relevance` mode. Takes `question`, `actual`, and `expected` as inputs; calls a configurable judge model with a structured JSON schema output (`correct: boolean`, `confidence: 0.0–1.0`, `reason: string`); falls back gracefully when no judge model is configured
- ✨ `packages/eval-rubric/src/matchers/index.ts`: extend `match()` signature to accept `input` field; wire `answer-relevance` rubric type to `matchLLMEq`
- ✨ `packages/eval-rubric/src/evaluate.ts`: pass `testCase.input` into `match()` calls to support input-aware matchers like `matchLLMEq`
- ✨ `packages/types/src/eval/rubric.ts`: add `'answer-relevance'` to `RubricType` union; add `RubricConfigLLM` type
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/features/DatasetEditModal/index.tsx`: add `answer-relevance` to eval mode selector; expand LLM judge config form (provider, model, system prompt, eval prompt) to apply to both `llm-rubric` and `answer-relevance` modes
- 🌐 `locales/en-US/eval.json` / `src/locales/default/eval.ts`: add `evalMode.answer-relevance` and `evalMode.answer-relevance.desc` keys; update `evalMode.llm-rubric.desc` to note scoring range `(0.0 to 1.0)`

***

✨ **New Eval Mode: `external`**

- ✨ `packages/eval-rubric/src/matchers/external.ts`: add `matchExternal` — returns a placeholder `{ passed: false, score: 0, reason: 'Waiting for external evaluation...' }` result immediately, signalling that scoring is deferred to an external system
- ✨ `packages/eval-rubric/src/matchers/index.ts`: wire `external` rubric type to `matchExternal`
- ✨ `packages/database/src/schemas/agentEvals.ts`: add `'external'` to `evalModes`, `agentEvalRuns.status` enum, and `agentEvalRunTopics.status` enum
- ✨ `packages/types/src/eval/agentEval.ts`: add `externalCases` to `EvalRunMetrics`; add `awaitingExternalEval?: boolean` flag to `EvalRunTopicResult`; expand `EvalThreadResult.status` union with `'external'` and `'completed'`
- ✨ `packages/types/src/eval/agentEvalRun.ts`: add `'external'` to `AgentEvalRunStatus` union type
- ✨ `src/server/services/agentEvalRun/index.ts`: short-circuit topic scoring when `evalMode === 'external'` — mark topic `status: 'external'` with `awaitingExternalEval: true` immediately after agent completion; propagate `external` status to run level when all threads await external scoring; exclude `external` cases from score accumulation; track `externalCases` in both real-time and finalized metrics
- ✨ `src/app/(backend)/api/workflows/agent-eval-run/finalize-run/route.ts`: update finalization logic — set run `status: 'external'` when `externalCases > 0`, taking priority over `failed` / `completed`
- ✨ `src/server/routers/lambda/agentEval.ts`: add `'external'` to `rubricTypeSchema` Zod enum
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/features/StatusBadge.tsx`: add `external` status entry with `Hourglass` icon and `warning` color class
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/bench/[benchmarkId]/runs/[runId]/features/CaseResultsTable/index.tsx`: add `external` (purple badge + tooltip) and `completed` (blue badge + tooltip) status renderers in `StatusBadge`; color thread dots warning/primary for `external`/`completed` threads respectively; add both statuses to the status filter dropdown
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/bench/[benchmarkId]/runs/[runId]/features/PendingState/index.tsx`: make hint text configurable via a `hint` prop instead of a hardcoded i18n key
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/bench/[benchmarkId]/runs/[runId]/index.tsx`: render `<PendingState hint={t('run.external.hint')} />` when run status is `'external'`
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/features/DatasetCreateModal/index.tsx`: add `external` option to the eval mode selector
- ✨ `src/routes/(main)/eval/features/DatasetEditModal/index.tsx`: add `external` option to the eval mode selector
- 🌐 `locales/en-US/eval.json` / `locales/zh-CN/eval.json` / `src/locales/default/eval.ts`: add keys for `run.status.external` (+ tooltip), `run.status.completed` (+ tooltip), `run.external.hint`, `evalMode.external`, and `evalMode.external.desc`

#### 🧪 How to Test

<!-- Please describe how you tested your changes -->

<!-- For AI features, please include test prompts or scenarios -->

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

#### 📸 Screenshots / Videos

<!-- If this PR includes UI changes, please provide screenshots or videos -->

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
| ...    | ...   |

#### 📝 Additional Information

<!-- Add any other context about the Pull Request here. -->

<!-- Breaking changes? Migration guide? Performance impact? -->

## Summary by Sourcery

Introduce LLM-based answer relevance judging and an external evaluation mode, including API, CLI, data model, and UI support for deferred scoring and richer benchmarks.

New Features:
- Add LLM-based `answer-relevance` rubric type that judges correctness using a configurable external model with access to the original question.
- Add `external` rubric type and workflow that lets agent runs complete while deferring scoring to an external system, including status propagation and exclusion from internal scoring.
- Expose an external eval TRPC router and CLI commands to inspect runs, datasets, topics, threads, messages, and to report or batch-report external scores.
- Extend dataset presets and import mapping to support several new research benchmarks and safer field inference.
- Enhance dataset and test case UIs to select new eval modes, configure LLM judge parameters, and display inherited eval modes per test case.
- Add new run and topic status handling in the UI (including badges, filters, and pending hints) and support copying run IDs from the header.

Enhancements:
- Track `externalCases` in real-time and finalized evaluation metrics while excluding them from scoring aggregates.
- Harden LLM rubric matching by handling missing scores from the judge model.

title: 🐛 fix: redirect to desktop onboarding when not completed
body:
## Summary
- Desktop app was missing the redirect to `/desktop-onboarding` when onboarding hadn't been completed
- `useDesktopUserStateRedirect` silently returned instead of calling `redirectIfNotOn`
- This caused two issues:
  1. Users never see the onboarding flow on fresh install
  2. `AuthRequiredModal` is suppressed because the onboarding completion guard fails

## Root Cause
The redirect was **never implemented** since the file was first created in `34a6312668`. The comment "Desktop onboarding is handled by desktop-only flow" referenced a flow that was never built.

## Test plan
- [x] Fresh desktop install: verify redirect to `/desktop-onboarding`
- [x] After completing onboarding: verify no redirect, normal app usage
- [x] 401 with `X-Auth-Required: true`: verify `AuthRequiredModal` shows after onboarding is completed

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Restore the missing redirect to the desktop onboarding route when onboarding has not been completed, preventing users from skipping the flow and ensuring auth gating works as intended.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `useDesktopUserStateRedirect`, the returned callback no longer uses `openExternalAndLogout`, so you can drop it from the dependency array (and potentially from the hook body) to avoid an unnecessary dependency.
- The redirect logic now depends directly on `window.location.pathname`; if this hook ever runs in non-browser contexts or during server-side rendering, consider sourcing the current path from your router or a passed-in location instead of `window` to avoid runtime issues and improve testability.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `useDesktopUserStateRedirect`, the returned callback no longer uses `openExternalAndLogout`, so you can drop it from the dependency array (and potentially from the hook body) to avoid an unnecessary dependency.
- The redirect logic now depends directly on `window.location.pathname`; if this hook ever runs in non-browser contexts or during server-side rendering, consider sourcing the current path from your router or a passed-in location instead of `window` to avoid runtime issues and improve testability.
~~~

</details>

***

🐛 fix: redirect to desktop onboarding when not completed

Desktop app was missing the redirect to `/desktop-onboarding` when
onboarding hadn't been completed. The `useDesktopUserStateRedirect`
callback silently returned instead of navigating, causing:
- Users never see the onboarding flow on fresh install
- `AuthRequiredModal` suppressed because onboarding guard fails

🐛 fix: remove desktop onboarding routes from proxy configuration

The `/desktop-onboarding` and its regex route have been removed from the proxy configuration. This change simplifies the routing logic as the onboarding flow is now handled directly in the user state redirect logic.

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>

title: ✨ feat: partial openapi
body:
### 💻 变更类型 | Change Type

<!-- For change type, change [ ] to [x]. -->

- [x] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

### 🔀 变更说明 | Description of Change

新增 OpenAPI v1 版本接口

#### 架构设计
在 LobeHub 中继承轻量化框架 [Hono](https://hono.dev/docs/getting-started/vercel)
通过在 `/api/v1` 下创建拦截式路由实现请求的接收，目录结构如下

```
|── v1
│   |── [[...route]]  // 拦截路由，将请求拦截后转发到 _hono 的总入口
│   |── _hono // hono 应用
│       |── app.ts // 总入口
│       |── common  //  公共库，存放一些基类
│       |── constant  // 常量
│       |── controllers  // controller
│       |── helpers  // 工具函数
│       |── middleware  // 中间件，登录态、rbac 权限管理等
│       |── routes  // 路由注册
│       |── services  // service
│       |── types  // 类型定义
```

**如何新增一个模块**

1. 在 `routes` 目录下新增一个路由文件，在 `routes/index.ts` 中注册该路由文件
2. 在  `controllers`  下新增一个控制器文件，继承 `common/base.controller.ts`，提供了基础的数据库实例注入、请求参数转换、操作请求响应等能力
3. 在 `services` 下新增相应的服务文件，继承 `common/base.service.ts`，提供了统一的日志、rbac 细粒度权限管理、各种类型的抛错等能力

**如何调用**
 
可以通过 Postman/Apifox 等工具通过调用 `/api/v1/ ` 相关接口实现调用

#### 开发自测
为了方便在本地测试接口，提供了新的环境变量： `MOCK_DEV_USER_ID`

1. 在 `.env` 文件中配置 `MOCK_DEV_USER_ID` 为特定的用户
2. 请求时携带特定的请求头 `lobe-auth-dev-backend-api: 1` 

`middleware/auth` 中间件会自动放行，并将 `userId` 放到相应的上下文中

#### 鉴权逻辑

目前仅支持通过 OIDC JWT Token 的鉴权方式，用户可以将第三方客户端接入 LobeChat 提供的标准化 OIDC Provider
通过获取到授权方的 IdToken 信息，在调用接口时，通过 `Authorization: Bearer {id_token}` 的方式传递
中间件会对请求合法性进行校验 通过后会将对应的用户信息注入到请求上下文中

📝 补充信息 | Additional Information

在接口中因为涉及到一些联表查询，因此在 `src/database/schemas/relations.ts` 中添加了所需的关联关系维护

## Summary by Sourcery

Add OpenAPI v1 endpoints using Hono to expose a fully featured backend API with authentication and RBAC, extend database schema, and enhance file, session, agent, user, message, topic, model, chat, and search services

New Features:
- Introduce Hono-based /api/v1 routes for versioned HTTP API
- Implement file management endpoints (upload, batch upload, public upload, parse, pre­signed URLs, listing, deletion)
- Add full CRUD support for sessions, agents, users, topics, messages, translations, models, and chat operations
- Enable OIDC JWT authentication with mock dev mode and RBAC enforcement
- Expose model and provider configuration, search, and AI chat translation services

Enhancements:
- Extend database schemas with new relations for users, messages (translation/tts/files), agents, sessions, AI providers and models
- Add skipAgentCreation flag in SessionModel to control agent creation
- Add S3.getPublicUrl method for generating public file URLs
- Refine placeholder parser formatting and ensure local temp directories exist

Build:
- Add dependencies: hono, @hono/zod-validator, Google AI SDKs

Documentation:
- Provide OpenAPI.md documentation for v1 API architecture and usage

This release includes a **database schema migration** adding a `key_hash` column to the `api_keys` table for authentication lookup.

### Migration 0089: Add API Key Hash Column

- Added `key_hash` column (`varchar(128)`, unique) to `api_keys` table
- Enables hash-based API key authentication lookup

### Notes for Self-hosted Users

- The migration runs automatically on application startup
- No manual intervention required
- The migration is backwards-compatible — existing API keys will continue to work

The migration owner: @arvinxx — responsible for this database schema change, reach out for any migration-related issues.

Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The new `messagesRelations.translation` relation looks reversed — using `fields: [messages.id]` and `references: [messageTranslates.id]` implies `messages.id` depends on `messageTranslates.id`; double-check that this matches the actual foreign key (it’s likely the FK lives on `messageTranslates` pointing back to `messages`).
- For the new `apiKeys.key`/`keyHash` fields, consider aligning the schema comments with the actual storage semantics (e.g., whether `key` is truly encrypted vs. stored raw, and whether `keyHash` is always present) to avoid confusion when reasoning about how API key lookup works.

title: 🐛 fix: add ffmpeg-static to default serverExternalPackages
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Follow-up to #12838 and #12844

#### 🔀 Description of Change

On Vercel with pnpm, `ffmpeg-static` must be in `serverExternalPackages` so turbopack preserves the `require('ffmpeg-static')` call. This allows NFT to trace the module through pnpm's resolution chain and automatically include the binary in the serverless function.

Changes:
1. **`define-config.ts`**: Add `ffmpeg-static` to the default `serverExternalPackages` list
2. **`next.config.ts`**: Remove `outputFileTracingIncludes` for ffmpeg (no longer needed)

This restores the approach from the original video generation PR that was inadvertently broken by a later optimization attempt.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

Deploy to Vercel and verify `/api/webhooks/video/[provider]` processes videos without ENOENT.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `getFfmpegPath()`, consider explicitly validating the fallback hoisted path with `existsSync` and throwing a clear error if neither the resolved nor hoisted paths exist, so failures don’t surface later as a generic ENOENT from `spawn`.
- Relying on `process.cwd()` for constructing the hoisted ffmpeg path may be brittle in some deployment setups; you might want to base the path on `__dirname` or `require.resolve('ffmpeg-static')`’s directory to ensure it’s stable regardless of how the process is started.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `getFfmpegPath()`, consider explicitly validating the fallback hoisted path with `existsSync` and throwing a clear error if neither the resolved nor hoisted paths exist, so failures don’t surface later as a generic ENOENT from `spawn`.
- Relying on `process.cwd()` for constructing the hoisted ffmpeg path may be brittle in some deployment setups; you might want to base the path on `__dirname` or `require.resolve('ffmpeg-static')`’s directory to ensure it’s stable regardless of how the process is started.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="src/server/services/generation/video.ts" line_range="28-34" />
<code_context>
   if (_ffmpegPath) return _ffmpegPath;
-  _ffmpegPath = require('ffmpeg-static') as string;
+
+  const resolved = require('ffmpeg-static') as string;
+  if (existsSync(resolved)) {
+    _ffmpegPath = resolved;
+  } else {
+    // On Vercel with pnpm, require('ffmpeg-static') returns the virtual store path
+    // but the binary is placed at the hoisted path by outputFileTracingIncludes
+    _ffmpegPath = path.join(process.cwd(), 'node_modules', 'ffmpeg-static', 'ffmpeg');
+  }
+
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** Handle the case where neither the resolved nor fallback ffmpeg paths actually exist with a clearer

commits.message:
🐛 fix: resolve ffmpeg-static binary path on Vercel with pnpm

On Vercel with pnpm, require('ffmpeg-static') returns the virtual store
path but the binary is placed at the hoisted path by tracing includes.
Add existsSync fallback to use the hoisted path when the resolved path
doesn't exist. Also simplify outputFileTracingIncludes to match the
vercel-labs/ffmpeg-on-vercel reference implementation.

ref: https://github.com/vercel-labs/ffmpeg-on-vercel

title: 🐛 fix: remove ffmpeg-static from outputFileTracingExcludes
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Follow-up to #12838

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The previous fix (#12838) added ffmpeg pnpm store path to `outputFileTracingIncludes`, but this didn't work because **Next.js applies `outputFileTracingExcludes` AFTER `outputFileTracingIncludes`** — excludes always win.

The fix: remove ffmpeg-static patterns from `outputFileTracingExcludes` entirely. The ffmpeg binary will be naturally traced by NFT (via `path.join(__dirname, ...)` detection in `ffmpeg-static/index.js`) only into routes that import the video service. The `outputFileTracingIncludes` remains as a safety net.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

Deploy to Vercel and verify `/api/webhooks/video/[provider]` can spawn ffmpeg without ENOENT.

## Summary by Sourcery

Ensure ffmpeg-static binaries are included in Next.js output file tracing for serverless functions on Vercel by adjusting tracing exclusions.

Bug Fixes:
- Allow ffmpeg-static to be traced and bundled into relevant serverless routes by removing its patterns from outputFileTracingExcludes.

Enhancements:
- Update Next.js config comments to reflect the new output file tracing behavior and adjusted bundle size savings.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider adding a brief inline comment near the removal of the ffmpeg-static patterns (or near `outputFileTracingExcludes`) explaining that Next.js applies excludes after includes and that ffmpeg-static must not be excluded for NFT to pick it up, so future changes don’t accidentally reintroduce the exclusion.
- If the ~45MB size estimate is important for capacity planning, it may be worth noting somewhere that ffmpeg-static is now bundled only into routes that import it, so readers don’t misinterpret the per-function savings comment as including or excluding the ffmpeg binary.

🐛 fix: remove ffmpeg-static from outputFileTracingExcludes

outputFileTracingExcludes runs AFTER outputFileTracingIncludes in
Next.js, so excluded files are always removed regardless of includes.
The previous approach of exclude-all + re-include-for-video-route
never worked. Remove ffmpeg from excludes and let NFT naturally trace
it only into the video webhook route that imports it.

title: 🐛 fix: filter v-prefixed Docker tags in manifest creation
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Fix the jq filter in `release-docker.yml` manifest creation step. The original filter operated on full image references (e.g. `lobehub/lobehub:v2.1.29`) but checked `startswith("v")` and `== "latest"` against the full string, which never matched. Changed to `test(":v\\d")` to correctly detect v-prefixed tags in full image references.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

CI workflow change — verified jq logic manually.

## Summary by Sourcery

CI:
- Adjust release-docker workflow to filter out v-prefixed Docker tags when building the manifest list, keeping only non-v version tags and latest.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `test(":v\d")` filter is a bit implicit and may miss or include unexpected tags (e.g., non-numeric `v` tags); consider matching on the tag portion explicitly (e.g., `.| split(":")[1] | startswith("v")`) and whitelisting `latest` to make the intent clearer.
- If all tags are filtered out, `$TAGS` will be empty and `docker buildx imagetools create` will run without any `-t` arguments; consider adding a simple check to skip the manifest creation step or fail fast with a clear message in that case.

title: 🐛 fix: include pnpm store path for ffmpeg-static in Vercel tracing
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔀 Description of Change

On Vercel with pnpm, `outputFileTracingExcludes` removes ffmpeg-static from all routes (both `node_modules/ffmpeg-static/**` and `node_modules/.pnpm/ffmpeg-static*/**`). The `outputFileTracingIncludes` only added back `./node_modules/ffmpeg-static/ffmpeg` (the hoisted symlink) for the video webhook route, but the actual binary at the pnpm store path (`.pnpm/ffmpeg-static@*/node_modules/ffmpeg-static/ffmpeg`) was never re-included — causing `ENOENT` at runtime.

This fix adds the pnpm virtual store glob path to `outputFileTracingIncludes` so both the symlink and the real binary are bundled into the video webhook serverless function.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

Deploy to Vercel and verify video generation webhook processes videos without `spawn ffmpeg ENOENT` error.

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure the ffmpeg-static binary from pnpm's virtual store is included in Vercel output file tracing for the video webhook route to prevent ENOENT errors at runtime.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider using a more generic glob (e.g. `**/ffmpeg-static/ffmpeg`) instead of hardcoding the `.pnpm` virtual store path so the tracing rule remains resilient to changes in pnpm’s store layout or configuration.
- If this include is only relevant on Vercel with pnpm, you might want to guard or comment it explicitly as such to make its purpose and environment-specific nature clear to future maintainers.

🐛 fix: include pnpm store path for ffmpeg-static in Vercel tracing

The outputFileTracingIncludes only included the hoisted symlink path
`./node_modules/ffmpeg-static/ffmpeg`, but in pnpm the actual binary
lives under `.pnpm/ffmpeg-static@*/...`. Since the excludes remove both
paths from all routes, the include must also add the pnpm store path
back for the video webhook route.

title: 🐛 fix: harden Anthropic message building and sampling parameter handling
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This PR addresses multiple Anthropic API request validation errors:

**1. Guard tool_use/tool_result message sequence**
- Replaced fragile sequential `pendingToolResults` accumulation with a two-pass Map-based pairing strategy
- Unpaired `tool_use` blocks are now filtered from assistant messages, preventing `tool_use ids were found without tool_result blocks` errors
- Unpaired `tool` messages gracefully degrade to plain text user messages

**2. Filter empty system prompts and user messages**
- Empty/whitespace-only system prompts are now treated as `undefined` across all Anthropic-compatible factories (anthropicCompatibleFactory, generateObject, bedrock)
- Empty/whitespace-only user messages return `undefined` and are skipped during message building
- User messages with all content parts filtered out also return `undefined`

**3. Resolve temperature/top_p conflict for Claude 4+ models**
- Extracted `resolveModelSamplingParameters` in `parameterResolver.ts` to encapsulate model conflict detection + parameter resolution
- Applied sanitization across all code paths: Chat Completion, custom client stream, and Responses API
- `ParameterConfig` now accepts `null` values (converted to `undefined` internally)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

#### 📝 Additional Information

All three fixes target production errors observed with Claude 4+ models (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, etc.).

## Summary by Sourcery

Harden Anthropic-compatible message construction and sampling parameter resolution to avoid API validation errors and model-specific conflicts.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure tool_use and tool_result messages are correctly paired and filter out unpaired tool_use blocks while degrading orphaned tool messages to plain user text.
- Skip empty or whitespace-only system prompts and user messages, including cases where all user content parts are filtered out, when building Anthropic and Bedrock payloads.
- Resolve temperature and top_p conflicts for Claude 4+ models by centralizing sampling parameter handling and respecting model-specific constraints across all Anthropic-compatible entry points.

Enhancements:
- Allow null sampling-related parameter values in configuration and normalize them to undefined in parameter resolution logic.
- Reuse a shared sampling parameter resolver across chat completions, custom streaming clients, and Responses API to consistently sanitize temperature and top_p inputs.

Tests:
- Add and update unit tests for Anthropic message building, system prompt filtering, parameter resolution, and model-specific sampling parameter behavior.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `buildAnthropicMessages`, `toolResultsByCallId` stores only a single `ToolResultBlockParam` per `tool_call_id`, so multiple `tool` messages for the same call id will be overwritten; consider either aggregating all matching tool messages or detecting and rejecting this case explicitly.
- In the OpenAI-compatible runtime, sampling parameters are resolved from `handledPayload` for the default client path but from `processedPayload` for the custom client path; aligning both to use the same base payload (probably `handledPayload`) would avoid subtle inconsistencies if `handlePayload` mutates temperature/top_p.
- The new `systemPromptText` logic only supports string system content and will drop non-string (e.g., content-part array) system messages; if you expect structured system content in the future, you may want to route it through the same content-to-block conversion used for user messages rather than silently omitting it.

## Overall Comments
- In `buildAnthropicMessages`, `toolResultsByCallId` stores only a single `ToolResultBlockParam` per `tool_call_id`, so multiple `tool` messages for the same call id will be overwritten; consider either aggregating all matching tool messages or detecting and rejecting this case explicitly.
- In the OpenAI-compatible runtime, sampling parameters are resolved from `handledPayload` for the default client path but from `processedPayload` for the custom client path; aligning both to use the same base payload (probably `handledPayload`) would avoid subtle inconsistencies if `handlePayload` mutates temperature/top_p.
- The new `systemPromptText` logic only supports string system content and will drop non-string (e.g., content-part array) system messages; if you expect structured system content in the future, you may want to route it through the same content

New Features: - Register three new Grok 4.20 experimental chat models (multi-agent, reasoning, and non-reasoning) with pricing, capabilities, and extended parameters. - Introduce a dedicated Grok 4.20 reasoning-effort slider control in the model switch panel and corresponding chat configuration options. - Route designated Grok models through the Responses API and wire Grok 4.20-specific reasoning effort into model extend parameters. Enhancements: - Disable the older Grok 4 flagship entry in favor of the new 4.20 series and remove the legacy Grok 2 Vision model from the catalog. - Document Grok 4.20 reasoning-effort behavior in model provider locale hints. ## Summary by Sourcery Add early support for the xAI Grok 4.20 chat model series and wire their reasoning-effort controls through the Responses API integration. New Features: - Register three experimental Grok 4.20 chat models, including multi-agent, reasoning, and non-reasoning variants, with capabilities and pricing metadata. - Expose a Grok 4.20-specific reasoning-effort slider and extend param so users can tune agent count and reasoning intensity for supported models. Enhancements: - Route Grok models through the Responses API by default and request encrypted reasoning content in responses. - Mark the older Grok 4 flagship model as disabled in favor of the new 4.20 series and remove the deprecated Grok 2 Vision model from the catalog. - Update provider configuration, chat config schema, and localization hints to support Grok 4.20 reasoning-effort options in settings and UI.
author: hezhijie0327

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The new `grok4_20ReasoningEffort` slider is wired only to the multi-agent model (`extendParams: ['grok4_20ReasoningEffort']`), so if the reasoning/non-reasoning 4.20 variants are meant to support this control as well, they should also declare the same `extendParams` key or the slider should be conditionally shown only for models that include it.
- You’ve forced only `grok-4.20-multi-agent-experimental-beta-0304` through the Responses API via `responsesAPIGrokModels`, while the other 4.20 models still default to Chat Completions unless `enabledSearch` is set; double-check whether the intent is for the entire 4.20 series to be Responses-only and update the model set or routing logic accordingly.

**Add 7 new NVIDIA NIM models:**
   - MiniMax-M2.1 / MiniMax-M2.5 - MoE models for coding and agent performance
   - DeepSeek V3.2 - Next-gen reasoning model with chain-of-thought capabilities
   - GLM-4.7 / GLM-5 - Zhipu AI flagship models, optimized for Agentic Coding
   - Kimi K2.5 - Moonshot AI's latest model with native multimodal support
   - Qwen3.5-397B-A17B - Alibaba's multimodal model supporting text/image/video input

   **Tweak the behavior of enable thinking:**
   Implemented different thinking parameter formats for different model types:
   - GLM models (`z-ai/glm4.7`, `z-ai/glm5`): use `enable_thinking + clear_thinking` (Preserved Thinking)
   - Qwen models (`qwen/qwen3.5-397b-a17b`): use `enable_thinking + thinking_budget`
   - Other models: use standard `thinking` parameter

- The model-specific thinking behavior (`supportPreservedThinkingModels` and `enableThinkingModels`) is hardcoded by ID in the provider; consider deriving these behaviors from the model metadata (e.g., capabilities or settings in `nvidiaChatModels`) to avoid future drift when adding similar models.

## Summary by Sourcery

Introduce a new LobeHub CLI application and shared device gateway client for connecting local environments to the LobeHub device gateway, executing remote tool calls, and managing authentication.

New Features:
- Add @lobehub/cli application with commands to log in via OIDC device flow, log out, connect to the device gateway, and check gateway connectivity status.
- Implement local tool execution framework for the CLI, including file operations and shell command execution with background support and output management.
- Introduce a reusable @lobechat/device-gateway-client package that encapsulates the WebSocket protocol, connection lifecycle, and HTTP APIs for device status, system info, and remote tool calls.
- Add secure local credential storage with automatic access token refresh and token resolution logic shared across CLI commands.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `shell.ts#getCommandOutput`, the `filter` logic uses a global regex (`/gm`) with `regex.test` inside a loop, which mutates `lastIndex` and can cause some matching lines to be skipped; consider either removing the `g` flag or resetting `regex.lastIndex = 0` on each iteration.
- In `file.ts#editLocalFile`, the `replace_all` branch mixes a regex (for counting matches) with `content.replaceAll(old_string, new_string)` (string replacement), so counts may not match actual replacements and escaping is duplicated; using a single `RegExp` for both matching and replacement (e.g. `content.replace(regex, new_string)`) would be more consistent and less error‑prone.
- In `connect.ts`, the `auth_expired` handler refreshes credentials via `resolveToken({})` but then always calls `cleanup()` and `process.exit(1)`, so the user cannot actually continue with the refreshed token; either update the `GatewayClient` instance with the new token and reconnect, or adjust the log messaging to reflect that the process will exit and require a manual restart.

Add first-class Lark and Feishu bot integration, including webhook handling, messaging, and configuration UI support.

New Features:
- Support Lark and Feishu as bot platforms alongside Discord and Telegram, including direct webhook handling for incoming messages and agent execution.
- Introduce a Lark/Feishu REST API client and platform adapter to send, edit, and reply to messages via tenant access tokens.
- Extend the integration UI and configuration model to handle Lark/Feishu app-secret based auth with verification and encryption keys, plus localized descriptions.

Enhancements:
- Generalize bot reply rendering so non-Discord platforms (Telegram, Lark, Feishu) display usage stats without Discord-specific markdown.
- Update the bot callback webhook to work with both token-based and app-secret based platforms, routing replies through the appropriate REST clients.

Documentation:
- Add and wire localization strings for new integration fields and platform descriptions for Lark and Feishu.

title: 🔒 fix: add rate limit custom rules for password reset and email verification
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-2887

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add stricter rate limiting (`customRules`) for two Better Auth endpoints that previously only had the loose global rate limit (10s/100 requests):

- `/request-password-reset`: 60s window, max 3 requests
- `/send-verification-email`: 60s window, max 3 requests

This mitigates brute-force password reset attempts and email abuse.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] No tests needed

## Summary by Sourcery

Tighten Better Auth configuration by introducing endpoint-specific rate limiting rules for sensitive password reset and email verification flows.

New Features:
- Add custom rate limit rules for the /request-password-reset endpoint.
- Add custom rate limit rules for the /send-verification-email endpoint.

Bug Fixes:
- Mitigate potential brute-force and abuse scenarios by restricting request frequency for password reset and verification email endpoints.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:

- The `rateLimit` block here fully replaces any existing `rateLimit` config on the Better Auth instance; if other rate limit options are configured elsewhere (e.g., global settings), consider merging `customOptions.rateLimit` or spreading existing config so those settings are not silently dropped.
- Double-check that the custom rule keys (`'/request-password-reset'` and `'/send-verification-email'`) match the actual routed paths (including any prefixes or versioning) so that the stricter limits are actually applied in production.

title: ✨ feat(cli): add generate command for text/image/video/tts/asr
body:
## Summary

close LOBE-5711

Implements `lh generate` command (LOBE-5711) with full content generation capabilities:

- **`lh generate text <prompt>`** — Single LLM text completion with SSE streaming, supports `--model provider/model`, `--system`, `--temperature`, `--pipe` (stdin)
- **`lh generate image <prompt>`** — Image generation via async task (`trpc.image.createImage`)
- **`lh generate video <prompt>`** — Video generation via async task (`trpc.video.createVideo`)
- **`lh generate tts <text>`** — Text-to-speech with openai/microsoft/edge backends, saves to file
- **`lh generate asr <file>`** — Speech-to-text via OpenAI Whisper
- **`lh generate status <genId> <taskId>`** — Check async generation task status
- **`lh generate list`** — List generation topics
- Adds shared HTTP auth helper (`api/http.ts`) for webapi endpoints

Also includes previous bug fixes:
- Fix ESM `require` in confirm helper → use ESM import
- Fix unhandled rejections in login.ts → add `return` after `process.exit(1)`
- Fix memory create → only support identity (other categories lack create procedures)
- Add `file` and `skill` commands, remove deprecated `session` command

## Test plan

- [x] 13 new tests for generate command (text streaming, non-streaming, provider parsing, image, video, tts, asr, status, list)
- [x] All 322 tests pass across 26 test files
- [x] Build succeeds with `tsup`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add a new `lh generate` CLI command that provides unified content generation for text, image, video, TTS, and ASR, along with status and listing of generation topics.

New Features:
- Introduce `generate`/`gen` CLI command with subcommands for text, image, video, TTS, and ASR generation.
- Support listing generation topics and checking async generation task status from the CLI.
- Add shared HTTP auth helper for authenticated web API access from CLI commands.

Tests:
- Add comprehensive tests covering text generation (streaming and non-streaming), image/video generation, TTS/ASR flows, status checks, and topic listing.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In `getAuthInfo` you call `process.exit(1)` when no token is found but then continue execution and use a non-null assertion on `result`; add an explicit `return`/`throw` after the exit and drop the `!` to avoid dead code and potential type unsafety.
- The TTS payload currently repeats `model` and `voice` at both the top level and inside `options`; consider normalizing this shape (e.g., only using `options`) to reduce redundancy and keep the request contract clearer.
