title: ✨ feat(cli): add full API integration commands In cli
body:
## Summary

Implements the full CLI API integration as specified in [LOBE-5706](https://linear.app/lobehub/issue/LOBE-5706). This PR adds comprehensive CLI commands for managing all LobeHub resources through the `lh` CLI tool.

### New Commands

**P0 — Search, Knowledge Base, Memory:**
- `lh search <query>` — Global unified search across agents, topics, files, messages, etc.
- `lh kb` — Knowledge base CRUD, file association management
- `lh memory` — User memory management (identity/activity/context/experience/preference), persona view, memory extraction

**P1 — Agent, Session, Topic, Message:**
- `lh agent` — Agent CRUD (list/view/create/edit/delete/duplicate). Note: `agent run` deferred to [LOBE-5771](https://linear.app/lobehub/issue/LOBE-5771)
- `lh session` — Session management with search
- `lh topic` — Topic CRUD with search and recent topics
- `lh message` — Message listing, search, deletion, count, heatmap

**P2 — Model, Provider:**
- `lh model` — Model listing per provider, toggle enable/disable, delete
- `lh provider` — Provider listing, toggle, delete

**P3 — Plugin, User/Config:**
- `lh plugin` — Plugin install/uninstall/update
- `lh whoami` — Display current user information
- `lh usage` — Usage statistics (monthly/daily)

### Architecture

- Extracted shared formatting utilities (`timeAgo`, `truncate`, `printTable`, `outputJson`, `confirm`) into `utils/format.ts`
- All commands support `--json [fields]` output for scripting and pipeline integration
- All commands follow the established pattern from the `doc` command

### Sub-Issues Created

- [LOBE-5770](https://linear.app/lobehub/issue/LOBE-5770) — Agent CRUD ✅ (done in this PR)
- [LOBE-5771](https://linear.app/lobehub/issue/LOBE-5771) — Agent Run (deferred)
- [LOBE-5772](https://linear.app/lobehub/issue/LOBE-5772) — Skills command (deferred)

## Test Plan

- [x] All 11 new test files with 290 tests passing
- [x] Build succeeds with `tsup`
- [x] All commands register correctly (`lh --help`)
- [x] Existing tests remain passing

Closes LOBE-5706, LOBE-5770

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Summary by Sourcery

Add comprehensive CLI commands for searching and managing LobeHub resources and factor shared formatting utilities into a common module.

New Features:
- Introduce a global `search` command for querying across agents, topics, files, messages, and other resources.
- Add `kb` command for knowledge base CRUD operations and file association management.
- Add `memory` command for managing user memories, persona summaries, and extraction tasks from chat history.
- Add `agent` command for agent CRUD operations and duplication.
- Add `session` command for listing, searching, creating, editing, and deleting chat sessions.
- Add `topic` command for topic listing, search, CRUD, and batch deletion, including recent topics.
- Add `message` command for listing, searching, deleting, counting, and visualizing message activity via heatmaps.
- Add `model` command for listing, inspecting, enabling/disabling, and deleting AI models per provider.
- Add `provider` command for listing, inspecting, enabling/disabling, and deleting AI providers.
- Add `plugin` command for listing, installing, updating, and uninstalling plugins.
- Add `whoami` and `usage` commands for user identity and usage statistics reporting from the CLI.

Enhancements:
- Extract shared CLI formatting, table printing, JSON output, and confirmation helpers into a reusable `utils/format` module.
- Wire all new commands into the CLI entrypoint so they are available via the main `lh` command.

Tests:
- Add dedicated test suites for each new command group (search, kb, memory, agent, session, topic, message, model, provider, plugin, config) to validate behavior and TRPC integration.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- The new `confirm` helper in `utils/format.ts` uses `require('node:readline')` instead of a top-level ES import like the rest of the CLI; consider switching to `import readline from 'node:readline'` for consistency with the module style and to avoid potential bundling/TS issues.
- There is a lot of repeated pagination and limit parsing logic across commands (e.g., `list`/`recent` handlers in `topic`, `session`, `message`, `model`); consider extracting a small shared helper (e.g., `parsePaginationOptions` or `parseLimit`) to reduce duplication and keep behavior consistent.
- Most commands use the shared `log` logger for error reporting, but the `search` command prints invalid type errors with `console.error`; aligning this with `log.error` would keep CLI error output consistent.

title: 🐛 fix: cli login and run browser in Windows
body: #### 💻 Change Type - [ ] ✨ feat - [x] 🐛 fix - [ ] ♻️ refactor - [ ] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [ ] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔗 Related Issue #### 🔀 Description of Change - openBrowser: - 先判断有没有浏览器，然后再执行 - Windows 下唤起浏览器 - route - 不拦截用于 device 认证的接口 #### 🧪 How to Test - [x] Tested locally - [x] Added/updated tests - [ ] No tests needed #### 📸 Screenshots / Videos | fix | Before | After | | --- | ------ | ----- | | 1| | | | 2 | | | | 3 | | ![fix-browser](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87261f60-2a75-435f-8be5-fd1ca43c49dd) | #### 📝 Additional Information ## Summary by Sourcery Improve CLI login robustness and cross-platform browser launching, and adjust proxy configuration for device authorization. Bug Fixes: - Handle non-JSON responses from authentication endpoints with clearer error messages instead of failing silently. - Fix automatic browser launching for CLI login across platforms, including Windows, and surface a warning when it cannot be opened. - Ensure Windows executables like rundll32 are resolved correctly using PATH, PATHEXT, and SystemRoot to avoid failures when opening the browser. - Expose the OIDC device authorization endpoint through the Next.js proxy configuration so CLI device login works correctly. Enhancements: - Add a reusable helper for resolving command executables and a dedicated JSON parsing utility to centralize response handling. Tests: - Add a unit test to verify Windows executable resolution via PATH and PATHEXT for the login command.

title: 🐛 fix: detect exceeded context window errors from message text
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5745

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Some providers (DeepSeek, Claude, Google Gemini, MiniMax, Aihubmix) return context window exceeded errors with non-standard error codes, causing them to be misclassified as `ProviderBizError` instead of `ExceededContextWindow`. This prevents the frontend from showing the dedicated context-exceeded UI.

This PR adds message-based fallback detection via a shared `isExceededContextWindowError` utility that matches 11 known error message patterns across providers:

- **New**: `isExceededContextWindowError` utility with pattern matching for all known provider error formats
- **OpenAI-compatible factory**: Added message-based fallback after error code switch-case (covers DeepSeek, MiniMax non-streaming)
- **Anthropic-compatible factory**: Added message-based detection in both `handleDefaultAnthropicError` and class `handleError` (covers Claude)
- **Google error parser**: Added early return detection in `parseGoogleErrorMessage` (covers Google Gemini)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [x] No tests needed

All 143 existing tests pass. The change is a fallback that only activates when error codes don't match, so existing behavior is preserved.

## Summary by Sourcery

Detect provider context-window-exceeded errors based on error message text to correctly classify them as ExceededContextWindow across providers.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure Anthropic-compatible runtimes classify context-window-exceeded errors using message-based detection when provider error codes are non-standard.
- Ensure OpenAI-compatible runtimes fall back to message-based detection to classify context-window-exceeded errors when error codes do not match.
- Correctly detect Google provider context-window-exceeded errors in the Google error parser using message patterns.

Enhancements:
- Introduce a shared isExceededContextWindowError utility that matches multiple known provider error message patterns for context window violations.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider extracting a small helper for deriving `errorMsg` from provider error shapes (e.g., Anthropic/OpenAI `errorResult`) to avoid duplicating slightly different `errorResult.message || errorResult.error?.message` logic in multiple places.
- In `isExceededContextWindowError`, you already lowercase the input message; you might want to add a brief comment clarifying that patterns are intentionally all lowercase to rely on this, so future additions don’t accidentally assume case-sensitive matching.

title: ✨ feat(topic): add sort by updated time option for topic sidebar
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5742

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Add a third topic display mode **"Group by Updated Time"** to the sidebar topic list dropdown menu.

**Changes:**
- Add `ByUpdatedTime` enum value to `TopicDisplayMode`
- Rename `ByTime` key to `ByCreatedTime` (value stays `'byTime'` for backward compatibility)
- Add parameterized `groupTopicsByField` utility to eliminate duplication between `groupTopicsByTime` and `groupTopicsByUpdatedTime`
- Add `createGroupedTopicsForSidebar` factory to eliminate selector duplication
- Touch topic's `updatedAt` in `MessageModel.create` and `MessageModel.update` so that sending/editing messages updates the topic's last edit time
- Support the new mode in all 4 topic list rendering paths (agent List, agent TopicListContent, group List, group TopicListContent)
- Fix `expandedKeys` to auto-expand new groups when switching display modes
- Update i18n: "Group by Time" → "Group by Created Time", add "Group by Updated Time"

#### 🧪 How to Test

1. Open the topic sidebar dropdown menu → should show 3 options: "Group by Created Time" / "Group by Updated Time" / "No Grouping"
2. Select "Group by Updated Time" → send a message to an old topic → it should move to the "Today" group
3. Select "Group by Created Time" → behavior identical to previous "Group by Time"
4. Select "No Grouping" → flat list, unchanged behavior
5. Switch between modes → all groups should be expanded (no collapsed groups from stale keys)

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

commits.message:
✨ feat(topic): add sort by updated time option for topic sidebar

Add a new "Group by Updated Time" display mode for the topic sidebar,
allowing users to see recently edited topics grouped by their last
update time instead of creation time. Also touch topic's updatedAt
when messages are created or updated.

Fixes LOBE-5742

♻️ refactor(topic): simplify topic grouping selectors and extract shared helpers

- Extract touchTopicUpdatedAt helper in MessageModel to consolidate 3 duplicate occurrences
- Add topic updatedAt touch in batchCreate for consistency
- Extract buildGroupedTopics to eliminate duplication between groupedTopicsSelector and groupedTopicsForSidebar
- Merge groupedTopicsForSidebar and groupedTopicsForSidebarByUpdatedTime into single selector with displayMode param
- Stabilize selector reference with useMemo in ByTimeMode components
- Remove unnecessary type assertions and unused imports

🐛 fix(topic): preserve collapsed group state when new groups appear

Previously, any new group key caused all groups to expand, losing
user's manually collapsed state. Now only new groups auto-expand
while existing collapsed groups stay collapsed.

🐛 fix(topic): fix group collapse and optimistic updatedAt update

- Fix expandedKeys logic: use useEffect to reset on display mode
  switch instead of hasNewGroups heuristic which broke collapse
- Optimistically update topic updatedAt on client after sending
  message to existing topic so sidebar re-groups immediately
✅ test: add tests for optimistic topic updatedAt update on message send

## Summary by Sourcery

Add background daemon support and API client utilities to the CLI and prepare the package for distribution as a built Node.js binary.

New Features:
- Add a daemon mode to the `connect` command with foreground/daemon logging and connection status tracking.
- Introduce `connect` subcommands to manage the daemon lifecycle, including stopping, restarting, checking status, and tailing logs.
- Add an API client wrapper for authenticated tRPC-style queries and mutations based on stored credentials.

Enhancements:
- Refine connect command authentication handling and logging, including updated messaging on authentication expiry.
- Add a daemon manager module to persist PID, status, and rotating logs under a user-specific directory.

Build:
- Switch the CLI package to build a distributable ESM Node.js binary with tsup and point the `lh` bin to the compiled entry file.
- Adjust package.json dependencies, type/module settings, and publish configuration to support publishing the CLI to npm.
- Exclude test files from the CLI TypeScript compilation configuration.

Tests:
- Extend connect command tests to cover daemon mode, lifecycle subcommands, and updated logging behavior.
- Add tests for the daemon manager module and update login tests to mock additional child_process APIs as needed.
author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `connect logs` subcommand shells out to `tail`, which will fail on non-Unix environments (e.g., Windows); consider using a Node-based tailing implementation or a cross-platform dependency instead of relying on the external binary.
- ApiClient methods call `process.exit(1)` on error, which tightly couples this utility to CLI execution and makes it hard to reuse or unit-test in isolation; consider throwing typed errors and letting the command handlers decide when/how to exit.
- The daemon vs foreground logging paths duplicate a fair amount of logic (manual info/error wrappers plus `createDaemonLogger`); consider unifying around a single logger abstraction that can be swapped (e.g., `log` vs daemon logger) to reduce branching and make behavior easier to reason about.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The `connect logs` subcommand shells out to `tail`, which will fail on non-Unix environments (e.g., Windows); consider using a Node-based tailing implementation or a cross-platform dependency instead of relying on the external binary.
- ApiClient methods call `process.exit(1)` on error, which tightly couples this utility to CLI execution and makes it hard to reuse or unit-test in isolation; consider throwing typed errors and letting the command handlers decide when/how to exit.
- The daemon vs foreground logging paths duplicate a fair amount of logic (manual info/error wrappers plus `createDaemonLogger`); consider unifying around a single logger abstraction that can be swapped (e.g., `log` vs daemon logger) to reduce branching and make behavior easier to reason about.
~~~

</details>

title: 🚀 release: 20260306
body:
This release includes **31 commits**. Key updates are below.

### New Features and Enhancements

- Added **Telegram bot access** support.
- Added **electron page tabs** functionality for desktop.
- Added **device code auth flow** for authentication.
- Added **GPT-5.4** model support.
- Show **last used auth provider** on sign-in page for better UX.
- Support **clearing hotkey bindings** in desktop ShortcutManager.
- Added **Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Preview** model and thinkingLevel5 extend param.
- Added **auto aspect ratio and image search** support for Nano Banana 2.
- User memories now default to inject user persona instead of identities.

### Desktop Improvements

- Unified **update channel switching** with S3 distribution.
- Added **S3 publish for canary/nightly** and S3 cleanup (keep latest 15).
- Added electron page tabs functionality.

### Stability and Fixes

- Fixed agents fork not working in community deploy.
- Fixed animation for single-line messages between reasoning and tool calls.
- Fixed Discord bot conflict with keyPrefix.
- Fixed skew plugin issue.
- Fixed `userMemories` database failure on extra structure mismatch.
- Fixed old LobeHub plugins update issue.
- Fixed context-engine tool type recovery from manifest when models strip suffixes.
- Added `await` to `handleResponseAPIMode` for proper error handling.
- Fixed M2M token for community agents/MCP/skill list.
- Fixed scripts to support Win32.
- Improved gateway and device gateway CI.

### Credits

Huge thanks to these contributors (alphabetical):

@arvinxx @huangkairan @Innei @LiJian @Luis-Sambrano @nekomeowww @rdmclin2 @ReneWang @sxjeru @tjx666

title: 🐛 fix(ci): add version prefix to S3 update manifest URLs
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The `merge-mac-files` step already renames `latest*.yml` to `{channel}*.yml` (e.g., `canary-mac.yml`, `canary-linux.yml`). The S3 publish action was only targeting `release/latest*.yml` for URL prefix injection, which matched **nothing** — so the `sed` was a no-op.

As a result, `canary-mac.yml` uploaded to S3 had URLs like:
```
url: LobeHub-Canary-2.1.38-canary.2-arm64-mac.zip
```

Resolving to `cdn.../canary/LobeHub-Canary-xxx.zip` (404), when the actual file is at:
```
cdn.../canary/2.1.38-canary.2/LobeHub-Canary-xxx.zip
```

**Fix:** Target `release/${CHANNEL}*.yml` (with `latest*.yml` as fallback) for the `sed -i` version prefix injection.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

Trigger a canary desktop release and verify `canary-mac.yml` in S3 contains version-prefixed URLs (e.g., `url: 2.1.38-canary.3/LobeHub-Canary-...`).

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Update the S3 manifest URL rewrite step to operate on channel-specific and latest YAML files so generated URLs include the version directory prefix.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The `sed -i "s|url: |url: $VERSION/|g"` replacement will re-prefix URLs if this step is ever re-run; consider making it idempotent (e.g., anchor on `^url:` and/or only replace when the line does not already start with `url: $VERSION/`).
- Using `sed -i` without a backup extension is GNU-specific; if this action might ever run on macOS runners, consider a portable form (e.g., `sed -i.bak ... && rm *.bak`) to avoid BSD `sed` compatibility issues.

title: 🐛 fix(ci): add version prefix to latest*.yml URLs in S3 upload
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [ ] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

The `latest*.yml` files uploaded to the S3 channel root directory lacked the `$VERSION/` prefix in their `url:` fields. When electron-updater reads `latest-mac.yml`, it resolves URLs like:

```
https://cdn-desktop-releases.lobehub.com/canary/LobeHub-Canary-2.1.38-canary.1-arm64-mac.zip
```

But the actual files are stored at:

```
https://cdn-desktop-releases.lobehub.com/canary/2.1.38-canary.1/LobeHub-Canary-2.1.38-canary.1-arm64-mac.zip
```

This results in **404 errors** during auto-update, preventing differential and full downloads.

**Fix:** Apply `sed -i` to `latest*.yml` in-place before uploading, adding the `$VERSION/` prefix to all `url:` fields. The `${CHANNEL}*.yml` files are then copied from the already-modified `latest*.yml` instead of re-running `sed`.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

Trigger a canary/nightly desktop release and verify the uploaded `latest-mac.yml` contains version-prefixed URLs (e.g., `url: 2.1.39-canary.1/LobeHub-Canary-...`).

#### 📝 Additional Information

This fixes the 404 auto-update failure reported for canary desktop builds. The `stable` channel was unaffected because electron-updater reads `stable-mac.yml` (which already had the prefix), but `latest-mac.yml` was also served without it.

## Summary by Sourcery

Ensure desktop release manifests in S3 use version-prefixed URLs to match the actual artifact storage layout and prevent updater 404s.

Bug Fixes:
- Add the version directory prefix to all URLs in latest*.yml manifests before upload so auto-updates resolve to existing files.

CI:
- Adjust the desktop S3 publish GitHub Action to modify latest*.yml in-place and reuse them when generating channel-specific manifest files.

reviews.body:

- The workflow step `uses: actions/setup-node@v6` does not correspond to a published major version of `actions/setup-node`; consider pinning to an existing major (e.g. `@v4`) or a specific commit SHA to avoid runtime failures.

title: 🐛 fix: resolve message reordering in Responses API input conversion
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes #9900

#### 🔀 Description of Change

`convertOpenAIResponseInputs` used `Promise.all(messages.map(...))` with side-effect pushes to a shared `input` array. When a message contained array content (e.g. images) requiring async processing, the `await` yielded control, allowing subsequent synchronous iterations to push their items first — resulting in out-of-order Responses API input.

**Root cause:** `await Promise.all(resolvedPromises)` always yields to the microtask queue, even when all inner promises are already resolved. This means messages with array content (which hit `await`) get pushed after messages that follow them in the original array but execute synchronously.

**Impact:** In multi-turn conversations where an earlier message has image content, later messages (including the latest user query) could appear before the image message in the API input. The model would then respond to the image message instead of the actual latest query.

**Fix:** Changed from side-effect pushes to a shared array to collecting items per-message and flattening, which preserves the original message order regardless of async timing.

#### 🧪 How to Test

1. Start a conversation with an image message (e.g. "describe this image")
2. After getting a response, send a text-only follow-up (e.g. "1+1=?")
3. Verify the model answers the follow-up correctly instead of re-describing the image

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Fix message reordering in Responses API input caused by shared array mutations during async message processing.

🐛 fix: resolve message reordering in Responses API input conversion

`convertOpenAIResponseInputs` used `Promise.all(messages.map(...))` with
side-effect pushes to a shared array. When a message required async content

Changed to collect items per-message and flatten, preserving original order.

title: 👷 build(ci): fix changelog auto-generation in release workflow
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [ ] ✨ feat
- [ ] 🐛 fix
- [ ] ♻️ refactor
- [ ] 💄 style
- [x] 👷 build
- [ ] ⚡️ perf
- [ ] ✅ test
- [ ] 📝 docs
- [ ] 🔨 chore

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

After `auto-tag-release.yml` was introduced (#12236), `semantic-release` in `release.yml` stopped working because the git tag already exists when it runs. This caused `CHANGELOG.md` and `changelog/*.json` to never be updated.

**Root cause:** `auto-tag-release.yml` creates the tag first → `release.yml` triggers on tag push → `semantic-release` sees tag exists → skips all plugins (including `@semantic-release/changelog`).

**Fix:**
- Add a self-contained changelog generation script (`scripts/changelogWorkflow/generateChangelog.ts`) that parses `git log` and produces gitmoji-formatted entries matching the existing CHANGELOG.md format
- Move changelog generation into `auto-tag-release.yml` (bump version → generate changelog → build static JSON → commit → tag)
- Remove the broken `semantic-release` step from `release.yml`

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Run `bun run workflow:changelog:gen` — verifies script executes (skips if no new commits since last tag)
2. Merge a PR to `main` with a release-triggering title → verify CHANGELOG.md is updated in the release commit

#### 📝 Additional Information

The script generates changelog entries in the exact same format as the previous `semantic-release` + `conventional-changelog-gitmoji-config` pipeline, including summary section, details section with issue links and commit hashes, and back-to-top badge.

## Summary by Sourcery

Generate changelogs during the auto-tag release workflow and remove the broken semantic-release step from the release workflow.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure CHANGELOG.md and static changelog JSON files are correctly generated and committed for each tagged release despite pre-existing tags.

Enhancements:
- Introduce a standalone changelog generation script that builds gitmoji-formatted changelog entries from git history to match the existing changelog format.

Build:
- Update auto-tag release workflow to install Node/Bun, bump version, generate changelog artifacts, and commit them before tagging.
- Remove semantic-release-based release step and tag/version verification from the release workflow in favor of the auto-tag pipeline.
- Add a new package script to run a standalone changelog generation script used by the release workflow.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 3 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In `generateChangelog.ts`, the `getPreviousTag` helper and `prevTag`/`fromTag` logic are effectively unused (since `fromTag` always resolves to `lastTag` when present), which makes the tag range selection confusing; consider either removing `getPreviousTag` and simplifying to `lastTag..HEAD` or explicitly handling the "HEAD already tagged" vs "new release" scenarios so the intent is clear.
- In `.github/workflows/auto-tag-release.yml`, the condition `if: steps.release.outputs.should_tag == 'true' || steps.patch.outputs.should_tag == 'true'` is repeated for the Node.js setup, Bun install, and dependency install steps while later logic uses `env.SHOULD_TAG`; you could simplify and reduce duplication by reusing `env.SHOULD_TAG` for these earlier conditional steps as well.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `generateChangelog.ts`, the `getPreviousTag` helper and `prevTag`/`fromTag` logic are effectively unused (since `fromTag` always resolves to `lastTag` when present), which makes the tag range selection confusing; consider either removing `getPreviousTag` and simplifying to `lastTag..HEAD` or explicitly handling the "HEAD already tagged" vs "new release" scenarios so the intent is clear.
- In `.github/workflows/auto-tag-release.yml`, the condition `if: steps.release.outputs.should_tag == 'true' || steps.patch.outputs.should_tag == 'true'` is repeated for the Node.js setup, Bun install, and dependency install steps while later logic uses `env.SHOULD_TAG`; you could simplify and reduce duplication by reusing `env.SHOULD_TAG` for these earlier conditional steps as well.

commits.message:
👷 build(ci): fix changelog auto-generation in release workflow

After auto-tag-release.yml was introduced, semantic-release in release.yml
stopped working because the tag already exists when it runs. This caused
CHANGELOG.md to never be updated.

Fix: move changelog generation into auto-tag-release.yml with a custom
script that parses git log and generates gitmoji-formatted entries,
matching the existing CHANGELOG.md format. Remove the broken
semantic-release step from release.yml.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path=".github/workflows/release-desktop-stable.yml" line_range="237-240" />
<code_context>
         run: |
           npm run desktop:package:app
-          tar -czf apps/desktop/release/lobehub-renderer.tar.gz -C out .
+          test -d apps/desktop/dist/renderer
+          tar -czf apps/desktop/release/lobehub-renderer.tar.gz -C apps/desktop/dist/renderer .
         env:
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**suggestion:** Consider making the directory existence check produce a clearer failure signal or message.

`test -d` will fail with a generic exit code if the directory is missing, which is hard to interpret from CI logs. Consider making the failure explicit, e.g.:

```sh
test -d apps/desktop/dist/renderer || { echo "renderer dir missing"; exit 1; }
```

Alternatively, if the directory should always exist after the previous step, you could skip the explicit check and rely on `tar` to fail with a clearer error. Either approach would make CI failures easier to diagnose.

```suggestion
        run: |
          npm run desktop:package:app
          test -d apps/desktop/dist/renderer || { echo "renderer directory 'apps/desktop/dist/renderer' is missing; desktop packaging likely failed in a previous step."; exit 1; }
          tar -czf apps/desktop/release/lobehub-renderer.tar.gz -C apps/desktop/dist/renderer .
```
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- In `DiscoverService`, the retry path now calls `_doRefreshToken()` directly instead of `injectMPToken()`, which bypasses the new `_tokenRefreshPromise` single-flight logic and can reintroduce concurrent refreshes; consider routing all refresh attempts through `injectMPToken()` to keep the behavior consistent.
- The authentication pre-check logic added to both `ForkAndChat` and `ForkGroupAndChat` is nearly identical; consider extracting a shared helper or hook to avoid duplication and keep future auth-related changes in one place.

This path calls `this._doRefreshToken()` directly, bypassing the `_tokenRefreshPromise` de-duping and `_waitForCookieSet` logic now provided by `injectMPToken`. As a result, 401-triggered refreshes can still run concurrently and won’t wait for the cookie before retrying. Plea

title: ✨ feat: add GPT-5.4 model support
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat
- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-5735

#### 🔀 Description of Change

- Add GPT-5.4 model card to model-bank (lobehub provider)
- Update planCardModels to use gpt-5.4 instead of gpt-5.2
- Add gpt-5.4 to responsesAPIModels set
- Fix `pruneReasoningPayload`: strip `logprobs` and `top_logprobs` for reasoning models (only allow when effort=none)
- Add `logprobs`, `top_logprobs` fields to `ChatStreamPayload` type
- Extend `reasoning_effort` type to include `none` and `xhigh`
- Add success log for non-fallback requests in RouterRuntime
- Fix log parameter mismatch in RouterRuntime success logging

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] No tests needed

## Summary by Sourcery

Add support for the new GPT-5.4 model and adjust reasoning payload handling and logging for OpenAI-based requests.

New Features:
- Introduce GPT-5.4 as a new LobeHub chat model with full capabilities and pricing metadata.
- Use GPT-5.4 as the plan card GPT model and include it in the responses API model set.
- Extend chat payload types to support logprobs/top_logprobs and expanded reasoning_effort options including none and xhigh.

Bug Fixes:
- Ensure reasoning payloads strip unsupported logprobs and top_logprobs fields for non-none reasoning_effort, preventing invalid OpenAI requests.
- Correct RouterRuntime success logging by adding a dedicated message for non-fallback requests with consistent parameters.

Enhancements:
- Add explicit success logging for non-fallback model requests in RouterRuntime.
author: tjx666

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `pruneReasoningPayload`, destructuring `logprobs` and `top_logprobs` out of the payload before the `effort` check means those fields are stripped even when no reasoning effort is set (i.e., non-reasoning requests); consider only pruning them when `effort` is defined and not `none` so standard requests can still use logprobs.
- The `reasoning_effort` union type now includes `'xhigh'`, but `pruneReasoningPayload` still throws for any value outside `['minimal','low','medium','high']`; update the allowed set there to include `'xhigh'` (and adjust the error message) to keep the type and runtime validation in sync.

commits.message:
✨ feat: add GPT-5.4 model support and fix reasoning payload pruning

- Add GPT-5.4 model card to model-bank
- Update planCardModels to use gpt-5.4
- Add gpt-5.4 to responsesAPIModels
- Fix pruneReasoningPayload to strip logprobs/top_logprobs for reasoning models
- Add logprobs, top_logprobs to ChatStreamPayload type
- Extend reasoning_effort to include none and xhigh
- Add success log for non-fallback requests in RouterRuntime
- Fix log parameter mismatch in RouterRuntime

Fixes LOBE-5735
🐛 fix: match gpt-5.4 to gpt5_2ReasoningEffort in openrouter and vercelaigateway
🐛 fix: update OpenRouterReasoning effort type to include none and xhigh
🐛 fix: use tiered pricing for gpt-5.4 based on 272K token threshold
🌐 chore: update i18n translations
🐛 fix: update claude-sonnet model version to 4-6 in planCardModels
✨ feat: add GPT-5.4 Pro model support
🐛 fix: remove dated snapshot for gpt-5.4-pro in responsesAPIModels

🐛 fix: add tierBy support for cross-unit tiered pricing threshold

OpenAI charges output at 1.5x when INPUT exceeds 272K tokens.
The tiered strategy previously only checked the unit's own quantity
to select a tier. Added optional tierBy field to TieredPricingUnit
so output/cacheRead tiers can reference input quantity for selection.

🐛 fix: use totalInputTokens for tiered pricing tier selection

Tiered pricing tiers should be determined by total prompt size
(totalInputTokens), not each unit's own quantity. This fixes output
and cacheRead being charged at the wrong tier rate when the prompt
exceeds the threshold but the individual unit quantity does not.

title: ✨ feat(auth): show last used auth provider on sign-in page
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-3908

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Remember the user's last selected OAuth provider in localStorage and highlight it on the next visit:

- Store the last used provider in `localStorage` (`lobehub:auth:last-provider:v1`) when user clicks a social sign-in button
- Sort the provider list so the last used provider appears first
- Show a `Badge.Ribbon` with "Last used" label on the last used provider button
- Badge uses `@lobehub/ui` Tag info color scheme (`--ant-color-info-fill-tertiary` bg, `--ant-color-info` text)

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

1. Configure multiple SSO providers (e.g. Google, GitHub)
2. Click a provider to sign in
3. Visit the sign-in page again
4. Verify the previously used provider is sorted to the top with a "Last used" ribbon badge
5. Clear localStorage and confirm the default order is restored

#### 📝 Additional Information

- The `localStorage.setItem` is called after `preSocialSigninCheck` passes, so only successful sign-in attempts are recorded
- Write operation is wrapped in try/catch to handle private browsing or quota errors gracefully

## Summary by Sourcery

Highlight and prioritize the last used social auth provider on the sign-in page by remembering it in local storage and surfacing it in the UI.

New Features:
- Remember the last used OAuth provider in local storage and sort it to appear first in the sign-in provider list.
- Display a localized "Last used" ribbon badge on the last used provider button to visually distinguish it.

Enhancements:
- Gracefully handle localStorage access failures when reading or writing the last used provider key.

Enhancements:

- Improve hotkey settings forms to always clear loading state via try/finally when persisting changes.

Build:

Documentation:

Tests:

title: 👷 ci(desktop): add S3 cleanup for canary/nightly (keep latest 15)
body:
## Summary

- Create `.github/actions/desktop-cleanup-s3/` reusable composite action for cleaning up old S3 release versions
- Add S3 cleanup step to canary and nightly workflows, keeping the latest 15 versions
- Cleanup job now waits for both `publish-release` and `publish-s3` to complete before running

## Problem

After #12721 added S3 upload for canary/nightly, old version directories accumulate in S3 with no cleanup mechanism. Each release creates a `{channel}/{version}/` directory with full installers (~500MB+), leading to unbounded storage growth.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `.github/actions/desktop-cleanup-s3/action.yml` | **New** — reusable composite action |
| `.github/workflows/release-desktop-canary.yml` | Add S3 cleanup step, `needs` includes `publish-s3` |
| `.github/workflows/release-desktop-nightly.yml` | Add S3 cleanup step, `needs` includes `publish-s3` |

## How it works

1. Lists all version directories under `s3://{bucket}/{channel}/`
2. Sorts by version (`sort -V`)
3. Keeps the latest 15, recursively deletes older ones
4. Gracefully skips if S3 bucket is not configured

## Summary by Sourcery

Add automated cleanup of old desktop release artifacts from S3 for canary and nightly channels, keeping only the most recent versions.

CI:
- Introduce a reusable composite GitHub Action to prune old S3 desktop release versions while retaining a configurable number of latest versions.
- Extend canary and nightly release workflows to run the S3 cleanup after both GitHub release and S3 publish jobs complete, keeping the latest 15 versions per channel.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- By adding `publish-s3` to `needs` for the cleanup jobs, the GitHub release pruning now also gets skipped whenever `publish-s3` is skipped or fails; consider splitting S3 cleanup into a separate job that `needs: [publish-s3]` while keeping the existing GitHub-release cleanup dependent only on `publish-release`.
- In the composite action, you might want to force a consistent sort order for versions by setting `LC_ALL=C` (e.g. `all_versions=$(LC_ALL=C aws s3 ls ... | sort -V)`), to avoid any locale-dependent differences in how `sort -V` orders version directories.

<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

- In the composite action, you might want to force a consistent sort order for versions by setting `LC_ALL=C` (e.g. `all_versions=$(LC_ALL=C aws s3 ls ... | sort -V)`), to avoid any locale-dependent differences in how `sort -V` orders version directories.

title: 👷 ci(desktop): add S3 publish for canary/nightly and extract reusable action
body:
## Summary

- Extract S3 upload logic into a reusable composite action (`.github/actions/desktop-publish-s3/`)
- **Add missing `publish-s3` job to canary and nightly release workflows** — previously only stable had S3 upload, causing canary/nightly desktop clients to get 404 when checking for updates via S3
- Refactor stable workflow to use the same shared action

## Problem

Desktop update logic (`UpdaterManager.ts`) uses S3 generic provider for all channels when `UPDATE_SERVER_URL` is set. The CI builds for canary and nightly inject `UPDATE_SERVER_URL` at build time, but never upload artifacts to S3. This means:

- Canary clients check `{S3}/canary/canary-mac.yml` → **404**
- Nightly clients check `{S3}/nightly/nightly-mac.yml` → **404**

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `.github/actions/desktop-publish-s3/action.yml` | **New** — reusable composite action |
| `.github/workflows/release-desktop-canary.yml` | Add `publish-s3` job using shared action |
| `.github/workflows/release-desktop-nightly.yml` | Add `publish-s3` job using shared action |
| `.github/workflows/release-desktop-stable.yml` | Refactor to use shared action (no behavior change) |

## How to Test

- [ ] Verify canary workflow YAML is valid (publish-s3 job references correct action)
- [ ] Verify nightly workflow YAML is valid
- [ ] Verify stable workflow still works (refactor only, same behavior)
- [ ] Trigger a manual canary build and confirm S3 upload succeeds

## Summary by Sourcery

Add a reusable composite GitHub Action for publishing desktop release artifacts to S3 and wire it into all release channels.

New Features:
- Introduce a shared desktop S3 publish composite action to handle uploading release artifacts and manifests per channel.
- Enable S3 publishing for desktop canary and nightly release workflows so their builds are uploaded alongside stable.

Enhancements:
- Refactor the stable desktop release workflow to use the shared S3 publish composite action instead of inline scripting.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 3 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In the stable workflow the previous default of `us-east-1` when `UPDATE_S3_REGION` is unset was implemented via `${{ secrets.UPDATE_S3_REGION || 'us-east-1' }}`, but now `s3-region: ${{ secrets.UPDATE_S3_REGION }}` overrides the composite action’s default with an empty string, so you may want to only pass `s3-region` when the secret is set or reintroduce the `|| 'us-east-1'` fallback for all channels.
- The composite action generates `${CHANNEL}-renderer.yml` whenever `lobehub-renderer.tar.gz` exists regardless of channel, but the comment suggests only stable should have a renderer tar; if that’s truly the contract, consider guarding that step with a `CHANNEL == 'stable'` check to avoid surprising manifests for canary/nightly.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

- In the stable workflow the previous default of `us-east-1` when `UPDATE_S3_REGION` is unset was implemented via `${{ secrets.UPDATE_S3_REGION || 'us-east-1' }}`, but now `s3-region: ${{ secrets.UPDATE_S3_REGION }}` overrides the composite action’s default with an empty string, so you may want to only pass `s3-region` when the secret is set or reintroduce the `|| 'us-east-1'` fallback for all channels.
- The composite action generates `${CHANNEL}-renderer.yml` whenever `lobehub-renderer.tar.gz` exists regardless of channel, but the comment suggests only stable should have a renderer tar; if that’s truly the contract, consider guarding that step with a `CHANNEL == 'stable'` check to avoid surprising manifests for canary/nightly.

title: ✨ feat: add electron page tabs functionality
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Related to Electron desktop app enhancements

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Implement browser-style page tabs in the Electron titlebar:

- Add TabBar component with explicit tab creation (no auto-creation)
- Tabs created via: TopicItem/PageItem context menu "Open in New Tab" or double-click (desktop only)
- TabBar only visible when tab count ≥ 2
- Active tab's reference updates when navigating within it (tab follows user navigation)
- Reuse existing plugin system (pluginRegistry, 11 page plugins, PageReference types)
- Persist tabs to localStorage with automatic recovery on restart

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- Navigate to TopicItem/PageItem and double-click or use context menu "Open in New Tab"
- Verify tab is created and visible in titlebar
- Switch tabs and verify navigation
- Navigate within active tab and verify tab reference is updated
- Close tab and verify proper activation of adjacent tab
- Restart app and verify tabs are restored from localStorage

#### 📝 Additional Information

Shared `getCachedDataForReference` helper extracted from `useNavigationHistory` for reuse by `useTabNavigation`.
Updated agent TopicItem, group TopicItem, and PageItem with double-click and context menu support.

## Summary by Sourcery

Add browser-style tabbed navigation to the Electron titlebar and wire it into existing navigation and plugin systems.

New Features:
- Introduce a TabBar component in the Electron titlebar that displays and manages page tabs when multiple tabs are open.
- Enable opening agent topics, group topics, and pages in new Electron tabs via double-click or an "Open in New Tab" context menu action.
- Persist Electron tab state (open tabs and active tab) in localStorage and restore it on app start, keeping tabs in sync with route changes.

Enhancements:
- Extract shared cached page data resolution into a reusable helper for both navigation history and tab navigation.
- Extend the Electron store with a dedicated tabPages slice to manage tab lifecycle, ordering, and cached data.
- Add localization strings for the new "Open in New Tab" actions in topic and page menus.

title: ✨ feat(desktop): unified update channel switching with S3 distribution
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat
- [x] ♻️ refactor
- [x] 👷 build

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Implement runtime update channel switching for the desktop app, unify all channels (stable/nightly/canary) to use S3 distribution with explicit manifest naming.

**Core changes:**

- **Runtime channel switching**: Users can switch between stable/nightly/canary in Settings > Beta. Channel preference is persisted in electron store and survives restarts.
- **Unified S3 distribution**: All three channels now use the generic HTTP provider with URL format `{UPDATE_SERVER_URL}/{channel}/`, looking for `{channel}-mac.yml` manifests. Removes GitHub Releases as update source (kept as fallback for local dev without S3).
- **Unified appId**: All channels share `com.lobehub.lobehub-desktop` to enable seamless cross-channel updates.
- **CI workflows**: Added `publish-s3` and `cleanup-s3` jobs to nightly and canary workflows. S3 cleanup retains the 15 most recent versions.
- **Merge script**: Updated `mergeMacReleaseFiles.js` to handle `stable-mac.yml`, `nightly-mac.yml`, `canary-mac.yml` prefixes.
- **Settings UI**: New desktop-only "Beta" tab with Update Channel selector (stable/nightly/canary).
- **IPC**: New `getUpdateChannel` / `setUpdateChannel` methods with input validation.
- **Channel downgrade**: `allowDowngrade` automatically set to `true` when switching to a less-frequent channel (e.g., canary → stable).

**Key files:**

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| Updater core | `UpdaterManager.ts`, `configs.ts`, `UpdaterCtr.ts` |
| IPC types | `electron-client-ipc/types/update.ts`, `events/update.ts` |
| Store | `types/store.ts`, `const/store.ts` |
| Build | `electron-builder.mjs`, `mergeMacReleaseFiles.js` |
| CI | `release-desktop-{nightly,canary,stable}.yml` |
| Settings UI | `settings/beta/`, `componentMap.ts`, `useCategory.tsx` |
| Test scripts | `scripts/update-test/*` |

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

**Local testing:**

```bash
cd apps/desktop/scripts/update-test
./run-test.sh          # Full flow (requires packaged app)
./run-test.sh --dev    # UI-only (dev mode)
```

**Verify channel switching via logs:**

```bash
tail -f ~/Library/Logs/lobehub-desktop-dev/main.log | grep -E 'Switching|Configuring|channel'
```

Expected log output when switching to canary:
- `Switching update channel: stable -> canary`
- `Configuring generic provider for canary channel`
- `Update server URL: http://localhost:8787/canary`
- `Channel set to: canary (will look for canary-mac.yml)`

**Unit tests:** 29/29 UpdaterManager tests passing.

#### 📝 Additional Information

- Squirrel.Mac requires matching code signatures for update installation — local unsigned builds can verify channel switching logic but cannot complete the install step. CI builds with real Apple Developer certificates will work end-to-end.
- The `beta` build channel value is mapped to `nightly` for backward compatibility.
- S3 secrets (`UPDATE_S3_BUCKET`, `UPDATE_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, etc.) need to be configured in GitHub repository settings for the nightly/canary workflows.

🔧 chore(desktop): update local app update configuration

- Change provider from GitHub to Generic for local testing.
- Update local server URL and cache directory settings.
- Revise comments for clarity on usage and configuration.

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>

🐛 fix(desktop): fix update channel switch race condition and downgrade flag

- P1: Use generation counter to discard stale check results when channel
  is switched mid-flight. Pending recheck is scheduled after current check
  completes instead of forcing concurrent checks.
- P2: Explicitly reset allowDowngrade=false on non-downgrade transitions
  to prevent stale downgrade permission from persisting.
- Fix GitHub fallback repo name (lobe-chat -> lobehub).

🔧 chore(settings): remove dynamic import for Beta component from componentMap

- Eliminated the dynamic import for the Beta settings tab, streamlining the component map.

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>

🔧 chore(settings): simplify UpdateChannel component structure

- Refactored the UpdateChannel component to streamline the Select component usage by removing unnecessary nested children.

Signed-off-by: Innei <tukon479@gmail.com>
update

🐛 fix(desktop): strip channel suffix from UPDATE_SERVER_URL before appending channel

The UPDATE_SERVER_URL secret may already contain a channel path (e.g., /stable).
Previously, the code unconditionally appended /{channel}, resulting in double
paths like /stable/stable/stable-mac.yml.

Now both electron-builder.mjs and UpdaterManager strip any trailing channel
suffix before re-appending the correct channel, supporting both legacy URLs
(with channel) and clean base URLs.

redesign ui

- Enhanced menu items for macOS and Windows to display appropriate update statuses.
- Localized new update messages in English and Chinese.

This improves user experience by providing real-time feedback during the update process.

Enhance UpdaterManager tests and mock implementations

- Modified mock implementations in macOS and Windows test files to include `getUpdaterState` and `installNow` methods for better state management.
- Improved test coverage for update availability and download processes.

These changes ensure more accurate testing of the update flow and enhance the overall reliability of the UpdaterManager functionality.

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `MarketApiService.searchSkill`, calling `discoverService.injectMPToken()` introduces a cross-service dependency that can easily lead to circular imports; consider moving token injection into `MarketApiService` itself or into a shared utility to keep responsibilities and dependencies clearer.
- Since `injectMPToken` is now awaited in multiple public methods, it may be worth ensuring it is idempotent and/or cached per session/request to avoid redundant token fetches and unnecessary latency on repeated marketplace calls.

title: 🐛 fix(conversation): disable animation for single-line messages between reasoning and tool calls
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

When a short (single-line) assistant message is sandwiched between reasoning and tool call blocks during streaming, the `fadeIn` animation on `MarkdownMessage` replays due to component remount caused by the stateless `parse()` rebuilding the entire message tree on every streaming update.

This PR disables the `animated` prop for these tool-adjacent single-line messages (`hasTools && isSingleLine`), preventing the visual flicker while preserving animation for all other message types.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] No tests needed

1. Start a conversation with an agent that uses tools
2. Send a prompt that triggers: reasoning → short text → tool call → more text
3. Observe that the short text message no longer replays its fadeIn animation when subsequent tool calls arrive

#### 📝 Additional Information

Root cause is architectural: `parse()` from `@lobechat/conversation-flow` is stateless and rebuilds the entire `assistantGroup` children structure on every streaming update. Combined with `refreshMessages()` refetching from DB after tool execution, this creates race conditions that cause component remount. A full fix would require structural caching in the parse layer — this PR applies a targeted visual fix instead.

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Disable fade-in animation on single-line assistant messages adjacent to tool calls to eliminate visual flicker caused by component remounts.
author: Innei

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider pulling the `hasTools && isSingleLine` heuristic into a small helper or adding an inline comment so the specific conditions under which animations are disabled are easier to understand and maintain.
- You could simplify `animated={isToolSingleLine ? false : markdownProps.animated}` to something like `animated={!isToolSingleLine && markdownProps.animated}` (or `animated={isToolSingleLine ? false : markdownProps.animated ?? false}` if `animated` is optional) to make the override behavior slightly clearer.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- Consider pulling the `hasTools && isSingleLine` heuristic into a small helper or adding an inline comment so the specific conditions under which animations are disabled are easier to understand and maintain.
- You could simplify `animated={isToolSingleLine ? false : markdownProps.animated}` to something like `animated={!isToolSingleLine && markdownProps.animated}` (or `animated={isToolSingleLine ? false : markdownProps.animated ?? false}` if `animated` is optional) to make the override behavior slightly clearer.
~~~

</details>

Add support for OIDC device code authorization flow with corresponding UI, configuration, and safeguards around device code persistence.

New Features:
- Enable OIDC device flow including device and verification routes and native client configuration for the CLI.
- Introduce device authorization UI pages for code entry, confirmation, and success states, wired into the OIDC device flow.

Enhancements:

author: arvinxx

reviews.body:
Hey - I've found 2 issues, and left some high level feedback:

- In the OIDC adapter, the `this.name !== 'DeviceCode'` check relies on a magic string; consider centralizing model names in a shared enum/constant to avoid typos and make future refactors safer.
- In the Next.js device flow pages, `searchParams` is typed as a `Promise` and then awaited, but in the app router it is passed as a plain object; simplifying the type to a plain object and removing the `await` will better align with the Next.js API and reduce confusion.

- Display an inline email contact hint within the feedback modal instead of exposing email support in the main footer.

Enhancements:
- Adjust command menu empty-state handling to account for force-mounted result groups and avoid incorrect no-results messaging.
- Always mount command groups for all result types to ensure consistent rendering and loading skeleton behavior.
- Update localization strings for knowledge base search labels and refine Discord label text.

author: nekomeowww

reviews.body:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider tightening the typing around `parseAssociationExtra` and the `extra` field so you can avoid the repeated `as any` casts and mutation via `delete`, e.g., by having the schema/model represent `extra` as `Record<string, unknown> | undefined` instead of a string.
- The new behavior drops the `extra` field entirely when the value is falsy (e.g., empty string), whereas previously `JSON.parse(parsed.data.extra || '{}')` would yield an empty object; if this change is intentional, it might be clearer to treat empty string consistently (e.g., as `{}`) rather than silently omitting the field.

title: 💄 style: Add capability inference for web search, image output and video recognition in model parsing and update UI form items to support search, imageOutput and video abilities
body: #### 💻 变更类型 | Change Type - [ ] ✨ feat - [ ] 🐛 fix - [ ] ♻️ refactor - [x] 💄 style - [ ] 👷 build - [ ] ⚡️ perf - [ ] ✅ test - [ ] 📝 docs - [ ] 🔨 chore #### 🔀 变更说明 | Description of Change 1) 拉取模型服务商的模型列表时，补齐模型能力标识 - 新增能力位：search（联网搜索）、imageOutput（图像输出）、video（视频识别）。 - 能力判定优先级：model. > knownModel.abilities. > 关键词匹配 > 默认 false。 - 新增 DEFAULT_SEARCH_KEYWORDS（默认包含 -search），以及 google 的 imageOutputKeywords（-image-） 和 videoKeywords（-2.5-）。 2) UI 支持手动勾选“联网搜索 / 图片生成 / 视频识别” - 勾选“联网搜索”时自动补齐 searchImpl： - 优先使用本地的模型设置； - 若服务商在预设名单中，则采用对应预设； - 若无预设或为自定义服务商，统一设置为 params。 - 勾选“图片生成”或“视频识别”时保存能力，不再在后续更新时被覆盖。 3) 修复对话页“联网搜索”UI 交互 - 当 searchImpl=internal 时，不再显示“选择辅助搜索模型”，并且只能选择“智能联网”（Perplexity、Jina 等及自定义模型）。 - 当 searchImpl=params 或 tool 时，需要满足不支持函数调用且未使用模型内置搜索，才会显示“选择辅助搜索模型 - 不支持模型内置搜索的逻辑未修改 4) 修复通过 UI 更新模型配置后，原有“联网搜索 / 图片生成 / 视频识别”能力丢失的问题 - 合并逻辑改为“保留用户能力优先”，不会被默认模型覆盖或清空。 5) 生成白名单图像模型时，去掉冗余 abilities 字段 - 仅保留实际使用的能力位，避免误导或侧写。 6) 修复拉取模型时，错误地把reasoning、functionCall等能力加到图片生成模型上（如gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview） 7) 修复调用 searchImpl=internal 的模型可能会触发函数调用搜索工具 #### 📝 补充信息 | Additional Information - fix #8760 #### Search 参数预设映射 | 服务商 | searchImpl | searchProvider | 备注 | |---|---|---|---| | ai360 | params | | | | aihubmix | params | | | | anthropic | params | | | | baichuan | params | | | | google | params | google | | | hunyuan | params | | | | jina | internal | | internal 不显示辅助搜索模型选择 | | minimax | params | | | | openai | params | | 默认 params；4o系列的“-search-”模型特判为 internal | | perplexity | internal | | internal 不显示辅助搜索模型选择 | | qwen | params | | | | spark | params | | 默认 params；max-32k 特判为 internal | | stepfun | params | | | | vertexai | params | google | | | wenxin | params | | | | xai | params | | | | zhipu | params | | | | default（未列出/自定义） | params | | 无预设或自定义服务商统一使用 params | 说明： - 用户模型不会保存 settings；searchImpl 和 searchProvider 由后端在合并时补齐。 - 当用户关闭 search 能力时，会移除已有的 searchImpl 和 searchProvider （即使内置模型存在该设置）。 ## Summary by Sourcery Infer and expose network search and image output capabilities for models in the parsing and storage layers, inject default search settings per provider, update UI to allow toggling these abilities, and extend tests and state to cover the new logic. New Features: - Expose `search` and `imageOutput` flags in ChatModelCard and infer them via explicit properties, known-model registry, and keyword matching - Inject default `searchImpl` and `searchProvider` settings for models with `search` ability based on provider defaults, preserving user overrides and removing settings when disabled - Add UI form fields in the custom model modal for enabling `search` and `imageOutput` capabilities - Update chat input search controls to conditionally show built-in and auxiliary search options based on `searchImpl` and `useModelBuiltinSearch` Enhancements: - Refactor model parsing to load provider local configurations and merge enabled overrides - Clean up redundant ability fields when generating whitelist image variants in post-processing - Pass provider identifier to processModelList in Google runtime to apply local configs - Extend Redux AI model slice to include `search` and `imageOutput` abilities Documentation: - Add localization entries for search and imageOutput in model provider default locales Tests: - Add unit tests for search and imageOutput inference in processModelList and processMultiProviderModelList - Add tests for injecting and removing search settings in getEnabledModels and getAiProviderModelList - Add sanity check for `MODEL_LIST_CONFIGS.google.imageOutputKeywords`

body:

title: 🐛 fix(context-engine): allow tool type recovery from manifest when models strip suffixes
body:
### 🐛 Bug Description

Models from Zhipu AI (like GLM-4) incorrectly strip the tool type suffix (e.g., `____builtin` or `____standalone`) from the tool name when returning function calls. This violates the OpenAI specification and causes `ToolNameResolver` in `@lobechat/context-engine` to misidentify the tool type as `default`, breaking all builtin tools (Notebook, GTD, Web Browsing) for GLM users.

This PR adds a robust recovery mechanism to `ToolNameResolver.resolve()`. If the LLM strips the suffix and the parsed `type` is missing, it will now fallback to looking up the exact plugin's type from the injected `manifests` map (`manifests[identifier]?.type`). A regression test has been added to ensure models that strip suffixes are handled correctly.

### 📝 Additional Information

Fixes #12557

### ✅ Validations

- [x] Read the [docs](https://lobehub.com/zh/docs).
- [x] Check that there isn't [already an issue](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/issues) that reports the same bug to avoid creating a duplicate.
- [x] Make sure this is a LobeChat issue and not a third-party library or provider issue.
- [x] Check that this is a concrete bug. For Q&A, please use [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat/discussions) or join our [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/rGHwKq4R).

title: ✨ feat(editor): implement AI input auto-completion
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ✨ feat

#### 🔗 Related Issue

Fixes LOBE-6586
close https://github.com/lobehub/lobehub/issues/10486

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Implements GitHub Copilot-style ghost text auto-completion in the chat input, powered by `@lobehub/editor`'s `ReactAutoCompletePlugin`.

**System Agent Config:**
- New `inputCompletion: SystemAgentItem` in `UserSystemAgentConfig`
- Default: `enabled: false`, mini model
- Settings UI in Service Model page with enable toggle

**Prompt Chain (V2 — benchmarked optimal):**
- Concise system prompt with few-shot examples
- Supports conversation context for contextually relevant completions
- `max_tokens: 100` to keep completions short

**Editor Integration:**
- Conditionally mounts `ReactAutoCompletePlugin` when enabled
- 600ms delay before triggering completion
- Uses plugin's built-in `abortSignal` for cancellation
- Calls `chatService.fetchPresetTaskResult` to get completions

**Data Flow:**
- `getMessages` callback wired through ChatInput store → StoreUpdater → Provider
- Conversation page provides `dbMessages` as context; Home page has no context

**Benchmark Results (84 runs, 21 scenarios × 4 models):**
- Default model: `gpt-5.4-mini` — 21/21 PASS, ~3.9s latency, best quality stability
- Speed option: `groq/llama-4-scout` — ~3.2s latency, $0.00004/call
- Full results: LOBE-6596

#### 🧪 How to Test

1. Go to Settings → Service Model → Enable "Input Auto-Completion Agent"
2. Navigate to any chat conversation
3. Type a partial message (e.g., "How do I implement ") and wait ~1 second
4. Ghost text should appear in gray after the cursor
5. Press `Tab` to accept, or continue typing to dismiss

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

#### 📝 Additional Information

- Feature is disabled by default — users must opt-in via settings
- Prompt was selected through systematic benchmarking (V2 few-shot beat V1 XML-tags and V3 structured across all models)
- 10+ models tested; rejected models documented with reasons in benchmark sub-issue LOBE-6596

Autocomplete needs the full result before displaying ghost text,
so streaming adds unnecessary overhead. Setting stream: false

receiving the completion result.

Autocomplete waits for the full result before displaying ghost text.
fetchSSE handles non-streaming responses via its fallback path
(response.clone().text()), avoiding SSE chunking overhead.

Call getChatCompletion directly instead of fetchPresetTaskResult

title: 🐛 fix: Expose tool discovery config to context engine and inject available tools
body:
### Motivation

- Enable injection of an `<available_tools>` variable into server-side context when the activator is enabled so the agent can surface discoverable tools. 
- Compute a lightweight list of available tools from the operation tool set without exposing already-enabled tools.

### Description

- Add `buildToolDiscoveryConfig` to compute `availableTools` from `OperationToolSet` and `resolved.enabledToolIds`, gated by `LobeActivatorIdentifier` presence. 
- Import `LobeActivatorIdentifier` and wire `toolDiscoveryConfig` into the LLM call flow by passing it into `serverMessagesEngine` and into the `MessagesEngine` constructor. 
- Expose `toolDiscoveryConfig` in the `ServerMessagesEngineParams` types as `ToolDiscoveryConfig` for context engineering. 
- Keep existing tool/skill resolution behavior and only provide discovery data when there are additional tools available.

### Testing

- Ran TypeScript type-check and project build via `tsc`/build, which succeeded. 
- Ran unit test suite via `yarn test`, which completed successfully.

title: 🐛 fix: stream tool call arguments incrementally in Response API
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Response API tool call streaming broken -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Fix Response API streaming for tool calls. The internal `tools_calling` stream chunks contain **accumulated** arguments (the full string up to that point), but the Response API was treating each chunk as a complete independent `output_item` — emitting a full lifecycle (`added → delta → done → item.done`) per token delta and incrementing `output_index` from 0 to 90+.

**Before:** Each token delta creates a new output_item:
```
output_index=0: added → delta("") → done → item.done
output_index=1: added → delta('{"des') → done → item.done
output_index=2: added → delta('{"description"') → done → item.done
... up to output_index=93
```

**After:** Single stable output_item with true incremental deltas:
```
output_index=1: added (in_progress)
output_index=1: delta('{"des')
output_index=1: delta('cription"')
output_index=1: delta(': "生成')
... 
output_index=1: arguments.done → item.done (completed)
```

**Implementation:**
- Track active tool calls in a `Map<callId, {fcItemId, outputIndex, prevArguments}>`
- First chunk: emit `output_item.added` + initial delta
- Subsequent chunks: compute incremental delta via `args.slice(prevArgs.length)`
- Finalize with `arguments.done` + `output_item.done` when stream ends or `tool_end` arrives

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] No tests needed (no existing test infra for openapi package)

Test with: `POST /api/v1/responses` with a model that returns tool calls (e.g., code sandbox). Verify:
1. `output_index` stays stable per tool call
2. `delta` fields contain only incremental content
3. Only one `output_item.added` / `output_item.done` pair per tool call
4. `response.completed` output matches the streamed tool call

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.message:
🐛 fix: stream tool call arguments incrementally in Response API

The tool_calling stream chunks contain accumulated arguments (not
deltas), but the Response API was treating each chunk as a complete
independent output_item — creating a new lifecycle (added → delta →
done) per token and incrementing output_index to 90+.

Fix: track active tool calls by call_id and compute true incremental
deltas by slicing off previously-seen content. Each tool call now
gets a single stable output_item with proper streaming deltas,
finalized only when the stream ends or tool execution begins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

🐛 fix: clear stale tool-call state on LLM stream retry

When call_llm retries after a failed attempt, activeToolCalls may
contain entries from the failed stream that never received a
tool_end. Without clearing, finishActiveToolCalls would emit
phantom function_call done events and misalign output_index for
the successful attempt. Reset the map on stream_retry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: 🐛 fix(database): apply injectSearchSettings consistently for unmodified builtin models
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix
- [x] ✅ test

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Fix inconsistency in `injectSearchSettings` application during model list construction:

- **Bug**: In `getEnabledModels`, unmodified builtin models skipped `injectSearchSettings` (line 210 fast path), while user-modified models went through it (line 234). This caused identical models to have different `searchImpl` values depending on whether the user had ever touched them in DB.
- **Fix**: Wrap the unmodified model return path with `injectSearchSettings()` to ensure consistent behavior.
- **Model bank**: Remove `search: true` from all Anthropic models in LobeHub provider config, since LobeHub routes Claude through Bedrock which doesn't support built-in search.
- **Test refactor**: Split the monolithic `index.test.ts` (2031 lines) into 7 focused test files under `__tests__/`.

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Added/updated tests

```bash
cd packages/database && bunx vitest run src/repositories/aiInfra/__tests__/
```

3 new test cases added for search settings injection consistency.

title: ♻️ refactor: unify tool content formatting with ComputerRuntime and shared UI
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

N/A

#### 🔀 Description of Change

**Problem:** Tool content formatting was inconsistent across `local-system`, `cloud-sandbox`, and `skills` packages:
- `local-system` used `formatCommandResult()` for `runCommand`
- `cloud-sandbox` used `JSON.stringify()` for `runCommand`, `grepContent`, `getCommandOutput`, `killCommand`
- `skills` used plain text concatenation for `runCommand`

Inspector and Render components were duplicated across all three packages with 90%+ identical code.

**Solution:**

1. **New `@lobechat/tool-runtime` package** — `ComputerRuntime` abstract class that unifies content formatting via `@lobechat/prompts` format functions. Subclasses only implement `callService()`:
   - `CloudSandboxExecutionRuntime extends ComputerRuntime`
   - `LocalSystemExecutionRuntime extends ComputerRuntime` (new)
   - `SkillsExecutionRuntime` uses `formatCommandResult()` directly

2. **New `@lobechat/shared-tool-ui` package** — Shared Render and Inspector components:
   - `RunCommandRender` — unified render used by all 3 packages
   - `createXxxInspector(translationKey)` factories for RunCommand, EditLocalFile, ReadLocalFile, WriteLocalFile, ListLocalFiles, GlobLocalFiles, GrepContent
   - Shared `FilePathDisplay` component and inspector styles
   - Cloud-sandbox renders now reuse local-system renders for all overlapping tools

3. **State types unified** — Flat state types (`RunCommandState`, `GlobFilesState`, etc.) defined once in `@lobechat/tool-runtime`, re-exported by each package

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] No tests needed

Verified via:
- Type-check passes (`bun run type-check`)
- Unit tests pass (local-system: 55 tests, prompts: 71 tests)
- Electron desktop app tested with both **Local** and **Cloud Sandbox** modes — `runCommand` tool renders correctly in both environments with consistent formatting

#### 📝 Additional Information

**Breaking changes:** State shape for local-system inspector components changed from nested (`pluginState.result.output`) to flat (`pluginState.output`). This only affects the internal tool rendering — no public API changes.

title: 🐛 fix(model-runtime): ensure before* hook errors trigger on*Error handlers
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] 🐛 fix

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- N/A -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

Move `applyHooks`/`before*` calls inside try-catch for `chat()`, `generateObject()`, and `embeddings()` in `ModelRuntime` so that errors thrown by lifecycle hooks (e.g. budget checks in `beforeChat`) are processed by `onChatError`/`onGenerateObjectError`/`onEmbeddingsError` handlers.

Previously, `beforeChat` errors were thrown before the try-catch block, so `onChatError` (which sets `_responseBody` for error formatting) was never called. This caused the error response body to be `undefined`, breaking client-side error UI rendering (showing `{}` JSON instead of the proper error component).

Also adds a diagnostic `console.info` log in `ErrorMessageExtra` to help investigate a potential client-side rendering fallback issue in production.

#### 🧪 How to Test

```bash
cd packages/model-runtime && bunx vitest run --silent='passed-only' src/core/ModelRuntime.test.ts
```

All 110 tests pass, including 3 new tests:
- `beforeChat throwing triggers onChatError before re-throwing`
- `beforeGenerateObject throwing triggers onGenerateObjectError before re-throwing`
- `beforeEmbeddings throwing triggers onEmbeddingsError before re-throwing`

commits.message:
🐛 fix(model-runtime): ensure before* hook errors trigger on*Error handlers

Move applyHooks/before* calls inside try-catch for chat(), generateObject(),
and embeddings() so that errors thrown by lifecycle hooks (e.g. budget checks)
are processed by onChatError/onGenerateObjectError/onEmbeddingsError handlers.

Previously, beforeChat errors bypassed onChatError, causing the error response
body to be undefined and breaking client-side error UI rendering.

Also add diagnostic log in ErrorMessageExtra for production debugging.

```

title: refactor: Extract web onboarding runtime to builtin package
body:
#### 💻 Change Type

- [x] ♻️ refactor

#### 🔗 Related Issue

<!-- Link to the issue that is fixed by this PR -->

#### 🔀 Description of Change

This PR refactors the web onboarding tool execution runtime by extracting it into the `@lobechat/builtin-tool-web-onboarding` package, improving code organization and reusability.

**Key Changes:**

1. **New ExecutionRuntime module** (`packages/builtin-tool-web-onboarding/src/ExecutionRuntime/`):
   - Created `WebOnboardingExecutionRuntime` class that implements the core runtime logic for web onboarding operations
   - Defines `WebOnboardingRuntimeService` interface for dependency injection
   - Implements methods: `getOnboardingState()`, `saveUserQuestion()`, `finishOnboarding()`, `readDocument()`, `updateDocument()`

2. **Server-side runtime factory** (`src/server/services/toolExecution/serverRuntimes/webOnboarding.ts`):
   - New factory implementation that instantiates `WebOnboardingExecutionRuntime` with actual service implementations
   - Integrates with `OnboardingService` and `AgentDocumentsService`
   - Handles both 'soul' and 'persona' document types with appropriate persistence logic

3. **Utility consolidation**:
   - Moved `webOnboardingToolResult` utilities to the builtin package
   - Updated type definitions to be more generic and decoupled from internal types
   - Updated all imports across the codebase to reference the new location

4. **Package exports**:
   - Added new export paths in `package.json` for `executionRuntime` and `utils`
   - Enables external consumption of the runtime and utilities

5. **Import updates**:
   - Updated imports in `src/server/routers/lambda/user.ts`
   - Updated imports in `src/store/tool/slices/builtin/executors/lobe-web-onboarding.ts`
   - Updated test imports in `src/utils/webOnboardingToolResult.test.ts`
   - Registered new runtime in the server runtime registry

#### 🧪 How to Test

- [x] Existing tests pass (test imports updated to reference new package location)
- [x] No new tests needed (refactoring maintains existing functionality)

The refactoring maintains backward compatibility while improving code organization. Existing unit tests for the utility functions continue to pass with updated import paths.

#### 📝 Additional Information

This refactoring improves separation of concerns by:
- Moving runtime logic into the builtin package where it logically belongs
- Decoupling the runtime from server-specific implementations through dependency injection
- Making the runtime reusable across different contexts
- Centralizing web onboarding utilities in a single package location
